title: Unify UserHandler dual-router pattern with single Routes method
VPC & Networking Foundation
description:
## Summary
Provision the foundational AWS VPC and networking infrastructure that all other AWS resources depend on.

## Region & CIDR Blocks
- **Region**: us-east-2
- **Staging VPC**: 10.0.0.0/16
- **Prod VPC**: 10.1.0.0/16

Non-overlapping CIDRs allow future VPC peering if needed.

## Scope
Terraform modules for:
- VPC with public/private/database subnets across 2-3 AZs
- fck-nat instance for private subnet egress (cost-optimized)
- Internet gateway for public subnets
- Route tables (public, private, database)
- Security groups (baseline, can be extended by dependent epics)
- S3 Gateway Endpoint (free) for ECR layers and logs

## Environment Strategy
Two AWS environments, both in the same account (can split later):

| Environment | Purpose | VPC CIDR |
|-------------|---------|----------|
| Staging | CI/CD, E2E tests, integration tests | 10.0.0.0/16 |
| Prod | Real users, isolated | 10.1.0.0/16 |

Local development uses Docker Compose (Postgres, Redis, mock auth) - no AWS resources.

The VPC module is instantiated twice with different tfvars (staging.tfvars, prod.tfvars).

## Subnet Tiers
| Tier | Purpose | Internet Access |
|------|---------|-----------------|
| Public | ALB, NAT | Direct (IGW) |
| Private | EKS pods, apps | Outbound only (NAT) |
| Database | RDS, Redis | None (isolated) |

## Key Decisions
- CIDR block sizing: /16 VPC with /20 subnets (4k IPs per subnet)
- AZ count: 2 for cost efficiency (can expand to 3 later)
- NAT: fck-nat on t4g.nano (~$3/mo) instead of NAT Gateway (~$32/mo)
- VPC Endpoints: S3 gateway only (free). Skip interface endpoints (~$7/mo each) - use fck-nat instead.
- DB access: Use kubectl exec into pods, no bastion needed
- Environments: Staging + Prod only. Local dev via Docker Compose.

## Deferred (add later if needed)
- **CloudFront**: Not needed initially. Easy to add in front of ALB later.
- **VPC Flow Logs**: Enable when debugging network issues. ~$0.50/GB to CloudWatch.
- **Route53**: DNS managed externally. ALB DNS name output is sufficient for CNAME setup.
- **Bastion/VPN**: Use kubectl exec or debug pods for DB access instead.
- **Tagging strategy**: Basic environment tags for now. Per-customer resource tagging will be important later for cost allocation.
- **Multi-account**: Prod can move to separate AWS account later if needed (~2 hours of work).

## Deliverables
- Terraform module: `infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/`
- Environment configs: staging.tfvars, prod.tfvars
- Outputs: VPC ID, subnet IDs (public, private, database), security group IDs

## References
- See `/docs/MIGRATION.md` Phase 1: Infrastructure Foundation
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for overall AWS topology and environment strategy
- fck-nat: https://fck-nat.dev/
close_reason: Obsolete: AWS VPC - project now uses GCP

title: Create VPC Terraform module structure
description:
Set up base Terraform module at infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/ with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf. Define input variables for environment, region, CIDR block, AZ count.

**Done when:**
- [ ] Directory structure exists: `infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/`
- [ ] Files created: main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf
- [ ] Variables defined: environment, region, vpc_cidr, az_count
- [ ] `terraform validate` passes
close_reason: Obsolete: AWS VPC tasks superseded by GCP migration (PLAT-vi8)

title: Implement VPC with public/private/database subnets
description:
Create VPC with /16 CIDR block. Implement three subnet tiers across 2 AZs (expandable to 3):

- **Public subnets** (/20, ~4k IPs): ALB, NAT instance
- **Private subnets** (/20, ~4k IPs): EKS Fargate pods, application workloads
- **Database subnets** (/20, ~4k IPs): RDS, ElastiCache - fully isolated, no NAT route

Database subnets have no route to internet (inbound or outbound). Only accessible from private subnets via security groups.

Use data source for available AZs in us-east-2.

**CIDR blocks** (from parent epic):
- Staging: 10.0.0.0/16
- Prod: 10.1.0.0/16

**Done when:**
- [ ] VPC created with /16 CIDR
- [ ] `enable_dns_hostnames = true` and `enable_dns_support = true` configured
- [ ] 2 public subnets (one per AZ)
- [ ] 2 private subnets (one per AZ)
- [ ] 2 database subnets (one per AZ)
- [ ] `terraform plan` shows expected resources
- [ ] Subnets tagged with tier (public/private/database)
close_reason: Obsolete: AWS VPC tasks superseded by GCP migration (PLAT-vi8)

title: Implement Internet Gateway and NAT Gateways
description:
Create Internet Gateway for public subnets. Deploy fck-nat instance (t4g.nano) for NAT instead of NAT Gateway to minimize costs (~$3/mo vs ~$32/mo). Single NAT instance initially - can upgrade to NAT Gateway later if needed. Use Elastic IP for the NAT instance. Reference: https://fck-nat.dev/

**fck-nat configuration:**
- Instance type: t4g.nano (ARM, ~$3/mo)
- AMI: Use fck-nat community AMI for us-east-2
- Placement: First public subnet (us-east-2a)
- Elastic IP: Associate with ENI

**Critical**: The ENI must have `source_dest_check = false` to allow traffic to flow through the instance (hairpinning). Without this, NAT will not work.

**Done when:**
- [ ] Internet Gateway created and attached to VPC
- [ ] Elastic IP allocated for NAT
- [ ] fck-nat instance launched in public subnet with EIP
- [ ] ENI has `source_dest_check = false`
- [ ] NAT instance can reach internet (test: curl from instance)
close_reason: Obsolete: AWS VPC tasks superseded by GCP migration (PLAT-vi8)

title: Implement route tables for public and private subnets
description:
Create route tables for each subnet tier:

- **Public route table**: Default route (0.0.0.0/0) to Internet Gateway. Associate with public subnets.
- **Private route table**: Default route to fck-nat instance. Associate with private subnets.
- **Database route table**: No default route (fully isolated). Associate with database subnets.

Database tier has no outbound internet path - RDS/Redis can only be reached from within the VPC.

**Done when:**
- [ ] Public route table with 0.0.0.0/0 → IGW
- [ ] Private route table with 0.0.0.0/0 → fck-nat ENI
- [ ] Database route table with local routes only
- [ ] Each subnet associated with correct route table
close_reason: Obsolete: AWS VPC tasks superseded by GCP migration (PLAT-vi8)

title: Implement baseline security groups (Fargate model)
description:
Create baseline security groups for Fargate-based EKS (no EC2 nodes):

- **ALB SG**: Inbound 80/443 from 0.0.0.0/0, outbound to EKS Pod SG
- **EKS Pod SG**: Inbound from ALB SG on app ports, outbound to RDS SG, Redis SG, and 0.0.0.0/0 (internet via NAT). Attached to Fargate pod ENIs via EKS SecurityGroupPolicy.
- **RDS SG**: Inbound 5432 from EKS Pod SG only
- **Redis SG**: Inbound 6379 from EKS Pod SG only
- **NAT SG**: Inbound all traffic from VPC CIDR, outbound all traffic to 0.0.0.0/0. Per fck-nat docs, traffic flows THROUGH the instance (hairpinning), not TO it.

Note: Fargate pods get individual ENIs with VPC IPs - no "node" security group needed. The EKS Pod SG is applied to pod ENIs, not EC2 instances.

**Important**: The fck-nat ENI must have `source_dest_check = false` to allow pass-through traffic. This is configured in PLAT-03e.3 (NAT instance task).

**Done when:**
- [ ] All 5 security groups created
- [ ] NAT SG allows all traffic from VPC CIDR inbound
- [ ] Rules reference other SGs by ID (not CIDR) where applicable
- [ ] Security group IDs exported as outputs
close_reason: Obsolete: AWS VPC tasks superseded by GCP migration (PLAT-vi8)

title: Implement S3 Gateway Endpoint
description:
Create S3 Gateway Endpoint (free) for ECR image layers, logs, and Terraform state. Skip interface endpoints (ECR API, CloudWatch, STS, Secrets Manager) - let this traffic go through fck-nat instead. Interface endpoints cost ~$7/mo/AZ each which defeats the cost savings from fck-nat.

Future consideration: Add interface endpoints if fck-nat becomes a bottleneck or if security requirements mandate traffic stay within VPC.

**Done when:**
- [ ] S3 Gateway Endpoint created
- [ ] Endpoint associated with private and database route tables
- [ ] S3 traffic from private subnets does not go through NAT (verify in route table)
close_reason: Obsolete: AWS VPC tasks superseded by GCP migration (PLAT-vi8)

title: Create environment configs (staging, prod)
description:
Create tfvars files for staging and prod environments. Both use single fck-nat instance initially.

**Environment configs:**
- staging.tfvars: us-east-2, 10.0.0.0/16, 2 AZs, single NAT
- prod.tfvars: us-east-2, 10.1.0.0/16, 2 AZs, single NAT

Local development does not use AWS - uses Docker Compose instead.

**Terraform organization:**
```
infrastructure/terraform/
├── modules/vpc/           # Reusable VPC module
├── environments/
│   ├── staging/
│   │   ├── main.tf        # Instantiates modules
│   │   ├── backend.tf     # S3 state (staging prefix)
│   │   └── terraform.tfvars
│   └── prod/
│       ├── main.tf
│       ├── backend.tf     # S3 state (prod prefix)
│       └── terraform.tfvars
```

**Done when:**
- [ ] tfvars files created for staging, prod with correct CIDR blocks
- [ ] `terraform plan` succeeds for both environments
- [ ] State stored in separate S3 prefixes (staging/, prod/)
- [ ] VPC module outputs exported: vpc_id, vpc_cidr, public_subnet_ids, private_subnet_ids, database_subnet_ids, alb_sg_id, eks_pod_sg_id, rds_sg_id, redis_sg_id, nat_sg_id
close_reason: Obsolete: AWS VPC tasks superseded by GCP migration (PLAT-vi8)

title: Bootstrap Terraform state backend
description:
Create S3 bucket and DynamoDB table for Terraform remote state before any other infrastructure.

**Resources:**
- S3 bucket: `eval-terraform-state` with versioning enabled
- DynamoDB table: `terraform-locks` with PAY_PER_REQUEST billing, LockID (string) as hash key

**Bootstrap process:**
1. Create `infrastructure/terraform/bootstrap/` with state backend resources
2. Apply locally (state stored in git initially, one-time only)
3. Configure backend in `infrastructure/terraform/environments/` to use remote state
4. All subsequent terraform runs use S3 + DynamoDB

**S3 bucket settings:**
- Versioning: enabled (recover from bad applies)
- Encryption: AES-256 (SSE-S3)
- Public access: blocked
- Lifecycle: keep all versions (state history)

**Done when:**
- [ ] S3 bucket `eval-terraform-state` exists with versioning enabled
- [ ] DynamoDB table `terraform-locks` exists with LockID key
- [ ] `terraform init` in environments/ configures remote backend successfully
- [ ] `terraform plan` acquires and releases lock
close_reason: Implemented and reviewed - Bootstrap module complete with S3 (AES256 encryption, versioning, public access blocked) and DynamoDB (terraform-locks, PAY_PER_REQUEST). terraform validate passes. Actual apply requires AWS credentials.

title: Extract shared E2E instructor setup helper to reduce duplication
description:
## Summary
The 'create class → create section → navigate to dashboard → start session' UI flow is copy-pasted ~70 lines across 3 locations:
- frontend/e2e/critical-paths.spec.ts (test 1, lines ~42-123)
- frontend/e2e/critical-paths.spec.ts (test 2, lines ~191-247)
- frontend/e2e/public-view.spec.ts (lines ~31-101)

## Implementation
Extract a shared helper in frontend/e2e/fixtures/ (e.g., setupClassSectionAndSession(instructorPage)) that returns { joinCode, sessionUrl }. This is consistent with how test-fixture.ts already abstracts namespace/user setup via API.

title: Add rate limiting to execute endpoint
close_reason: Implemented: per-instance rate limiting with golang.org/x/time/rate

title: Store integration tests hang when run with -race and concurrent Postgres usage
description: When running make test-integration-store (which uses -race flag) concurrently with E2E tests or other Postgres-intensive operations, the go test process hangs indefinitely with no output. Individual test subsets (e.g., -run TestIntegration_Migration014) complete fine. The full suite without -race also eventually hangs. Likely a connection pool exhaustion or deadlock issue under concurrent load. Investigate: (1) pool size settings in integration test setup, (2) whether test cleanup (defer db.close()) properly releases connections, (3) the warning failed to delete namespace closed pool messages that appear in normal runs.
close_reason: Fixed: removed redundant defer db.close() that blocked before t.Cleanup conn.Release. Added t.Cleanup(conn.Release) in storeWithRLS for safety.

title: Improve executor timeout tests to exercise real code paths
close_reason: Removed misleading tests instead of rewriting; integration test TestIntegration_Timeout covers real timeout behavior

title: Fix act() warnings in PublicViewContent tests
description:
The frontend tests for PublicViewContent emit noisy console.error warnings:

```
An update to PublicViewContent inside a test was not wrapped in act(...)
```

**Root cause:** `useRealtimePublicView` hook has async state updates (`setState`, `setLoading`, `setError`) that fire after an `await` inside a `useEffect`. React test utilities cannot automatically track state updates after async boundaries.

**Files to modify:**
- `frontend/src/app/(projector)/public-view/__tests__/page.test.tsx` - primary test file
- `frontend/src/hooks/__tests__/useRealtimePublicView.test.ts` - hook test file

**Fix options (pick one):**
1. Ensure all async renders settle with `waitFor()` before asserting — some tests already do this, but inconsistently
2. Suppress the specific console.error in test setup via `jest.spyOn(console, "error")` filter

**Impact:** Cosmetic only — tests pass, component works correctly in production. This just reduces test output noise.

title: Replace Resend API with Firebase email link sign-in for invitations
description: Eliminate Resend API dependency by using Firebase email link sign-in (passwordless) for instructor invitations. Identity Platform sends all emails via custom SMTP (Resend relay) from custom domain. Backend becomes pure CRUD for invitations; frontend handles email sending via Firebase SDK and reworks acceptance page for email link flow.
close_reason: Closed — went in a different direction. Invitation emails remain sent via Resend API.

title: Mobile: Instructor section students table needs horizontal scroll
description: InstructorSectionView.tsx:357-396 renders a students table with whitespace-nowrap cells and px-6 py-4 padding. On mobile the table overflows its container without horizontal scrolling. The table's parent div has overflow-hidden which clips content. Fix: wrap the table in a div with overflow-x-auto so it scrolls horizontally on small screens.

title: Fix N+1 query in ClassHandler.Get — add store-level join for class detail with sections and instructors
close_reason: Replaced N+1 loop with single ListClassInstructorNames join query

title: CI failures on PR #31: contract tests, frontend tests, and store integration tests
description:
## Summary
PR #31 (feat: frontend-to-API contract test harness) had 3 CI failures. All fixed.

## Status

### 1. Contract Tests - FIXED ✓
- **Root cause**: System-admin users have no namespace, so couldn't create classes (RLS policy violation).
- **Fix**: Split auth middleware (PLAT-loq), contract tests now create namespace + instructor user via invitation flow before creating classes.

### 2. Frontend Tests - FIXED ✓
- Frontend unit tests all pass (128 suites, 1820 tests)
- Contract tests separated into their own npm script `test:contract`

### 3. Store Integration Tests - FIXED ✓
- **Root cause**: Test isolation issues - leftover data from other test runs caused:
  - Join code unique constraint violations
  - Count assertions failing due to extra records
- **Fix**: 
  - createSection helper now appends unique suffix to join codes
  - ListProblemsFiltered/ListSessions filter by namespace
  - Count assertions changed to verify presence of created records

## PR Reference
- PR #31: https://github.com/jdelfino/eval/pull/31
- Branch: `feature/contract-test-harness`
close_reason: All CI failures fixed: contract tests via auth middleware split, store integration tests via proper test isolation

title: E2E session-lifecycle test silently accepts executor 500 as passing
description:
The session-lifecycle E2E test ("Full session lifecycle: create, join, replace, end, practice") silently swallows executor 500 errors in its practice mode assertion, counting them as a pass.

At frontend/e2e/session-lifecycle.spec.ts:133-138, the practice mode assertion accepts either a success result OR an error alert:

    const resultOrError = page.locator(".bg-gray-900, [data-testid=error-alert]").first();
    await expect(resultOrError).toBeVisible();

The comment says "executor may not be running", but the executor IS running in CI — it returns 500 "internal execution error" on the practice request. The frontend shows an error alert, the test sees it, and passes. This masks a real failure (PLAT-982u).

Evidence from CI logs:

    level=ERROR error="executor: unexpected status 500" path=/api/v1/sessions/.../practice
    session-lifecycle.spec.ts ... (13.3s) PASSED

Fix: assert on successful execution output, not accept errors as valid. Related: PLAT-982u (underlying sandbox failure this test is masking).

title: Port: allow students to join completed sessions (coding-tool aeef49c + a6f1210)
close_reason: Combined into PLAT-8ss2 — the join bug was an artifact of coding-tool's initial practice mode implementation, not an independent issue

title: Extract scanSession/scanClass helpers to reduce column-list duplication in store
Port practice mode and session-lifecycle E2E test from coding-tool
description:
## Summary

Port the practice mode feature (allowing students to run code after sessions end) and the new session-lifecycle E2E test from coding-tool. Port up to: 78067cb.

## Origin

coding-tool commits: dbaf12a (#59), 78067cb. These are the most recent functional commits since the last sync.

## Part 1: Practice mode for completed sessions

In coding-tool, practice mode has 3 parts:
1. **Backend: practice execution endpoint** - `POST /api/sessions/[id]/practice` with rate limiting
2. **Backend: allow code saving in completed sessions** - removes the completed-session block from code save
3. **Frontend: student page UI** - enables run button + editing in ended sessions, calls practice endpoint

### Eval implementation approach

The backend is different (Go, not Node API routes), so this needs:

**Go backend:**
- Add `POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/practice` handler that runs code via the executor service
- Rate limit: 15 req/min per user (use existing rate limiter or add one)
- Auth: verify user is a participant in the session
- Remove or relax the 'session must be active' check in the code save handler (if it exists)

**Frontend (`frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/page.tsx`):**
- Add `handlePracticeRun` that POSTs to the practice endpoint directly (bypasses realtime)
- In `handleRunCode`, delegate to `handlePracticeRun` when `sessionEnded` is true
- Remove `sessionEnded` from debounced code save dependency (allow saving in practice mode)
- Change CodeEditor props: `readOnly={false}`, `showRunButton={true}`, `onRun={handleRunCode}`, `onLoadStarterCode={handleLoadStarterCode}` (always enabled, not gated on sessionEnded)

## Part 2: Session-lifecycle E2E test

Port `e2e/session-lifecycle.spec.ts` from coding-tool. The test covers:
1. Instructor starts session from problem page (InstructorActions)
2. Student joins and types code
3. Instructor starts new session from different problem (session replacement)
4. Student sees replacement notification and joins new session
5. Student types code in new session, instructor verifies
6. Instructor ends session
7. Student runs code in practice mode

### Eval adaptation needed

- Replace Supabase Admin setup (`getSupabaseAdmin`, `setupTestNamespaceWithSection`, `createInstructorForSection`) with eval's API helpers (`testNamespace` fixture, `setupInstructor`, `apiFetch`)
- Add `createProblem` helper to `e2e/fixtures/api-setup.ts`: POST to `/api/v1/problems`
- Add `registerStudent` flow using eval's existing helper
- Replace `loginAsInstructor`/`loginAsStudent` with eval's `signInAs`
- Replace `cleanupNamespace` with eval's fixture-based cleanup
- Use eval's `logCollector` fixture for debug output
- Adapt URL patterns: `?sessionId=` vs `?session_id=` (check eval's conventions)

## Testing

The E2E test itself IS the test. Run with `make e2e` or equivalent.

title: Fix /admin page 403 for namespace-admin: admin.ts API client calls system-admin-only endpoints (/system/users, /admin/stats) instead of namespace-scoped endpoints (/admin/users, /admin/users/{id}/role, /admin/users/{id})
close_reason: Fixed: admin.ts now uses namespace-scoped endpoints for namespace-admin users, system-scoped for system-admin

title: Bootstrap page calls non-existent POST /auth/register endpoint
description: The frontend bootstrap page at frontend/src/app/(public)/auth/bootstrap/page.tsx calls POST /auth/register which does not exist in the Go backend. The backend has POST /auth/bootstrap instead (handler: PostBootstrap in go-backend/internal/handler/auth.go). The page is currently dead code - it shows a registration form but submits to the wrong endpoint. Either wire the page to POST /auth/bootstrap with proper Firebase custom claims, or remove the page entirely if bootstrap is CLI-only.
close_reason: Removed dead bootstrap page — never worked post-migration. Real bootstrap flow is CLI + AuthContext auto-detection.

title: Replace stubRepos + 17 wrapper types with code-generated mocks
description:
stubRepos is ~190 lines of hand-written panic stubs in testutil_test.go. There are 17 per-handler wrapper types each manually delegating methods (~400 lines of boilerplate). Adding a new method to any repository interface requires updating stubRepos and reviewing all 17 wrappers.

Use mockery or moq to generate mocks against store.Repos automatically.

Files: go-backend/internal/handler/testutil_test.go:214-397, plus 17 *_test.go files

title: E2E tests for core instructor + student workflows
description:
Add 4 high-value E2E tests covering major untested workflows. No infrastructure changes needed — uses existing test auth (localStorage injection).

## Motivation
E2E suite has 9 tests across 6 files. Good coverage of live session flows but zero coverage of:
- Problem CRUD (the entire problem library)
- Problem publishing + student practice
- Student progress review (grading workflow)
- Session start via UI (instructors use this every class)

## Subtasks
See child issues for individual test specs.

## Implementation Notes
- All tests follow existing patterns: namespace isolation, API setup, logCollector fixture
- Reuse fixtures from e2e/fixtures/api-setup.ts and e2e/fixtures/auth.ts
- Monaco interactions follow patterns from debugger.spec.ts
- Each test targets ~30s max

title: E2E: Problem CRUD (problem-management.spec.ts)
description:
## Summary
Add E2E test for the problem library CRUD workflow. Zero coverage today — a regression blocks all content creation.

## File to create
`frontend/e2e/problem-management.spec.ts`

## Test: "Instructor creates, edits, and deletes a problem"
1. Sign in as instructor, navigate to Problem Library (/instructor/problems)
2. Click "New Problem", fill title + description + starter code + tags
3. Save and verify it appears in the problem list
4. Reopen problem, edit title and add a test case
5. Save and verify changes persisted
6. Delete problem, verify removal from list

## Key files to reference
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/problems/` — Problem Library page and editor components
- `frontend/e2e/debugger.spec.ts` — Monaco editor interaction patterns
- `frontend/e2e/fixtures/api-setup.ts` — API helpers for test setup
- `frontend/e2e/fixtures/auth.ts` — signInAs(), loginAsInstructor()

## Notes
- Complex form with multiple Monaco editor instances
- Follow namespace isolation pattern from existing tests
- Target ~30s timeout

title: E2E: Problem publishing + student practice (problem-publishing.spec.ts)
description:
## Summary
Add E2E test for problem publishing flow and student practice workflow. Publishing is the bridge between instructor content and student access. Student practice (non-session coding + execution) is a primary workflow with zero coverage.

## File to create
`frontend/e2e/problem-publishing.spec.ts`

## Test: "Instructor publishes problem, student practices and runs code"
1. Create class, section, problem via API; register student via API
2. Instructor navigates to section detail → Problems tab
3. Publish problem via UI modal
4. Verify problem appears in published list
5. Toggle "Show Solution" setting
6. Student signs in, navigates to section → sees problem → clicks "Practice"
7. Student workspace loads with starter code
8. Student runs code → verifies execution output
9. Student views solution (when enabled)

## Key files to reference
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[sectionId]/` — Section detail page, publish modal
- `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/` — Student workspace
- `frontend/e2e/preview-mode.spec.ts` — Code execution assertion patterns
- `frontend/e2e/fixtures/api-setup.ts` — createProblem(), publishProblem(), registerStudent()

## Notes
- Two actors (instructor + student) but no real-time sync needed
- Target ~30s timeout

title: E2E: Student progress review (student-review.spec.ts)
description:

Add E2E test for the instructor student progress review / grading workflow. Complete feature area with zero E2E coverage.

`frontend/e2e/student-review.spec.ts`

title: E2E: Session start via UI (session-start.spec.ts)
description:
## Summary

`frontend/e2e/session-start.spec.ts`

title: Add comprehensive handler and store tests for API parity batch 2 endpoints
close_reason: 41 handler tests added covering all batch 2 endpoints, pushed to PR #24

title: ClassHandler.Get nil-check guard on sections dependency
description:
ClassHandler.Get checks h.sections != nil before enrichment. No other handler uses this pattern — deps should always be injected. Exists only so tests can pass nil.

Files: go-backend/internal/handler/classes.go (remove guard), go-backend/internal/handler/classes_test.go (provide mock section repo)
close_reason: Fixed: removed nil guard, tests provide mock

title: Add store layer integration tests (handler→store→PostgreSQL)
description:
## Summary

The current store integration tests (`store_*_integration_test.go`) don't actually test the Store methods. They copy-paste SQL queries and run them directly against a superuser connection, bypassing RLS entirely. This provides false confidence - tests pass but don't reflect production behavior.

## Problems with Current Tests

1. **Tests copy-paste SQL instead of calling Store methods** - Changes to actual Store methods won't be caught
2. **No RLS context set** - Tests run as superuser, bypassing all row-level security policies
3. **Don't instantiate Store type** - The `store.New()` constructor is never called
4. **Namespace isolation not tested** - A bug allowing cross-namespace data leaks would pass tests

## Example of Current (Broken) Pattern

```go
// Current: copies SQL from problems.go, runs directly
runFiltered := func(t *testing.T, filters ProblemFilters) []Problem {
    query := `SELECT ... FROM problems WHERE 1=1`  // <-- copied SQL, not real code
    rows, err := conn.Query(ctx, query, args...)   // <-- superuser connection
    // ...
}
```

## Required Fix

Tests should call actual Store methods with proper RLS context:

```go
// Acquire connection and set RLS context (like production middleware)
conn, _ := pool.Acquire(ctx)
defer conn.Release()

conn.Exec(ctx, "SET ROLE eval_app")
conn.Exec(ctx, "SELECT set_config('app.user_id', $1, true)", userID)
conn.Exec(ctx, "SELECT set_config('app.namespace_id', $1, true)", nsID)
conn.Exec(ctx, "SELECT set_config('app.role', $1, true)", "instructor")

// Create Store and call ACTUAL methods
s := store.New(conn)
problems, err := s.ListProblems(ctx, nil)  // <-- tests real code + RLS
```

## Files to Modify

- `go-backend/internal/store/store_methods_integration_test.go`
- `go-backend/internal/store/store_session_integration_test.go`
- `go-backend/internal/store/store_crud_integration_test.go`

## Implementation Steps

1. Add helper to set up RLS context (reuse pattern from `integration_test.go:setRLSContext`)
2. Rewrite each test to:
   - Acquire connection from pool
   - Set RLS context for test user
   - Call actual Store methods via `store.New(conn)`
   - Verify results
3. Add cross-namespace isolation tests (user in ns-A queries, verify ns-B data not returned)
4. Remove all copy-pasted SQL from test files

title: Instructor Preview as Student Mode
description:
Instructors need to see exactly what students see in their sections — the real student experience, not a simulation. This builds trust in the platform and lets instructors test problems end-to-end.

**Approach:** Create a shadow "preview student" user per instructor. When preview mode is active, middleware swaps the instructor's identity to the preview student. The preview student is a real user with a real student enrollment, so ALL existing RLS policies, permission checks, and handler logic work correctly without modification.

**UX:** In-page toggle on section page. "Preview as Student" button swaps to student view with a persistent banner. Full interaction (practice problems, run code, join sessions).

See plan: .claude/plans/foamy-mapping-twilight.md

title: Add preview store layer and auth context helpers
description:
**Summary:** Create the PreviewRepository store interface/implementation for managing preview student users and enrollments, plus auth context helpers for tracking preview state.

**Files to create:**
- `go-backend/internal/store/preview.go` — PreviewRepository interface + Store implementation

**Files to modify:**
- `go-backend/internal/auth/context.go` — add preview context helpers

**Files to read for context:**
- `go-backend/internal/store/users.go` — existing user CRUD patterns, column scanning, error handling
- `go-backend/internal/store/memberships.go` — membership CRUD patterns, CreateMembership/DeleteMembership
- `go-backend/internal/store/store.go` — Store struct, transaction patterns, pool vs connection usage
- `go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go` — existing repository interfaces and types (for reference only — do NOT add PreviewRepository here)
- `go-backend/internal/auth/context.go` — existing context helpers pattern (WithUser, UserFromContext, WithClaims)
- `go-backend/CLAUDE.md` — package conventions

**Implementation steps:**

Store layer (`store/preview.go`):
1. Define `PreviewStudent` struct: `InstructorID uuid.UUID`, `StudentUserID uuid.UUID`, `CreatedAt time.Time`
2. Define `PreviewRepository` interface as a **standalone interface** in this file — do NOT add it to the combined `Repos` interface in interfaces.go. PreviewRepository is pool-scoped (no RLS), injected directly into middleware and handlers, similar to how `UserLookup`/`UserRepository` is injected into `UserLoader`.
3. Interface methods:
   - `GetPreviewStudent(ctx, instructorID uuid.UUID) (*PreviewStudent, error)` — SELECT from preview_students JOIN users
   - `CreatePreviewStudent(ctx, instructorID uuid.UUID, namespaceID string) (*PreviewStudent, error)` — in a transaction: INSERT into users then INSERT into preview_students. **Important:** Cannot reuse existing `CreateUser` because `CreateUserParams.ExternalID` is a non-optional `string` and the preview user needs `external_id = NULL`. Write the INSERT query directly.
   - `EnrollPreviewStudent(ctx, studentUserID uuid.UUID, sectionID uuid.UUID) error` — INSERT INTO section_memberships ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (upsert)
   - `UnenrollPreviewStudentFromOtherSections(ctx, studentUserID uuid.UUID, keepSectionID uuid.UUID) error` — DELETE FROM section_memberships WHERE user_id = studentUserID AND section_id != keepSectionID
   - `UnenrollPreviewStudent(ctx, studentUserID uuid.UUID, sectionID uuid.UUID) error` — DELETE FROM section_memberships WHERE user_id AND section_id match
4. Implement all methods on `*Store`, following existing patterns (argCounter, error handling, scanners)

Auth context helpers (`auth/context.go`):
1. Define `PreviewContext` struct: `OriginalUser *User`, `SectionID uuid.UUID`
2. Add context key: `previewContextKey`
3. Add `WithPreviewContext(ctx context.Context, pc PreviewContext) context.Context`
4. Add `PreviewContextFrom(ctx context.Context) *PreviewContext` — returns nil if not set
5. Add `IsPreview(ctx context.Context) bool` — convenience, returns PreviewContextFrom(ctx) != nil

**Testing notes:** Unit tests for all store methods using integration test patterns (Docker Postgres). Test: create preview student, get preview student, enroll/unenroll, idempotent enrollment. Test auth context round-trip (WithPreviewContext → PreviewContextFrom).

title: Add preview middleware, handler, and server wiring
description:
**Summary:** Create the PreviewMiddleware (identity swap), preview handler (enter/exit endpoints), and wire everything into the server routes.

**Files to create:**
- `go-backend/internal/middleware/preview.go` — PreviewMiddleware
- `go-backend/internal/handler/preview.go` — PreviewHandler with enter/exit endpoints

**Files to modify:**
- `go-backend/internal/server/server.go` — wire middleware + mount routes

**Files to read for context:**
- `go-backend/internal/middleware/authn.go` — UserLoader pattern (takes a repo, returns middleware factory)
- `go-backend/internal/middleware/rls.go` — RLSContextMiddleware (understand ordering, how it reads auth.User)
- `go-backend/internal/middleware/authz.go` — RequirePermission pattern
- `go-backend/internal/handler/memberships.go` — handler pattern (chi URL params, JSON responses, error handling)
- `go-backend/internal/server/server.go` — route mounting, middleware chain order, dependency injection. Note line 276: `poolStore := store.New(pool.PgxPool())` for pool-level store creation pattern.
- `go-backend/internal/auth/context.go` — UserFromContext, WithUser, preview context helpers (from previous task)
- `go-backend/internal/auth/permissions.go` — PermContentManage constant
- `go-backend/internal/store/preview.go` — PreviewRepository interface (from previous task)
- `go-backend/CLAUDE.md` — package conventions

**Implementation steps:**

PreviewMiddleware (`middleware/preview.go`):
1. `PreviewMiddleware(previewRepo store.PreviewRepository) func(http.Handler) http.Handler`
2. On each request:
   - Read `X-Preview-Section` header; if empty, call next and return
   - Get instructor from `auth.UserFromContext(ctx)`; if nil or role is student, return 403
   - Parse section_id from header as UUID
   - Call `previewRepo.GetPreviewStudent(ctx, instructor.ID)`
   - If not found (ErrNotFound), return 412 Precondition Failed with message "preview not initialized, call POST /sections/{id}/preview first"
   - Build preview `auth.User`: ID=preview student's user_id, Role="student", NamespaceID=instructor's namespace_id, Email=preview student's email
   - Call `auth.WithUser(ctx, previewUser)` to swap identity
   - Call `auth.WithPreviewContext(ctx, PreviewContext{OriginalUser: instructor, SectionID: sectionID})`
   - Call next with updated context
3. **Critical ordering note:** This middleware MUST run after UserLoader and BEFORE RLSContextMiddleware. This is essential because RLSContextMiddleware reads `auth.UserFromContext(ctx)` to set PostgreSQL session variables (`app.user_id`, `app.role`, `app.namespace_id`). By swapping the user before RLS runs, all database queries execute with the preview student's identity — which is the entire point.

PreviewHandler (`handler/preview.go`):
1. `PreviewHandler` struct with `previewRepo store.PreviewRepository` (pool-level, no RLS)
2. `Routes() chi.Router` — apply `RequirePermission(auth.PermContentManage)` middleware
3. `POST /` (EnterPreview):
   - Parse section_id from chi URL param
   - Get instructor from `auth.UserFromContext(ctx)` — this endpoint does NOT go through PreviewMiddleware (mounted before it in the chain), so the user is the real instructor
   - **Authorization:** RequirePermission(PermContentManage) gates to instructor+. The preview student enrollment is sandboxed by RLS on subsequent requests, so additional section-level authorization is not strictly required. However, as defense-in-depth, verify the instructor can see the section by calling `repos.GetSection(ctx, sectionID)` through the RLS store — if the section is not visible to the instructor, this returns ErrNotFound.
   - Call previewRepo.GetPreviewStudent; if ErrNotFound, call CreatePreviewStudent
   - Call EnrollPreviewStudent(studentUserID, sectionID)
   - Call UnenrollPreviewStudentFromOtherSections(studentUserID, sectionID) — best-effort cleanup
   - Return JSON: `{ "preview_user_id": "...", "section_id": "..." }`
4. `DELETE /` (ExitPreview):
   - Parse section_id
   - Get preview student for this instructor
   - Call UnenrollPreviewStudent(studentUserID, sectionID) — best-effort
   - Return 204

Server wiring (`server.go`):
1. Create a pool-level Store for preview operations: `previewStore := store.New(pool.PgxPool())` — following the established pattern on line 276
2. Create PreviewHandler with previewStore
3. Mount preview routes on the authenticated route group but BEFORE PreviewMiddleware applies:
   - `r.Route("/api/v1/sections/{section_id}/preview", previewHandler.Routes)` — uses normal auth (JWT + UserLoader + RLS), no preview middleware
4. Add PreviewMiddleware to the authenticated route group, between UserLoader and RLSContext middleware:
   - Current: JWT → UserLoader → RLS
   - New: JWT → UserLoader → PreviewMiddleware → RLS

**Testing notes:** Unit tests for middleware (mock repo, verify context swap, verify passthrough when no header). Integration tests for handler (enter preview → verify preview student created and enrolled, exit preview → verify unenrolled). Integration test for full flow: enter preview → make API call with preview header → verify student-level RLS filtering.

title: Add frontend preview context, API client, and preview API
description:
**Summary:** Create the React context for preview mode state, the preview API client functions, and modify the base API client to inject the preview header on all requests when preview is active.

**Files to create:**
- `frontend/src/contexts/PreviewContext.tsx` — preview mode state management
- `frontend/src/lib/api/preview.ts` — enterPreview/exitPreview API functions

**Files to modify:**
- `frontend/src/lib/api-client.ts` — add preview header injection via module-level setter
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/layout.tsx` — wrap app with PreviewProvider

**Files to read for context:**
- `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` — existing context provider pattern, useAuth hook
- `frontend/src/lib/api-client.ts` — getAuthHeaders, apiFetch, base API patterns
- `frontend/src/lib/api/auth.ts` — existing API client function patterns
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/layout.tsx` — existing provider wrapping
- `frontend/src/scripts/check-api-imports.ts` — API import boundary rules (ensure preview imports don't violate them)
- `frontend/CLAUDE.md` — frontend conventions, API import boundary documentation

**Implementation steps:**

API client modification (`lib/api-client.ts`) — do this first to avoid circular imports:
1. Add a module-level variable: `let _previewSectionId: string | null = null`
2. Export setter: `export function setPreviewSectionId(id: string | null) { _previewSectionId = id; }`
3. In `getAuthHeaders()` or `apiFetch`: if `_previewSectionId` is set, add `X-Preview-Section: {_previewSectionId}` header
4. **Why module-level setter instead of importing from PreviewContext:** Avoids circular dependency. api-client.ts → (no import from contexts). PreviewContext → api/preview.ts → api-client.ts. Clean unidirectional flow.

Preview API (`lib/api/preview.ts`):
1. `enterPreview(sectionId: string): Promise<{ preview_user_id: string, section_id: string }>` — POST to `/api/v1/sections/${sectionId}/preview`
2. `exitPreview(sectionId: string): Promise<void>` — DELETE to `/api/v1/sections/${sectionId}/preview`
3. **Important:** These functions must NOT have the preview header set when called. Since enterPreview is called before setPreviewSectionId is set, this happens naturally. For exitPreview, PreviewContext should call setPreviewSectionId(null) BEFORE calling the API, or use a direct fetch without the header.

PreviewContext (`contexts/PreviewContext.tsx`):
1. Define `PreviewContextType`: `{ isPreview: boolean, previewSectionId: string | null, enterPreview: (sectionId: string) => Promise<void>, exitPreview: () => Promise<void> }`
2. Create `PreviewProvider` component:
   - State: `previewSectionId: string | null` (null = not in preview)
   - `enterPreview(sectionId)`: call API `POST /sections/{sectionId}/preview`, on success call `setPreviewSectionId(sectionId)` and set state
   - `exitPreview()`: call `setPreviewSectionId(null)` first, then call API `DELETE /sections/{sectionId}/preview` (best-effort), then clear state
   - Derive `isPreview` from `previewSectionId !== null`
   - Import `setPreviewSectionId` from `@/lib/api-client` (allowed direction, no circular import)
3. Export `usePreview()` hook

Layout (`app/(app)/layout.tsx`):
1. Wrap existing providers with `<PreviewProvider>`

**Testing notes:** Component tests for PreviewContext: verify enter/exit toggle state, verify setPreviewSectionId is called correctly. Test that enter/exit preview API calls do NOT include the preview header. Verify no API import boundary violations with `make check-api-imports`.

title: Add frontend preview UI — section page toggle, banner, and navigation
description:
**Summary:** Add the "Preview as Student" button to the instructor section view, create the preview mode banner, and update navigation to show student items during preview.

**Files to create:**
- `frontend/src/components/preview/PreviewBanner.tsx` — persistent banner shown during preview mode

**Files to modify:**
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/page.tsx` — add preview button, conditional view rendering
- `frontend/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` — show student nav items during preview
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/layout.tsx` — render PreviewBanner when preview active (if not already in section page)

**Files to read for context:**
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/page.tsx` — current section page, instructor vs student view branching
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/components/InstructorSectionView.tsx` — instructor view (where preview button goes)
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/components/StudentSectionView.tsx` — student view (what renders during preview)
- `frontend/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` — navigation rendering, getNavItemsForRole
- `frontend/src/config/navigation.ts` — nav item definitions, role filtering
- `frontend/src/contexts/PreviewContext.tsx` — usePreview hook (from previous task)
- `frontend/CLAUDE.md` — component and styling conventions

**Implementation steps:**

Section page (`sections/[section_id]/page.tsx`):
1. Import `usePreview` hook
2. In the instructor/student branching logic: if `isPreview && previewSectionId === section_id`, render `StudentSectionView` even if user is instructor
3. Add `?view=student` URL param handling: on mount, if param present and not already in preview, call `enterPreview(section_id)`
4. When entering preview, add `?view=student` to URL; when exiting, remove it

Instructor section view — add preview button:
1. Add "Preview as Student" button (could be in the section page header area or in InstructorSectionView)
2. On click: call `enterPreview(sectionId)`
3. Style: secondary/ghost button, visually distinct from primary actions

PreviewBanner (`components/preview/PreviewBanner.tsx`):
1. Fixed banner at top of page (or above main content)
2. Text: "You are previewing this section as a student"
3. "Exit Preview" button that calls `exitPreview()`
4. Visually distinct (e.g., yellow/amber background) so instructor always knows they're in preview
5. Should be rendered in the app layout when `isPreview` is true

Navigation (`components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`):
1. Import `usePreview` hook
2. When `isPreview` is true, use `getNavItemsForRole('student')` instead of the instructor's actual role
3. This ensures the instructor sees student navigation during preview

**Testing notes:** Component test for section page: verify preview button renders for instructors, verify StudentSectionView renders when preview active. Component test for PreviewBanner: verify renders with exit button. Test navigation shows student items during preview.

title: Fix CreatePreviewStudent TOCTOU race condition
Fix CreatePreviewStudent TOCTOU race condition
description:
Two concurrent POST /sections/{id}/preview calls for the same instructor both see ErrNotFound from GetPreviewStudent, both enter CreatePreviewStudent, and the second gets a unique violation → 500.

Files to modify:
- go-backend/internal/store/preview.go (~line 87-114)

Fix: Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (instructor_id) DO UPDATE SET instructor_id = EXCLUDED.instructor_id RETURNING student_user_id to make CreatePreviewStudent idempotent, or catch the unique violation and re-query.

Also fix trivial: EnrollPreviewStudent ON CONFLICT should specify explicit columns: ON CONFLICT (user_id, section_id) DO NOTHING.

title: Add RLS policy tests for preview_students table
description:
Migration 017 defines RLS policies on preview_students but no test verifies them.

Files to modify:
- go-backend/internal/store/rls_integration_test.go (or new preview_rls_integration_test.go)

Tests needed following patterns in rls_integration_test.go:
1. Instructor cannot see another instructor's preview_students row
2. INSERT via app role works when caller is instructor-or-higher
3. Student cannot INSERT a preview_students row

title: Fix preview handler and API client pattern divergences
description:
Three pattern divergences found by architecture review:

1. go-backend/internal/handler/preview.go uses httputil.WriteError for 500s instead of WriteInternalError (no error logging). Fix: replace all WriteError(w, 500, ...) with WriteInternalError(w, r, err, ...).

2. frontend/src/lib/api/preview.ts uses raw apiFetch for DELETE instead of apiDelete helper. Fix: use apiDelete.

3. frontend/src/lib/api/preview.ts hardcodes /api/v1/ prefix while all other domain clients omit it. Fix: use /sections/${sectionId}/preview.

4. Trivial: middleware/preview.go returns 403 for nil user but message says "authentication required" — should be 401.

title: Document coding-tool sync point after porting
description:
## Summary

After completing the current port (PLAT-dlg, PLAT-49j, and the practice mode/E2E issue), document the last-synced coding-tool commit hash so future sessions know where to pick up.

## Implementation

Add a section to CLAUDE.md or a dedicated file (e.g. `frontend/UPSTREAM_SYNC.md`) recording:
- Last synced coding-tool commit: (hash after porting is complete)
- Date of sync
- What was ported

This prevents re-analyzing the full commit history each time we need to port changes.

## Acceptance criteria

Each porting issue should update this file as part of its work. The file should show the most recent coding-tool commit that has been fully ported to eval.
close_reason: Created frontend/UPSTREAM_SYNC.md documenting last synced coding-tool commit (78067cb) with ported issues table.

title: Identity Platform SAML Integration
description:
## Summary
Configure Identity Platform for SAML-based authentication with enterprise identity providers (university SSO systems).

## Scope
- SAML Identity Provider configuration in Identity Platform
- Attribute mapping from SAML assertions
- Testing with a mock SAML IdP

## SAML Configuration (Manual in GCP Console)
1. Go to: https://console.cloud.google.com/customer-identity/providers
2. Click "Add a Provider" > "SAML"
3. Configure:
   - Provider name
   - IdP Entity ID
   - SSO URL
   - X.509 Certificate
4. Note the SP Entity ID and ACS URL for your IdP

## SAML Attribute Mapping Strategy
Map standard attributes:
- `email` → email
- `given_name` → first name
- `family_name` → last name

**IdP-specific attributes** (eduPersonAffiliation, eppn, department, etc.) are captured by the Go API on first login and stored in the application database per-tenant.

## Flows to Support
1. SP-initiated SSO (user clicks "Login with SSO" → redirect to IdP)
2. IdP-initiated SSO (user starts at IdP, lands at app)

## Deliverables
- SAML provider configured in Identity Platform
- Documentation on claim passthrough
- Mock SAML IdP setup for testing (e.g., samlidp, Keycloak)
- Integration test scripts

## Dependencies
- Requires: Identity Platform base setup (PLAT-dar)

## References
- GCP Identity Platform SAML docs
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for auth flow overview

title: Wire up Resend email for invitations (GCP Secret Manager + config + error handling)
Create GCP Secret Manager Terraform module
description:
## Summary
Create a reusable Terraform module for managing secrets via GCP Secret Manager, replacing the 1Password-based workflow.

## Files to create
- `infrastructure/terraform/modules/secrets/variables.tf` — accepts a list/map of secret IDs to manage
- `infrastructure/terraform/modules/secrets/main.tf` — creates `google_secret_manager_secret` resources and reads latest versions via `google_secret_manager_secret_version` data sources
- `infrastructure/terraform/modules/secrets/outputs.tf` — exposes secret values (marked sensitive) for consumption by k8s secrets
- `infrastructure/terraform/modules/secrets/versions.tf` — require google provider ~> 5.0 (match existing modules)

## Implementation steps
1. Create the module directory `infrastructure/terraform/modules/secrets/`
2. Define `variables.tf` with: `environment`, `project_name`, `project_id`, `region` (standard), plus `secret_ids` (list of string secret names to manage)
3. In `main.tf`:
   - Use `google_project_service` to enable `secretmanager.googleapis.com` API
   - Use `google_secret_manager_secret` with `for_each` over `secret_ids` to create secret resources with automatic replication
   - Use `data.google_secret_manager_secret_version` with `for_each` to read the latest version of each secret
4. In `outputs.tf`: output a map of secret_id → secret_data (sensitive)
5. In `versions.tf`: require `hashicorp/google ~> 5.0`

## Bootstrapping / chicken-and-egg
The data source `google_secret_manager_secret_version` will fail if a secret has no versions. Therefore, secret values MUST be created in GCP before the first `terraform apply`. The operator steps in PLAT-2b6.2 reflect this: create secrets and set values first, then apply. The module's `data` sources use `depends_on` on the `google_secret_manager_secret` resources to ensure correct ordering within Terraform.

## Design notes
- Secret *values* are set manually via `gcloud secrets versions add` or GCP Console — Terraform only manages the resource and reads values
- The module uses `for_each` so adding new secrets is just appending to the `secret_ids` list
- Follow the exact same module convention as `infrastructure/terraform/modules/cloudsql/` (common vars, versions.tf structure)

title: Wire secrets module into prod environment and migrate 1Password secrets
description:
## Summary
Integrate the new secrets module into the prod Terraform environment, migrating existing 1Password-sourced secrets and adding RESEND_API_KEY.

## Files to modify
- `infrastructure/terraform/environments/prod/main.tf` (lines 251-271 kubernetes_secret, lines 220-249 kubernetes_config_map)
- `infrastructure/terraform/environments/prod/variables.tf` (lines 193-211 oauth variables)

## Files to delete
- `infrastructure/terraform/environments/prod/secrets.tfvars.1password`
- `infrastructure/terraform/environments/prod/secrets.tfvars` (local-only, gitignored)

## Implementation steps
1. Add `module "secrets"` block to `main.tf` referencing `../../modules/secrets`:
   - `secret_ids = ["resend-api-key", "oauth-client-id", "oauth-client-secret"]`
2. Update `kubernetes_secret.app_secrets` data block:
   - Replace `OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET = module.identity_platform.oauth_client_secret` with `OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET = module.secrets.values["oauth-client-secret"]`
   - Add `RESEND_API_KEY = module.secrets.values["resend-api-key"]`
3. Update `kubernetes_config_map.app_config` data block:
   - Add `INVITE_BASE_URL = var.invite_base_url` (new variable, non-secret)
4. Update `module.identity_platform`:
   - Change `oauth_client_id` and `oauth_client_secret` to read from Secret Manager module outputs
5. Remove `oauth_client_id` and `oauth_client_secret` variables from `variables.tf` (lines 200-211) — the identity-platform module has defaults of "" for these, so removing the env-level variables is safe
6. Add `invite_base_url` variable to `variables.tf` (string, no default — must be set in terraform.tfvars)
7. Add `invite_base_url` to `terraform.tfvars` with the production frontend URL
8. Delete `secrets.tfvars.1password` (checked into git, contains 1Password references not actual secrets)
9. Delete `secrets.tfvars` if present locally (gitignored, contains resolved secrets)

## Manual operator steps — MUST run BEFORE first terraform apply
1. Create secrets in GCP: `gcloud secrets create resend-api-key --replication-policy=automatic` (repeat for oauth-client-id, oauth-client-secret)
2. Set values: `echo -n "value" | gcloud secrets versions add resend-api-key --data-file=-`
3. Copy oauth values from 1Password into GCP Secret Manager before proceeding
4. THEN run `terraform apply` — the data sources will read the values you just set
5. Verify pods pick up new env vars (rolling restart if needed)

title: Fix silent email failure in invitation handlers
description:
## Summary
Fix the silent email failure on invitation Create/SystemCreate. Add a logger to InvitationHandler, log email errors at WARN level, and return `email_sent` in the response so the frontend can prompt the user to resend. Resolves PLAT-px6.

## Files to modify
- `go-backend/internal/handler/invitations.go`
  - Add `logger *slog.Logger` field to `InvitationHandler` struct
  - Update `NewInvitationHandler` signature to accept `*slog.Logger`
  - Define a response wrapper struct for create responses:
    ```go
    type createInvitationResponse struct {
        *store.Invitation
        EmailSent bool `json:"email_sent"`
    }
    ```
  - In Create: replace `_ = h.emailClient.SendInvitation(...)` with error check, log at WARN, set `emailSent`, respond with wrapper struct
  - In SystemCreate: same fix
- `go-backend/internal/server/server.go`
  - Pass logger to `NewInvitationHandler`

## Design notes
- This is the first handler to use an injected logger — other handlers don't currently hold one. This is the correct pattern (matches AsyncSessionPublisher, RevisionBuffer) rather than using slog.Default().
- Keep Resend/SystemResend handlers unchanged — they already return HTTP 500 on failure, which is correct for explicit resend operations
- The `email_sent` field is only in create responses (not Get/List)

  ```
  RESEND_API_KEY=

  ```
- `.env.1password` — add `RESEND_API_KEY=op://${OP_VAULT}/resend/api-key` for devs using 1Password locally

title: Add tests for email failure logging and email_sent response field
description:
## Summary
Add unit tests covering the new email_sent response field and error logging behavior in invitation Create/SystemCreate handlers.

## Files to modify
- `go-backend/internal/handler/invitations_test.go`

## Implementation steps
1. Create a `failingEmailClient` test double that implements `email.Client` and returns an error from `SendInvitation`
2. Add test: Create with successful email → response includes `"email_sent": true` (decode into `createInvitationResponse` wrapper, not raw `store.Invitation`)
3. Add test: Create with failing email → response includes `"email_sent": false`, HTTP status is still 201
4. Add test: Create with failing email → verify WARN log is emitted (use `slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(buf, nil))` to capture log output, assert it contains "failed to send invitation email" and the invitation ID)
5. Add equivalent tests for SystemCreate
6. Update existing `TestCreateInvitation_Success` (and similar) to decode into the new `createInvitationResponse` wrapper type and assert `email_sent: true` — existing tests use `store.Invitation` which silently ignores the new field

## Design notes
- Follow existing test patterns in the handler package
- The `email.NoOpClient{}` already exists and returns nil — use it for success cases
- Use a custom failing client for error cases

title: Add concurrency limit to executor service
description:
## Summary
The executor service has no concurrency limit on nsjail process spawning. A burst of requests can exhaust pod resources before KEDA scales up.

## Files to modify
- `executor/internal/handler/execute.go`
- `executor/internal/config/config.go`

## Implementation
1. Add MaxConcurrentExecutions config field (default: e.g., 10)
2. Add a semaphore (chan struct{} or golang.org/x/sync/semaphore) in the handler
3. Return 429 Too Many Requests when semaphore is full
4. Add tests for concurrency limiting behavior

title: Add practice mode for completed sessions
description:
## Summary

Allow students to continue editing and running code after a session ends. Practice mode uses ephemeral execution (no persistent state) with rate limiting.

## Origin

coding-tool commit dbaf12a (#59). Port up to: 78067cb.

## Backend (Go)

**New handler:** `go-backend/internal/handler/practice.go` (or add to sessions handler)
- `POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/practice` - executes code via executor service
- Auth: verify user is a participant in the session
- Rate limit: 15 req/min per user
- Request body: `{ code, execution_settings }`
- Response: execution result (stdout, stderr, exit_code, etc.)

**Modify:** Remove or relax 'session must be active' check in the code save handler if it exists, so students can persist practice edits.

## Frontend

**File:** `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/page.tsx`

1. Add `handlePracticeRun` function that POSTs to `/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/practice`
2. In `handleRunCode`, delegate to `handlePracticeRun` when `sessionEnded` is true
3. Remove `sessionEnded` gate from debounced code save (allow saving in practice)
4. Change CodeEditor props to always enable interaction:
   - `readOnly={false}` (was `readOnly={sessionEnded}`)
   - `showRunButton={true}` (was `showRunButton={\!sessionEnded}`)
   - `onRun={handleRunCode}` (was `onRun={sessionEnded ? undefined : handleRunCode}`)
   - `onLoadStarterCode={handleLoadStarterCode}` (was gated on sessionEnded)

**Tests:** `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/__tests__/page-session-ended.test.tsx` - add practice run tests
close_reason: Added PracticeExecute handler with rate limiting (15/min), practice API client, updated student page for always-editable practice mode. 7 Go tests + 13 frontend tests pass.

title: Rebase conflict in PR #56: rate limiting middleware vs nested section routes
description:
## Summary
PR #56 (feature/rate-limiting) cannot merge after PR #60 was merged. Non-trivial conflict in go-backend/internal/server/server.go.

## Conflict Details
PR #60 moved section sub-resource routes (ListMembers, Leave, ListSessions, RegenerateCode, ListInstructors, AddInstructor, RemoveInstructor) from flat routes in server.go into SectionHandler.Routes() in sections.go. It also changed NewSectionHandler() to take a membershipHandler dependency.

PR #56 adds rate limiting middleware (readRL, writeRL, ForCategory) to those same routes at the flat-route level in server.go. Since those routes no longer exist in server.go, the rate limiting needs to be applied inside SectionHandler.Routes() in sections.go instead.

## Files Affected
- go-backend/internal/server/server.go — constructor signature conflict (NewSectionHandler takes membershipHandler now)
- go-backend/internal/handler/sections.go — rate limiting middleware needs to be added to the nested Routes() method

## Resolution Steps
1. Take HEAD's NewSectionHandler(membershipHandler) constructor call
2. Remove the flat section sub-resource routes from the incoming commit (they're now in Routes())
3. Add rate limiting middleware to the routes inside SectionHandler.Routes() in sections.go
4. SectionHandler needs access to the rate limiter — either pass it as a dependency or use middleware at the mount point
5. Run make test-api and make lint-api to verify

## PR Reference
PR #56: https://github.com/jdelfino/eval/pull/56

title: DeleteMembershipIfNotLast uses FOR UPDATE with aggregate — incompatible with PG 15+
description:
SELECT COUNT(*) ... FOR UPDATE is not valid in PostgreSQL 15+. Aggregates cannot use FOR UPDATE. Discovered by integration tests in 19c5304.

Files: go-backend/internal/store/memberships.go (DeleteMembershipIfNotLast)

Fix: Use a subquery or SELECT ... FOR UPDATE without the aggregate, then count in application code. Or use advisory locks.
close_reason: Fixed in e687a5a — replaced COUNT(*) FOR UPDATE with row-level SELECT FOR UPDATE

title: Fix ExecutionResult type: output and error should be optional (omitempty)
description: The ExecutionResult interface in frontend/src/types/api.ts declares output and error as required string fields, but the Go backend uses omitempty on these fields (pkg/executorapi/types.go). When the backend omits them, they are undefined at runtime despite TypeScript thinking they are always present. Fix: change to output?: string and error?: string.

title: Implement /trace endpoint in executor service
RBAC permission system with RequirePermission middleware
description:
Current RequireRole() middleware is brittle — adding a grader role requires updating every route. Replace with permission-based RBAC.

Simplified permission set (~10 capabilities vs coding-tool's 57):
- session.join, session.manage
- content.manage (CRUD classes/sections/problems)
- data.viewOwn, data.viewAll, data.export
- user.manage, user.changeRole
- namespace.manage, system.admin

Role→permission mapping in a single Go map. Middleware: RequirePermission("content.manage").

Files:
- go-backend/internal/auth/permissions.go — permission constants + role map
- go-backend/internal/middleware/authz.go — add RequirePermission() alongside existing RequireRole()
- Migrate existing RequireRole() calls to RequirePermission() in route definitions
close_reason: Completed - replaced RequireRole with RequirePermission, simplified from 57 to 10 permissions

title: Instructor student work review
description: Add ability for instructors to review student work in a section. Students roster on section page + student detail page with expandable code viewer.

title: Backend store layer: student progress + work review queries
description:
Add types and store methods for instructor student review.

## Summary
Add `StudentProgress` and `StudentWorkSummary` types to `store/interfaces.go`, extend `StudentWorkRepository` with two new methods, and implement the SQL queries in `store/student_work.go`.

## Files to modify
- `go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go` — Add `StudentProgress` and `StudentWorkSummary` types; add `ListStudentProgress` and `ListStudentWorkForReview` to `StudentWorkRepository` interface
- `go-backend/internal/store/student_work.go` — Implement both methods with SQL queries

## Implementation steps

1. Add `StudentProgress` struct to `interfaces.go`:
   - `UserID uuid.UUID`, `DisplayName string`, `Email string`, `ProblemsStarted int`, `TotalProblems int`, `LastActive *time.Time`
   - Note: DisplayName is non-pointer string because the SQL query uses COALESCE(u.display_name, u.email)

2. Add `StudentWorkSummary` struct to `interfaces.go`:
   - `Problem Problem`, `PublishedAt time.Time`, `StudentWork *StudentWork`

3. Add two methods to `StudentWorkRepository` interface:
   - `ListStudentProgress(ctx context.Context, sectionID uuid.UUID) ([]StudentProgress, error)`
   - `ListStudentWorkForReview(ctx context.Context, sectionID, studentUserID uuid.UUID) ([]StudentWorkSummary, error)`

4. Implement `ListStudentProgress` in `student_work.go`:
   - JOIN section_memberships (role='student') + users + LEFT JOIN student_work
   - Use `COALESCE(u.display_name, u.email)` for display_name to handle nullable column
   - Scalar subquery for total_problems from section_problems
   - ORDER BY last active DESC (with COALESCE fallback to joined_at)

5. Implement `ListStudentWorkForReview` in `student_work.go`:
   - JOIN section_problems + problems, LEFT JOIN student_work for specific user
   - Return full problem details + nullable student work
   - Use nullable scan pattern (see `scanPublishedProblemWithStatus` in `section_problems.go` for reference)

6. Add store integration tests in `go-backend/internal/store/student_work_integration_test.go`:
   - Test ListStudentProgress with students who have/haven't started work
   - Test ListStudentWorkForReview with partial problem completion

## Notes
- No migration needed — existing RLS policies on student_work already allow is_section_instructor(section_id) to SELECT
- Follow existing scan helper patterns (scanStudentWork, etc.)
- users.display_name is nullable (*string in Go User struct) — use COALESCE in SQL to avoid null scan errors

title: Backend handler + routes for student review

title: Frontend API types + client for student review
Frontend students roster in InstructorSectionView
description:
Add Students section to InstructorSectionView showing enrolled students with progress stats. Include component tests.

## Summary
Fetch student progress data on the section page and render a Students section in InstructorSectionView with name, progress bar, and last active time. Each row links to the student detail page.

## Files to modify
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/page.tsx` — Conditionally fetch listStudentProgress for instructors, pass as prop
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/components/InstructorSectionView.tsx` — Add students prop, render Students section

## Files to modify for tests
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/__tests__/InstructorSectionView.test.tsx` (if exists, otherwise create)

## Implementation steps

1. Modify `page.tsx`:
   - Import `listStudentProgress` from `@/lib/api/student-review`
   - Add `students` state: `useState<StudentProgress[]>([])`
   - IMPORTANT: `listStudentProgress` is gated behind PermContentManage (instructor-only). The current `loadSectionData` runs for all users. Must conditionally fetch: check `isInstructor` before calling, or fetch separately after knowing the role. The `user.role` is available from the auth context before `loadSectionData` runs. Approach: add `listStudentProgress(section_id)` to the Promise.all only when the user role is instructor/namespace-admin/system-admin. Otherwise students will get a 403.
   - Pass `students={students}` to `InstructorSectionView`

2. Modify `InstructorSectionView.tsx`:
   - Import `StudentProgress` from `@/types/api`
   - Add `students: StudentProgress[]` to props interface
   - Add "Students" section after "Published Problems":
     - Heading: "Students" with count badge (like Active Sessions)
     - Table/list rows, each showing:
       - Student display_name (or email fallback)
       - Progress: "X / Y problems"
       - Last active: relative time (e.g., "2h ago") or "Never" if null
       - Link: clicking row navigates to `/sections/${section.id}/students/${student.user_id}`
     - Empty state: "No students enrolled yet"

3. Add component tests:
   - Renders "Students" heading with count
   - Renders student names and progress
   - Empty state when no students
   - Student rows are clickable links to detail page

## Notes
- Follow existing section styling (white cards, shadow, hover states)
- Use Link from next/navigation for student rows (not onClick + router.push)
- Relative time can be simple: calculate from Date.now() - last_active
- The role check for conditional fetch should use the same logic as `isInstructor` on line 108 of page.tsx

title: Frontend student detail page with expandable code viewer
description:
Create student detail page showing all problems with expandable read-only code viewer. Include component tests.

## Summary
New page at `/sections/[section_id]/students/[user_id]` that fetches the student's work across all section problems and displays them as expandable cards with code preview.

## Files to create
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/students/[user_id]/page.tsx` — Student detail page

## Files to create for tests
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/students/[user_id]/__tests__/page.test.tsx`

## Files to modify
- `frontend/src/config/navigation.ts` — Add breadcrumb hierarchy entry

## Implementation steps

1. Create `page.tsx` as a 'use client' page:
   - Extract `section_id` and `user_id` from `useParams()`
   - Fetch `listStudentWorkForReview(section_id, user_id)` and `listStudentProgress(section_id)` on mount
   - Find student name from progress data (matching user_id)
   - Verify user is instructor (redirect if not)

2. Page layout:
   - BackButton to `/sections/${section_id}` with "Back to Section"
   - Header card: student display_name (or email), progress summary ("X / Y problems started")
   - Problem list below header

3. Problem list items (expandable):
   - Each problem shows: title, status badge (Started / Not started), last_update time
   - Click toggles expand/collapse (useState for expanded problem IDs)
   - When expanded: show code in `<pre><code>` block with monospace font, gray background
   - When student_work is null: show "Not started" in muted text, no expand
   - When student_work exists but code is empty: show "No code yet"

4. Loading and error states:
   - Loading spinner while fetching
   - Error state with back button

5. Update breadcrumb hierarchy in `frontend/src/config/navigation.ts`:
   - Add entry: `'/sections/[section_id]/students/[user_id]': '/sections/[section_id]'`

6. Add component tests:
   - Renders student name and progress summary
   - Shows problem titles with correct status
   - Expanding a problem shows code
   - "Not started" problems are not expandable
   - Back button links to section page

## Notes
- No Monaco editor in v1 — use <pre><code> for simplicity
- Follow existing page patterns (loading state, error state, auth check)
- Style code block: bg-gray-50, p-4, rounded, overflow-x-auto, text-sm font-mono
- Fetching listStudentProgress to get student name is acceptable for v1. Can be optimized later by adding student name to the work review endpoint response.

title: Port projector view and CodeEditor improvements from coding-tool
description:
## Summary Several UX improvements were made to the projector/public-view and CodeEditor in the coding-tool repo that need to be ported to this project. These improve the projector display by maximizing code visibility. ## Origin Ported from coding-tool commits: cc27abb (#57), f14f3db, 61b1176, aa77530, 724dd02. ## Changes needed ### 1. CodeEditor: add `outputCollapsible` prop **File:** `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/components/CodeEditor.tsx` Add a new optional prop `outputCollapsible?: boolean` (default false). When true AND output is positioned right AND on desktop: - Show a collapse/expand toggle button (ChevronLeft/ChevronRight) between the editor and output panel - When collapsed, output panel width is 0 with overflow hidden - Toggle button uses `data-testid='output-collapse-toggle'` State: `const [outputCollapsed, setOutputCollapsed] = useState(false)` Condition: `const showOutputCollapseToggle = outputCollapsible && outputPosition === 'right' && isDesktop` Reference: coding-tool CodeEditor.tsx lines 45, 73, 110-112, 1011-1040 ### 2. Projector view: remove collapsible header, show join code in header slot **File:** `frontend/src/app/(projector)/public-view/page.tsx` - Remove the entire collapsible header section (lines 154-187) including the ChevronDown/ChevronUp toggle, problem title, description, and inline join code - Move the join code into the header slot alongside ConnectionStatus: ```tsx setHeaderSlot(
{state?.join_code || '------'}
); ``` - Remove unused imports: ChevronDown, ChevronUp, MarkdownContent - Remove `headerCollapsed` state - Add `outputCollapsible={true}` to both CodeEditor instances ### 3. Editor gutter refinements **File:** `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/components/CodeEditor.tsx` In Monaco editor options (~line 967): - Add `lineNumbersMinChars: 2` (reduces gutter width) - Change `lineDecorationsWidth: debuggerHook?.hasTrace ? 10 : 0` to `lineDecorationsWidth: debuggerHook?.hasTrace ? 10 : 8` ### 4. Projector favicon **New file:** `frontend/src/app/(projector)/icon.svg` Copy the green gradient SVG favicon from coding-tool (`/workspaces/coding-tool/src/app/(projector)/icon.svg`). This gives the projector tab a distinct green icon vs the main app's blue icon. ## Testing - Add tests for CodeEditor outputCollapsible (see coding-tool's CodeEditor-output-collapsible.test.tsx) - Update public-view page tests to verify no collapsible header and outputCollapsible prop - Verify projector favicon renders in browser

title: Namespace admin oversight view for all classes/sections in namespace
Database Schema & Row-Level Security
description:
## Summary
Design and implement the PostgreSQL database schema with Row-Level Security (RLS) policies for multi-tenant isolation.

## Scope
- Complete database schema (tables, indexes, constraints)
- RLS policies using PostgreSQL session variables
- Database migrations tooling (golang-migrate or similar)
- Seed data for development/testing

## Core Tables (based on existing app)
- `namespaces` - multi-tenant organizations
- `users` - user accounts (linked to Cognito)
- `classes` - courses
- `sections` - class sections
- `problems` - assignment problems
- `sessions` - live coding sessions
- `invitations` - user invitations

## RLS Implementation
```sql
-- Example RLS policy pattern
ALTER TABLE classes ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON classes
  USING (namespace_id = current_setting('app.namespace_id', true)::uuid);
```

Session variables set by middleware:
- `app.user_id` - current user UUID
- `app.namespace_id` - current tenant UUID
- `app.role` - user role for permission checks

## Deliverables
- SQL migration files: `go-backend/migrations/`
- Schema documentation
- RLS policy test suite
- Development seed data script

## Dependencies
- Requires: RDS PostgreSQL Database (PLAT-etu)

## References
- See `/docs/MIGRATION.md` Phase 2: Auth + Database Schema
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for RLS middleware pattern

title: Student home/dashboard page showing enrolled sections and active sessions
Unify local and CI build tooling (eliminate Make/CI mismatch)
description:
## Summary
The Makefile is used for some local tasks (build, test, lint, docker-build) but CI workflows duplicate the same commands in GitHub Actions YAML. This creates a maintenance burden where changes must be made in two places, and CI behavior can drift from local behavior.

## Problem
Examples of duplication:
- `make test-api` runs `go test -race ./...` — go-api.yaml also runs `go test -v -race ./...` (different flags)
- `make lint-api` runs `golangci-lint run ./...` — go-api.yaml uses `golangci-lint-action@v4`
- `make docker-build-api` builds with `go-backend/` as context — go-api.yaml builds with `.` as context and `-f go-backend/Dockerfile`
- Frontend CI has no `docker-build-frontend` make target, while go-api and executor do

The Makefile is incomplete (missing `docker-build-frontend`) and CI doesn't use it, so adding to one doesn't fix the other.

## Options to evaluate
1. **Make CI use Makefile targets** — CI steps become `make test-api`, `make lint-api`, etc. Single source of truth. But some CI-specific needs (caching, actions) may not fit.
2. **Remove Makefile, use scripts/** — Each task gets a script (`scripts/test-api.sh`), CI and local both call it. More explicit.
3. **Use a task runner** (just, taskfile, etc.) — Modern alternative to Make with better cross-platform support.
4. **Accept the duplication** — Document it and move on. Pragmatic but accumulates drift.

## Action items
- Decide on approach
- Implement chosen solution
- Ensure `docker-build-frontend` works identically locally and in CI

title: Create Firebase email link helper
description:
## Summary
Create a shared helper module for Firebase email link sign-in operations. This centralizes all email link logic and handles test mode gracefully.

## Files to create
- `frontend/src/lib/firebase-email-link.ts`

## Existing patterns to follow
- `frontend/src/lib/auth-provider.ts` — uses `isTestMode()` check and lazy imports
- `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` — lazy-imports Firebase modules with `await import('firebase/auth')`

## Implementation steps
1. Create `frontend/src/lib/firebase-email-link.ts` with three exported functions:

### `sendInvitationEmail(email: string, invitationToken: string): Promise<void>`
- If `isTestMode()`, return immediately (no-op)
- Lazy import `sendSignInLinkToEmail` from `firebase/auth` and `firebaseAuth` from `@/lib/firebase`
- Build `actionCodeSettings`: `{ url: '${window.location.origin}/invite/accept?token=${invitationToken}', handleCodeInApp: true }`
- Call `sendSignInLinkToEmail(firebaseAuth, email, actionCodeSettings)`

### `checkIsSignInWithEmailLink(url: string): Promise<boolean>`
- If `isTestMode()`, return `false`
- Lazy import `isSignInWithEmailLink` from `firebase/auth` and `firebaseAuth` from `@/lib/firebase`
- Return `isSignInWithEmailLink(firebaseAuth, url)`

### `completeSignInWithEmailLink(email: string, url: string): Promise<UserCredential>`
- Lazy import `signInWithEmailLink` from `firebase/auth` and `firebaseAuth` from `@/lib/firebase`
- Return `signInWithEmailLink(firebaseAuth, email, url)`

2. Add unit tests in `frontend/src/lib/__tests__/firebase-email-link.test.ts`:
   - Test that all functions no-op / return false in test mode
   - Test that Firebase SDK functions are called with correct args in production mode (mock firebase/auth)

## Notes
- Import `isTestMode` from `@/lib/auth-provider` (already exported)
- Use lazy imports (`await import(...)`) to avoid importing Firebase in test mode
