title: Change executor ScaledObject minReplicaCount to 0 for scale-to-zero
description:
## Summary
The executor ScaledObject has minReplicaCount: 1, which keeps at least one executor pod running at all times. This prevents the executor node pool from scaling to zero. Change to minReplicaCount: 0 so the entire executor pool can scale down when idle.

## Files to modify
- `k8s/base/executor-scaledobject.yaml` (line 8)

## Before → After
Before:
```yaml
  minReplicaCount: 1
```

After:
```yaml
  minReplicaCount: 0
```

## Notes
- With minReplicaCount: 0, there will be a cold start delay when the first execution request arrives (node pool scale-up + pod scheduling). This is acceptable for development.
- For production, change back to minReplicaCount: 1 to eliminate cold starts.

title: Port input echo from coding-tool: prepend Python preamble overriding input() to print values inline in stdout
Unify CI and local test scripts to use the same entrypoint
Add handler-level error logging for 500 responses
Replace NoRLSStoreMiddleware with limited registration role
description:
## Summary

The `NoRLSStoreMiddleware` bypasses RLS entirely for registration routes, giving handlers unrestricted database access. This is a security concern - we should instead use a limited PostgreSQL role with specific RLS policies.

## Current Problem

Registration routes (`/auth/accept-invite`, `/auth/register-student`) need database access before a user exists. Currently, `NoRLSStoreMiddleware` provides a Store without any RLS context, meaning handlers have full access to all tables.

## Solution

Create RLS policies for a 'registration' context that only allow the specific operations needed:

### Exact Permissions

| Table | SELECT | INSERT | UPDATE | DELETE |
|-------|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| invitations | pending only | ❌ | pending→accepted | ❌ |
| sections | active only | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| classes | ✅ (via section) | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| users | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| section_memberships | ❌ | student role only | ❌ | ❌ |
| namespaces | active only | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |

### Implementation

1. Add migration with helper function and RLS policies:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION is_registration_context() RETURNS boolean AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN current_setting('app.role', true) = 'registration';
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE;

-- invitations: SELECT pending, UPDATE pending→accepted
CREATE POLICY "invitations_registration_select" ON invitations
  FOR SELECT USING (is_registration_context() AND status = 'pending');

CREATE POLICY "invitations_registration_update" ON invitations
  FOR UPDATE USING (is_registration_context() AND status = 'pending')
  WITH CHECK (status = 'accepted');

-- sections: SELECT active only
CREATE POLICY "sections_registration_select" ON sections
  FOR SELECT USING (is_registration_context() AND active = true);

-- classes: SELECT (needed for join code info)
CREATE POLICY "classes_registration_select" ON classes
  FOR SELECT USING (is_registration_context());

-- users: INSERT only
CREATE POLICY "users_registration_insert" ON users
  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (is_registration_context());

-- memberships: INSERT student role only
CREATE POLICY "memberships_registration_insert" ON section_memberships
  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (is_registration_context() AND role = 'student');

-- namespaces: SELECT active only (needed for FK)
CREATE POLICY "namespaces_registration_select" ON namespaces
  FOR SELECT USING (is_registration_context() AND active = true);
```

2. Rename `NoRLSStoreMiddleware` → `RegistrationStoreMiddleware`

3. Update middleware to set `app.role = 'registration'` instead of bypassing RLS

## Files to Modify

- `migrations/00X_registration_rls.up.sql` (new)
- `migrations/00X_registration_rls.down.sql` (new)
- `go-backend/internal/middleware/rls.go`
- `go-backend/internal/server/server.go`

## Security Benefit

- RLS is still enforced, just with limited policies
- Registration can only access what it needs
- Cannot read existing users, problems, sessions, revisions, etc.
- Audit trail maintained

title: ElastiCache Redis Cluster
description:
## Summary
Provision ElastiCache Redis for caching, rate limiting, and Centrifugo pub/sub backend.

## Scope
Terraform modules for:
- ElastiCache Redis cluster (cluster mode disabled for simplicity)
- Subnet group (database subnets)
- Security group (allow access from EKS only)
- Parameter group with appropriate settings

## Configuration
- Node type: cache.t3.micro (staging), cache.r6g.large (prod)
- Replicas: 0 for staging, 2 for prod (Multi-AZ)
- Encryption: In-transit and at-rest
- Eviction policy: volatile-lru

## Use Cases
- Centrifugo pub/sub backend (channel presence, message routing)
- Rate limiting (sliding window counters)
- Session caching (optional, JWTs are stateless)
- API response caching (optional)

## Deliverables
- Terraform module: `infrastructure/terraform/modules/elasticache/`
- Outputs: Primary endpoint, reader endpoint, port

## Dependencies
- Requires: VPC & Networking Foundation (PLAT-03e)

## References
- See `/docs/MIGRATION.md` Phase 1: Infrastructure Foundation
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for caching strategy

title: Upgrade beads from v0.49.1 to latest Dolt-backed release
description:
Upgrade beads (bd) from v0.49.1 to the latest stable Dolt-backed release (currently v0.57.0, but wait for stability).

## Why

The SQLite+JSONL backend we're on is no longer maintained. The Dolt backend provides:
- Cell-level merge (vs line-level JSONL conflicts)
- Shared database across git worktrees (instant visibility, no stale copies)
- Better concurrent write support (server mode)
- Sync via existing GitHub repo using git+ssh:// remote (refs/dolt/data)

## When to pull the trigger

Wait until 2-3 consecutive releases without major Dolt backend fixes (transaction isolation, schema re-init, batch commit races). As of v0.57.0 the fix rate is still high -- check release notes before upgrading.

## Migration steps

1. bd export -- save current issues as JSONL backup
2. Upgrade bd binary (brew, mise, or install script)
3. bd init -- re-initializes with Dolt backend
4. bd dolt remote add origin git+ssh://git@github.com/jdelfino/eval.git
5. bd dolt push -- initial push to GitHub
6. Update AGENTS.md -- replace bd sync with bd dolt push / bd dolt pull
7. Remove sync-branch from .beads/config.yaml
8. Optionally enable auto-push in config (dolt.auto-push: true)
9. Verify: bd list, bd ready, bd dolt pull from a fresh clone
10. Remove legacy .beads/beads.db and JSONL sync artifacts

## Files to update

- .beads/config.yaml -- remove sync-branch, add dolt config
- AGENTS.md -- replace bd sync references
- CLAUDE.md -- update bd commands if needed
- Devcontainer setup if bd install method changes

## Risks

- Data loss during migration (mitigated by JSONL export backup)
- git+ssh:// remote is new (Feb 2026) -- may have rough edges
- Dolt server auto-start may need devcontainer adjustments

title: Fix executor scale-to-zero: deploy GMP frontend, configure KEDA properly
Featured student code inputs not populated on public view
description:
## Bug

When an instructor features a student on the public view, the student's code inputs (stdin) are not populated. The code appears but inputs are empty.

## Root Cause

The `student_code_updated` realtime event only publishes `code` — it does NOT include `execution_settings` (which contains stdin inputs). When the instructor's frontend receives code updates via WebSocket, it updates the student's code but never receives their execution_settings. So when the instructor clicks "Feature", the `students` map has stale/missing execution_settings, and null is sent to the backend.

## Files to Modify

1. `go-backend/internal/realtime/events.go` (~line 34-37) — Add `ExecutionSettings json.RawMessage` to `StudentCodeUpdatedData`
2. `go-backend/internal/realtime/session_publisher.go` (~line 53-57) — Update `CodeUpdated` signature to accept and pass execution_settings
3. `go-backend/internal/handler/session_students.go` (~line 173) — Pass `req.ExecutionSettings` in the `CodeUpdated` publisher call
4. `frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts` (~line 260-279) — Update `student_code_updated` handler to store received execution_settings on the student

Also update all callers/mocks/tests for the changed `CodeUpdated` signature:
5. `go-backend/internal/realtime/session_publisher_test.go` — Update test for CodeUpdated
6. `go-backend/internal/handler/testutil_test.go` — Update mock publisher
7. `go-backend/internal/handler/session_students_test.go` — Update tests
8. `go-backend/internal/realtime/session_publisher.go` — Update SessionPublisher interface
9. `frontend/src/hooks/__tests__/useRealtimeSession.test.ts` — Update test for student_code_updated event

## Implementation Steps

1. Add `ExecutionSettings json.RawMessage` field to `StudentCodeUpdatedData` struct (with `json:"execution_settings,omitempty"`)
2. Update `CodeUpdated` method signature on `SessionPublisher` interface to accept `executionSettings json.RawMessage`
3. Update `sessionPublisher.CodeUpdated` implementation to pass execution_settings into the data struct
4. Update handler call at session_students.go:173 to pass `req.ExecutionSettings`

title: Reopen session does not end other active sessions in the same section
description:
## Summary

The Reopen handler (POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/reopen) in go-backend/internal/handler/sessions.go:316-349 sets a completed session back to active without first ending any other active sessions in the same section. This violates the one-active-session-per-section invariant and can result in two active sessions in the same section.

## Reproduction

1. Create session A in section X (A is active)
2. End session A (A is completed)
3. Create session B in section X (B is active)
4. Reopen session A - both A and B are now active

## Files to modify

- go-backend/internal/handler/sessions.go - Reopen handler (~line 316): call EndActiveSessions before setting status to active, publish SessionReplaced events for any ended sessions
- go-backend/internal/handler/sessions_test.go - Add test for this scenario

## Implementation

1. In Reopen handler, after the conflict check, call repos.EndActiveSessions(r.Context(), existing.SectionID)
2. Publish SessionReplaced events for ended session IDs (mirroring Create handler pattern at lines 189-192)
3. Add test: reopen a session when another is active in the same section, verify the other was ended

Configure Resend SMTP for Identity Platform
description:
## Summary
Configure Identity Platform to use Resend for sending verification and password reset emails.

## Prerequisites
- Resend account configured
- Domain verified in Resend
- API key available

## Configuration Steps

### 1. Get Resend SMTP Credentials
From Resend dashboard:
- SMTP Host: `smtp.resend.com`
- Port: `465` (SSL) or `587` (TLS)
- Username: `resend`
- Password: Your Resend API key

### 2. Configure in GCP Console
1. Go to: https://console.cloud.google.com/customer-identity/settings
2. Navigate to "Email templates" or "SMTP settings"
3. Enable custom SMTP
4. Enter Resend SMTP credentials
5. Set sender address (must match verified domain in Resend)

### 3. Verify Configuration
- Send test verification email
- Check Resend dashboard for delivery status
- Verify email arrives with correct sender

## Terraform (if supported)
```hcl
# Note: SMTP config may require manual setup in console
# Document the configuration for reproducibility
```

## Done When
- [ ] Resend SMTP configured in Identity Platform
- [ ] Test email sends successfully
- [ ] Sender shows custom domain (not firebaseapp.com)
- [ ] Configuration documented for other environments

title: Typed API client layer
description:
Frontend hooks call generic apiGet<T>('/path') where T is whatever the developer guesses. Hook unit tests mock responses with wrong shapes (e.g., { classes: [...] } when the Go API returns [...]). Contract tests validate the real shapes but share zero code with hooks or unit tests. Result: systematic response-format bugs that only surface in E2E tests.

Solution: Create typed API functions (one per endpoint) in lib/api/ that hooks call instead of raw apiGet<T>. Contract tests call the same typed functions against the real API. Hook unit tests mock the typed module — mocks are type-safe by construction.

Scope: All 8 hooks (~35 API calls), their unit/integration tests, contract tests.
Out of scope: ~70 direct apiFetch calls in components/pages (follow-up epic PLAT-7vg).

See plan: .claude/plans/serialized-noodling-dijkstra.md

title: Typed API client: foundation + classes
description:
Create lib/api/ module structure and lib/api/classes.ts with 9 typed functions. This subtask establishes the pattern for all other subtasks.

## Implementation steps

1. Create frontend/src/lib/api/classes.ts with typed functions:
   - listClasses(): Promise<Class[]> — GET /classes
   - createClass(name, description?): Promise<Class> — POST /classes
   - updateClass(id, updates): Promise<Class> — PATCH /classes/{id}
   - deleteClass(id): Promise<void> — DELETE /classes/{id}
   - createSection(classId, name, semester?): Promise<Section> — POST /classes/{classId}/sections
   - updateSection(sectionId, updates): Promise<Section> — PATCH /sections/{sectionId}
   - regenerateJoinCode(sectionId): Promise<Section> — POST /sections/{sectionId}/regenerate-code
   - addCoInstructor(sectionId, email): Promise<void> — POST /sections/{sectionId}/instructors
   - removeCoInstructor(sectionId, userId): Promise<void> — DELETE /sections/{sectionId}/instructors/{userId}

2. Create frontend/src/lib/api/index.ts re-exporting all modules.

3. Migrate frontend/src/hooks/useClasses.ts to import and call typed functions instead of raw apiGet/apiPost/apiPatch/apiDelete.

4. Update hook unit tests to mock typed module with correct shapes:
   - hooks/__tests__/useClasses.test.ts — jest.mock('@/lib/api/classes'), mock returns plain arrays/objects (not wrapped)
   - hooks/__tests__/useClasses.integration.test.ts — fix mock fetch shapes from { classes: [...] } to [...]
   - hooks/__tests__/useClasses-logic.integration.test.ts — same fix

5. Update contract test infrastructure for typed function calls:
   - __tests__/contract/helpers.ts — add setupTestAuth() that calls setTestUser() from @/lib/test-auth
   - __tests__/contract/globalSetup.ts — set NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_MODE env vars

6. Update __tests__/contract/classes.integration.test.ts to call listClasses() directly instead of contractFetch('/api/v1/classes').

## Key response shape corrections
- GET /classes: { classes: Class[] } → Class[] (plain array)
- POST /classes: { class: Class } → Class (direct object)
- PATCH /classes/{id}: { class: Class } → Class
- POST /classes/{classId}/sections: { section: Section } → Section
- PATCH /sections/{id}: { section: Section } → Section
- POST /sections/{id}/regenerate-code: { join_code: string } → Section (full object)

## Files to modify
- frontend/src/lib/api/classes.ts (new)
- frontend/src/lib/api/index.ts (new)
- frontend/src/hooks/useClasses.ts
- frontend/src/hooks/__tests__/useClasses.test.ts
- frontend/src/hooks/__tests__/useClasses.integration.test.ts
- frontend/src/hooks/__tests__/useClasses-logic.integration.test.ts
- frontend/src/__tests__/contract/classes.integration.test.ts
- frontend/src/__tests__/contract/helpers.ts
- frontend/src/__tests__/contract/globalSetup.ts

title: Rename 'Coding Tool' to 'Eval' in UI branding
Add named port to go-api deployment for PodMonitoring clarity
Rate limiting on auth routes
description:
Auth endpoints are public attack surface. Need brute-force protection.

Redis-based sliding window rate limiter (already have Redis for Centrifugo):
- Auth routes (login, register): 5 req/min per IP
- Join-code validation: 10 req/min per IP
- Middleware that wraps specific route groups
- Graceful degradation if Redis unavailable

Note: Token revocation (Redis blocklist) should be implemented alongside user management endpoints (PLAT-vyf.10). When a user is deleted/deactivated, add their subject to a Redis blocklist with TTL matching token expiry (~1h). No separate issue — Redis infrastructure comes from this work.

Files:
- go-backend/internal/middleware/ratelimit.go — rate limit middleware
- go-backend/internal/server/server.go — apply to auth route group

title: Fix type mismatches in migrated components
description:
## Summary
After migrating components from direct apiFetch calls to the typed API client (PLAT-7vg),
there are type mismatches between local component interfaces and the canonical API types.

## Root Cause
Components define local interfaces (e.g., local User, Invitation, ClassInfo types) that use
`| undefined` for optional fields, while the API types use `| null`. Also, some local types
expect additional computed properties that the API doesn't return.

## Files Affected
- frontend/src/app/(app)/admin/page.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(app)/classes/components/SectionCard.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/ClassList.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/InstructorDashboard.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/ProblemCreator.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/ProblemLibrary.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/SectionView.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/SessionsList.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(app)/namespace/invitations/page.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[sectionId]/page.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(app)/system/page.tsx
- frontend/src/app/(projector)/public-view/page.tsx
- frontend/src/components/NamespaceHeader.tsx

## Implementation Steps
1. For each file, either:
   a. Import the canonical type from @/types/api or @/lib/api/* and remove the local definition
   b. Update the local interface to match the API type (null instead of undefined)
2. Handle cases where components expect computed properties (e.g., section_count) by either:
   a. Updating the API to return these properties
   b. Computing them client-side from the returned data
3. Run TypeScript and tests after each file to verify compatibility

## Test Command
cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit
close_reason: Stale: tsc --noEmit compiles clean, all files already use canonical API types

title: Executor timeout detection is unreliable and has no Go-level deadline
description:
## Summary
Timeout detection uses a fragile wall-clock heuristic that can misclassify non-timeout failures. Also, no Go-level context deadline is set on the exec.Command, so if nsjail hangs, the sandbox.Run blocks indefinitely.

## Files to modify
- `executor/internal/sandbox/sandbox.go` (lines 168-171, and around line 140)

## Implementation
1. Pass a context with timeout to exec.CommandContext (e.g., timeoutSec + small buffer for nsjail overhead)
2. Detect timeout via context.DeadlineExceeded rather than wall-clock heuristic
3. Keep exit code 137 as a secondary signal
4. Add tests for the improved timeout detection
close_reason: Implemented: context deadline replaces wall-clock heuristic

title: Consolidate duplicate testDB/integrationDB helpers in store integration tests
Port: practice mode entry point from public problem page (coding-tool a6f1210)
description:
## Summary

Add a "Practice" button to the public problem page so students can jump into a practice session directly from a problem listing. When clicked, the backend finds an existing completed session for that problem+section, or creates one on the fly, then redirects the student to the student workspace in practice mode.

Eval already has:
- Practice *execution* (POST /sessions/{id}/practice in go-backend — lets students run code in completed sessions)
- Frontend practice mode (student page detects sessionEnded, switches to practiceExecute API)

What's missing is the *entry point* — the API + UI to find/create a practice session from a problem page.

## Pitfall from coding-tool: joinSession for completed sessions

In coding-tool, the initial practice mode implementation had a bug: the student page skipped joinSession() for completed sessions (early-return set local state only), so students were never added to participants. The practice endpoint requires participants, causing silent 403 failures. This was fixed in a follow-up commit (aeef49c).

**When porting, avoid this by ensuring the student page calls joinSession() for completed sessions from the start.** The current eval code at `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/page.tsx` lines 122-129 has the same early-return pattern. Remove it and let completed sessions fall through to joinSession(), then set sessionEnded in the .then() callback.

## Upstream reference

- coding-tool commit: a6f1210 (feat: practice mode for problems #60)
- coding-tool commit: aeef49c (fix: add students to participants when joining completed sessions)
- Absorbs former PLAT-0t5g (closed, combined here)
- New files in coding-tool:
  - `src/app/(public)/problems/[id]/StudentActions.tsx` — Practice button component
  - `src/app/api/problems/[id]/practice/route.ts` — API to find/create practice session

## Architecture mapping (coding-tool -> eval)

The coding-tool Next.js API route needs to become a Go backend handler:

| coding-tool | eval |
|---|---|
| `POST /api/problems/[id]/practice` (Next.js route) | New Go handler: `POST /api/v1/problems/{id}/practice` |
| `createStorage(accessToken)` | `store.ReposFromContext(r.Context())` (RLS-scoped) |
| `createStorage(SERVICE_ROLE_MARKER)` | SECURITY DEFINER functions or admin store |
| `SessionService.createSessionWithProblem + endSession` | Store methods to create session + mark completed |
| `SessionService.addStudent` | `repos.JoinSession()` |

## Files to create/modify

### Go backend
- **New:** `go-backend/internal/handler/practice_session.go` — Handler for `POST /problems/{id}/practice`
- **New:** `go-backend/internal/handler/practice_session_test.go` — Handler tests
- **Modify:** `go-backend/internal/server/server.go` — Mount the new route
- **Possibly modify:** `go-backend/internal/store/sessions.go` — Add query to find completed sessions by problem ID in a section

### Frontend
- **Modify:** `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/page.tsx` — Fix joinSession for completed sessions (remove early-return at lines 122-129, set sessionEnded in .then() callback)
- **Modify:** `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/__tests__/page-session-ended.test.tsx` — Update tests for join flow change
- **New:** `frontend/src/app/(public)/problems/[id]/StudentActions.tsx` — Practice button component
- **New:** `frontend/src/app/(public)/problems/[id]/__tests__/StudentActions.test.tsx`
- **Modify:** `frontend/src/app/(public)/problems/[id]/page.tsx` — Render StudentActions
- **Modify:** `frontend/src/lib/api/problems.ts` — Add `startPractice(problemId, sectionId)` function

## Implementation steps

1. **Fix student page join for completed sessions** — remove early-return, call joinSession(), set sessionEnded in callback
2. **Backend: Add store method** to find a completed session by problem_id within a section
3. **Backend: Add handler** — validates problem exists, validates student is section member, finds or creates completed session, joins student, returns `{ session_id }`
4. **Backend: Mount route** at `POST /problems/{id}/practice` with auth + rate limiting
5. **Frontend: Add API client** function for the new endpoint
6. **Frontend: Port StudentActions component** — fetches student's sections, shows Practice button, auto-starts for single section or shows picker, redirects to /student?session_id=<id>
7. **Frontend: Update public problem page** to render StudentActions in a Suspense boundary
8. **Tests** for handler, store method, component, and updated student page behavior

title: E2E debugger test failing on main: 500 error on trace endpoint (debugger.spec.ts:141)
description:
## Summary

The E2E test \"Student can debug code from session editor\" (debugger.spec.ts:94) fails intermittently in CI. The executor returns 500 \"internal execution error\" on the second POST /api/v1/trace request in the test suite. The first trace always succeeds (~260ms); the second fails instantly (~5ms), indicating the sandbox runner errors before execution starts.

## Observed behavior

- First trace: 200 OK, ~260ms (sandbox runs successfully)
- Second trace: 500, ~5ms (sandbox.Run returns error immediately)
- Console log: `Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 500 (Internal Server Error)`
- UI stuck on \"Loading Trace...\" — never transitions to \"Active Debugging\"

## Root cause (suspected)

The executor sandbox (nsjail) leaves state that causes the second invocation to fail. The 5ms response time rules out actual execution — the error occurs before or at sandbox startup. Possible causes:
- nsjail cleanup issue (leftover cgroups, mount points, or temp dirs from first execution)
- Resource exhaustion in the CI container (cgroup limits, PID limits)
- Race condition in sandbox.Run when two trace requests run close together

## Reproduction

- Fails consistently in CI (GitHub Actions) using the pre-built executor:latest Docker image
- Passes locally when executor is built from source via `docker compose up --build`
- Same failure exists on main branch (not introduced by rate-limiting PR)

## Files to investigate

- `executor/internal/sandbox/sandbox.go` — nsjail invocation and cleanup
- `executor/internal/handler/trace.go:136` — where `h.runner()` returns the error
- `.github/workflows/e2e.yaml` — how the executor image is pulled/started in CI

## Relevant CI log lines

```
time=...T03:40:07.177Z level=INFO  msg="http request" method=POST path=/api/v1/trace status=200 duration_ms=262.08
time=...T03:40:12.817Z level=ERROR msg="internal server error" error="executor: unexpected status 500: {\"error\":\"internal execution error\"}" path=/api/v1/trace duration_ms=4.91
```
close_reason: Fixed in PR #59: added tmpfs mount to docker-compose and CI to match K8s emptyDir config

title: Port: E2E testing skill from coding-tool and adapt for eval
description:
## Summary

Port the E2E testing skill document from coding-tool (.claude/skills/e2e-testing/SKILL.md) and adapt it for eval's different infrastructure. This skill teaches AI agents how to write and debug Playwright E2E tests effectively.

## Upstream reference

- coding-tool commits: 6a76fc6, f1da90c, bb52887
- Source file: coding-tool/.claude/skills/e2e-testing/SKILL.md

## What to port

The skill covers:
1. **Core principles**: failing tests indicate real bugs, debug locally, fix the app not the test
2. **Debugging approach**: read error messages, check page structure output, check screenshots/video, trace back bugs
3. **Writing tests**: setup via DB helpers, separate browser contexts, explicit waits, cleanup
4. **Running tests**: commands to run single/all tests
5. **Key files**: fixtures, helpers, test data utilities

## Adaptations needed for eval

The coding-tool skill references Supabase/Next.js patterns. Eval has different infrastructure:

| Aspect | coding-tool | eval |
|---|---|---|
| Test runner | `npx playwright test` | `make test-e2e` (orchestrates full stack) |
| Prerequisites | `npx supabase start && source .env.local` | Docker Compose (postgres, redis, centrifugo, executor) + Go API + Next.js build |
| Test fixtures | `e2e/helpers/test-data.ts`, `e2e/fixtures/auth-helpers.ts` | `frontend/e2e/fixtures/test-fixture.ts`, `frontend/e2e/fixtures/auth.ts`, `frontend/e2e/fixtures/api-setup.ts` |
| DB setup | Direct Supabase DB helpers | API-based setup via `api-setup.ts` (createClass, createSection, etc.) |
| Auth | Supabase auth | Test auth mode (`AUTH_MODE=test`) with `signInAs()` |
| Error artifacts | `test-results/<test-name>/error-context.md` | Same Playwright pattern but under `frontend/test-results/` |
| Test namespace | Not mentioned | Per-test namespace isolation (`testNamespace` fixture) |
| Running single test | `npx playwright test e2e/your-test.spec.ts` | `cd frontend && npx playwright test e2e/your-test.spec.ts` (requires backend running) |
| Headed/debug mode | `--headed`, `--debug` flags | Same flags, but need `scripts/run-e2e-tests.sh` for infrastructure |

## Files to create

- `.claude/skills/e2e-testing/SKILL.md` — Adapted skill document

## Implementation steps

1. Copy the structure and principles from coding-tool's SKILL.md (the core debugging philosophy is universal)
2. Update all file paths to eval's frontend/e2e/ structure
3. Replace Supabase-specific setup instructions with eval's Docker Compose + Go backend setup
4. Document eval's test namespace isolation pattern
5. Update the "Running Tests" section with eval's make targets and manual commands
6. Update "Key Files" to reference eval's actual fixture files
7. Add eval-specific notes: API-based test data setup, Centrifugo for realtime, multi-service orchestration

title: Student activity badges on instructor dashboard
Thread last_code_update and replace badges with Active/Inactive/Not Started
description:
Replace the instructor dashboard's static "In progress" / "Finished" / "Not started" student badges with real-time activity indicators: Active (green, edited within 30s), Inactive (yellow, no edits for 30s+), and Not Started (gray, no code yet).

Frontend-only — no backend changes. The useRealtimeSession hook already tracks last_update: Date per student on every student_code_updated WebSocket event. We thread that timestamp to the UI and add a 10s timer for badge transitions.

The "Finished" badge (from AI analysis) is intentionally dropped. The AI analysis groups/navigation still work; we just remove the per-student Finished badge from the student list.

## Files to modify
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/types.ts — Add last_code_update?: Date to Student
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/session/[id]/page.tsx — Include last_code_update: s.last_update in student mapping (line 85-97)
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/StudentList.tsx — Replace badge logic, add 10s timer for re-render, remove finished_student_ids prop
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/SessionStudentPane.tsx — Remove finished_student_ids from StudentList call (line 294)
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/__tests__/StudentList.test.tsx — Update tests for new badge behavior
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/__tests__/SessionStudentPane.test.tsx — Update tests that reference finished_student_ids

## Files to read for context
- frontend/src/types/session.ts — Hook's Student type with last_update: Date
- frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts — How last_update is set (lines 122, 276)
- frontend/src/components/ui/Badge.tsx — Available variants: default, success, warning, error, info

## Implementation steps

1. In types.ts, add last_code_update?: Date to the Student interface
2. In page.tsx, update the students useMemo (line 85-97) to include last_code_update: s.last_update
3. In StudentList.tsx:
   - Remove finished_student_ids prop from interface and destructuring
   - Add a now state with useState(Date.now()) and a useEffect with setInterval(() => setNow(Date.now()), 10_000) for 10s re-render ticks
   - Replace the badge IIFE (lines 73-82) with:
     - No code (!has_code): <Badge variant="default">Not started</Badge>
     - Active (has_code && last_code_update && (now - last_code_update.getTime() < 30_000)): <Badge variant="success">Active</Badge>
     - Inactive (else): <Badge variant="warning">Inactive</Badge>
4. In SessionStudentPane.tsx, remove finished_student_ids prop from <StudentList> call (line 294)
5. Update StudentList.test.tsx:
   - Update student fixtures to include last_code_update
   - Replace "In progress" / "Finished" badge tests with "Active" / "Inactive" tests
   - Add test for badge transition (student with recent vs stale last_code_update)
   - Remove finished_student_ids test
6. Update SessionStudentPane.test.tsx: Remove any assertions around finished_student_ids being passed to StudentList

## Known behavior
On page refresh, all students with code show "Inactive" until their next code update WebSocket event (~300ms if typing). This is acceptable for the frontend-only approach.

## Testing notes
- Unit tests in StudentList.test.tsx covering all 3 badge states
- Test Active → Inactive transition using fake timers
- Verify the 10s interval is set up and cleaned up properly
- Verify existing tests for Featured badge, View/History/Feature buttons still pass
- Update SessionStudentPane.test.tsx for removed finished_student_ids prop

## Example — Badge logic before → after

Before:
```tsx
const isFinished = finished_student_ids?.has(student.id);
if (isFinished) return <Badge variant="success">Finished</Badge>;
if (student.has_code) return <Badge variant="info">In progress</Badge>;
return <Badge variant="default">Not started</Badge>;
```

After:
```tsx
if (!student.has_code) return <Badge variant="default">Not started</Badge>;
const isActive = student.last_code_update && (now - student.last_code_update.getTime() < 30_000);
if (isActive) return <Badge variant="success">Active</Badge>;
return <Badge variant="warning">Inactive</Badge>;
```

title: Remove finished classification from AI analysis prompts
description:
Remove the "classify each student as finished/in-progress" instruction from the AI analysis prompts. Since the "Finished" badge is being dropped from the UI (PLAT-9gig.1), we should stop asking the AI to waste tokens on this classification.

The backend response types (AnalyzeResponse.FinishedStudentIDs, CompletionEstimate) can stay — they'll just return empty/zero values. No schema changes needed.

## Files to modify
- go-backend/internal/ai/prompt.go — Remove the finished classification paragraph from DefaultCustomDirections (line 12)
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/SessionStudentPane.tsx — Remove the finished classification paragraph from DEFAULT_PROMPT (the "Also classify each student..." paragraph)

## Implementation steps

1. In prompt.go, remove the paragraph: "Also classify each student as either finished (code appears complete and correct) or in-progress (still working, has bugs, or incomplete). Include finished student IDs in finished_student_ids."
2. In SessionStudentPane.tsx, remove the matching paragraph from DEFAULT_PROMPT. The comment says it must match the backend's DefaultCustomDirections, so keep them in sync.

title: Type Session.problem as Problem | null instead of unknown
description:
## Summary

`Session.problem` in `frontend/src/types/api.ts:95` is typed as `unknown`, forcing every consumer to cast through `any` or use defensive `?.` chains on untyped data. This is the root cause of multiple `as any` casts and unnecessary optional chaining throughout the codebase. The backend always sends either a Problem object or null — the type should reflect that.

## Files to modify

1. `frontend/src/types/api.ts` — line 95: change `problem: unknown` to `problem: Problem | null` on the `Session` interface (import Problem from the same file where it's already defined)
2. After changing the type, remove now-unnecessary casts in consumers:
   - `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/page.tsx:157` — `session.problem as Problem` cast becomes unnecessary
   - `frontend/src/lib/api/sessions.ts:128` — `raw.session.problem as Record<string, unknown> | null` can use the typed field directly
   - `frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/session/[id]/page.tsx:122-123` — `realtimeSession.problem` access simplifies
   - `frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts:329` — `prev.problem` cast to `Session['problem']` becomes unnecessary

## Implementation steps

1. In `types/api.ts`, change `Session.problem` from `unknown` to `import('./problem').Problem | null` (matching the existing `Problem` import pattern used on line 80 for test_cases)
2. Run `make typecheck-frontend` to find all places that now have type errors from the improved typing
3. Remove unnecessary casts and fix any legitimate type mismatches
4. Run `make test-frontend` to verify no regressions

title: Port Playwright E2E tests from old repo
description:
## Summary
Port Playwright E2E tests from old repo to this project. Tests cover system-admin, critical user paths (instructor+student), and public view flows.

## Current Status: BLOCKED on PLAT-817

Work paused to fix systematic frontend response-shape mismatches (PLAT-817: typed API client layer). Once that PR merges and this branch is rebased, resume debugging.

## Branch & Worktree
- Branch: `feature/e2e-tests` (PR #38)
- Worktree: `/workspaces/eval-e2e-tests`
- Worktree has LOCAL MODIFICATIONS to 2 contract test files (stale edits, can discard with `git checkout -- .`)

## What's Done
- E2E test infrastructure: Playwright config, Docker Compose, test fixtures, API helpers
- 3 test specs ported: system-admin.spec.ts, critical-paths.spec.ts, public-view.spec.ts
- Console log capture on test failure (test-fixture.ts)
- Manual hook response-shape fixes in E2E worktree (will be SUPERSEDED by PLAT-817)
- Namespace detail page fix (will be SUPERSEDED by PLAT-817.2)

## What Remains After PLAT-817 Merges

### 1. Rebase and discard superseded changes
The E2E worktree has manual hook fixes that PLAT-817 replaces properly. After rebasing:
- Discard hook changes (useClasses, useNamespaces, useSections, useInvitations, useSessionHistory)
- Discard namespace detail page fix (covered by PLAT-817.2)
- Keep: test infrastructure, spec files, console log capture

### 2. Re-verify system-admin test
The system-admin test navigated successfully to the namespace detail page but failed finding "Test Organization" text. Root cause was the response-shape bug in `system/namespaces/[id]/page.tsx` which PLAT-817.2 fixes. Should pass after rebase.

### 3. Debug instructor blank-page bug (MAIN REMAINING WORK)
3 tests fail with blank white page after instructor login:
- critical-paths test 1: "Complete workflow: Instructor setup and student participation"
- critical-paths test 2: "Student code sync: code changes sync to instructor and public view"
- public-view: "Public view updates when instructor features different students"

All fail at the same point: after instructor signs in via test auth, the page shows blank white (or redirects back to login). The locator `h2:has-text("Dashboard"), button:has-text("Create Your First Class")` never appears.

This is NOT a timeout issue — the page is genuinely blank. Likely causes to investigate:
- Auth context not propagating to the instructor's page (cookie/localStorage not set?)
- The instructor user may not have the right role/namespace setup for the dashboard to render
- A component-level apiFetch call (not covered by PLAT-817 hooks) may be crashing silently
- Check browser console logs (now captured by test-fixture.ts) for errors

### 4. Screenshots from last run
- system-admin: `test-results/system-admin-System-Admin--52384-pace-and-view-invitation-UI-chromium/`
- critical-paths: `test-results/critical-paths-Critical-Us-0470a--instructor-and-public-view-chromium/`
- public-view: `test-results/public-view-Public-View-Fe-c3000-features-different-students-chromium/`

## Dependencies
- PLAT-817 (typed API client) — fixes response-shape bugs that affect E2E tests

title: Playwright infrastructure + frontend test auth mode
description:
## Summary
Set up Playwright test infrastructure and a frontend test auth mode that pairs with the backend's existing AUTH_MODE=test. This is the foundation for all E2E tests.

## Backend (already exists)
- `AUTH_MODE=test` accepts tokens in format `test:<external_id>:<email>` (go-backend/internal/auth/testvalidator.go)
- JWT middleware still runs — requests without auth headers get 401
- Contract tests already use this pattern (frontend/src/__tests__/contract/helpers.ts)

## Frontend changes needed

### New: `frontend/src/lib/test-auth.ts`
- `setTestUser(externalId, email)` / `getTestToken()` / `clearTestUser()`
- Module-level state, used by AuthContext and api-client

### Modify: `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`
- When `NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_MODE=test`: export a TestAuthProvider instead of AuthProvider
- TestAuthProvider.signIn(email, password) derives externalId from email, stores via test-auth module, calls GET /auth/me with test token
- TestAuthProvider.signOut() clears test user
- No Firebase SDK calls in test mode

### Modify: `frontend/src/lib/api-client.ts`
- getAuthHeaders(): when NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_MODE=test, return `Bearer test:<id>:<email>` from test-auth module
- Otherwise: existing Firebase path unchanged

## Playwright setup

### New: `frontend/playwright.config.ts`
- Based on old repo's config (/workspaces/coding-tool/playwright.config.ts)
- webServer array: Go backend (AUTH_MODE=test) + Next.js (NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_MODE=test)
- Chromium only, 30s timeout, HTML reporter

### New: `frontend/e2e/fixtures/auth.ts`
- signInAs(page, role, externalId?, namespace?) — navigates to /auth/signin, fills form
- loginAsInstructor(), loginAsStudent(), loginAsSystemAdmin() convenience wrappers

### New: `frontend/e2e/fixtures/api-setup.ts`
- Direct HTTP helpers reusing contract test patterns
- testToken(), createNamespace(), createClass(), createSection(), startSession()
- cleanupNamespace() for teardown

### New: `frontend/e2e/fixtures/test-fixture.ts`
- Extended Playwright test with per-test namespace isolation + cleanup

### New: `scripts/run-e2e-tests.sh`
- Starts postgres (reuse ensure-test-postgres.sh), Go server, Next.js
- Runs playwright test, cleanup on exit

### Modify: `Makefile`
- Add test-e2e target

## Patterns to follow
- frontend/src/__tests__/contract/globalSetup.ts — API data setup via test tokens
- frontend/src/__tests__/contract/helpers.ts — testToken(), contractFetch()
- scripts/run-contract-tests.sh — launch script pattern

title: CI failure on PR #72: TestIntegration_Stdin expects old output without input echo
description:
## Summary

PR #72 (feat: echo input() values inline in stdout) introduces an input echo preamble that causes input() values to be printed to stdout. The existing integration test TestIntegration_Stdin was not updated to account for this new behavior.

## Failure

```
integration_test.go:104: expected 'hi Alice', got "Alice\nhi Alice\n"
```

The test sends stdin 'Alice' to code like `name = input(); print(f'hi {name}')`. With input echo, 'Alice' now appears in stdout before 'hi Alice'.

## Files to modify

- executor/internal/handler/integration_test.go (~line 104): Update TestIntegration_Stdin expected output to include the echoed input value

## Implementation

Update the expected stdout assertion in TestIntegration_Stdin to expect 'Alice\nhi Alice\n' instead of just 'hi Alice'.
close_reason: Fixed: updated TestIntegration_Stdin expected output to include echoed input

title: Drain HTTP response body on success in realtime client for connection reuse
Migration 014: validate backfill on production data before applying
description:
## Summary
Migration 014 backfills revisions.student_work_id from session_students, then adds a NOT NULL constraint. This will fail if any revisions lack a matching session_students record.

## Details
- File: migrations/014_cleanup_session_students_revisions.up.sql
- Backfill query (lines 41-47): UPDATE revisions SET student_work_id = ss.student_work_id FROM session_students WHERE r.session_id = ss.session_id AND r.user_id = ss.user_id
- NOT NULL constraint (line 56): ALTER TABLE revisions ALTER COLUMN student_work_id SET NOT NULL

## Risk scenarios
1. Orphaned revisions: revision has session_id but no matching session_students row — backfill misses it, NOT NULL fails
2. Practice revisions: revisions with no session_id that don't have student_work_id set — NOT NULL fails

## Validation steps before applying
1. Count orphaned revisions: SELECT count(*) FROM revisions r WHERE r.student_work_id IS NULL AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM session_students ss WHERE ss.session_id = r.session_id AND ss.user_id = r.user_id AND ss.student_work_id IS NOT NULL)
2. If count > 0, either: (a) create student_work records for orphans and backfill, or (b) delete orphaned revisions if they're not important
3. Run migration in a transaction on a staging copy first
close_reason: Migration 014 already applied to production (migrations 015 and 016 exist on top of it). The migration itself handles orphans safely by deleting revisions with NULL student_work_id before applying the NOT NULL constraint (line 55). No further validation needed.

title: ReposFromContext: return error instead of panicking
description:
store.ReposFromContext panics when repos are missing from context. While Chi's Recoverer middleware catches this and returns 500, panics produce misleading stack traces and are more expensive than clean error returns.

Change signature to return (Repos, error) or (Repos, bool) and have handlers return a structured 500. This also enables cleaner handler-level testing of the missing-repos path.

Files: go-backend/internal/store/store.go:75-80
close_reason: Won't fix: panic is appropriate for misconfiguration — fails loud and early during development, unreachable in production with correct wiring

title: Go API Health & Observability
description:
## Summary
Implement health checks, structured logging, and metrics for the Go API to enable monitoring and debugging.

## Scope
- Health check endpoints (liveness, readiness)
- Structured JSON logging with request context
- Prometheus metrics endpoint
- Request tracing with correlation IDs
- Error tracking integration (optional: Sentry)

## Health Endpoints
\`\`\`
GET /healthz        # Liveness - always 200 if process is running
GET /readyz         # Readiness - checks DB, Redis, dependencies
GET /readyz?verbose # Detailed component status
\`\`\`

Readiness checks:
- Database connection pool health
- Redis connectivity
- Cognito JWKS reachable (cached)

## Structured Logging
\`\`\`go
// Using slog (Go 1.21+ stdlib)
slog.Info("request completed",
    "method", r.Method,
    "path", r.URL.Path,
    "status", status,
    "duration_ms", duration.Milliseconds(),
    "request_id", middleware.GetReqID(r.Context()),
    "user_id", user.ID,
)
\`\`\`

## Metrics
Prometheus metrics at `/metrics`:
- `http_requests_total{method, path, status}` - request counter
- `http_request_duration_seconds{method, path}` - latency histogram
- `db_pool_connections{state}` - connection pool gauge
- `db_query_duration_seconds` - query latency histogram

## Request Tracing
- Generate request ID if not present (X-Request-ID header)
- Propagate through all logs and downstream calls
- Include in error responses for debugging

## Deliverables
- \`internal/handler/health.go\` - health endpoints
- \`internal/middleware/logging.go\` - request logging
- \`internal/middleware/metrics.go\` - Prometheus instrumentation
- \`internal/middleware/requestid.go\` - correlation ID handling
- Grafana dashboard JSON (optional)

## Dependencies
- Requires: Go API Project Scaffolding (PLAT-4zm)

## References
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for observability requirements
- Prometheus Go client: github.com/prometheus/client_golang
close_reason: Merged: feat: Go API Health & Observability (#12)

title: Rebase conflict: PR #25 feature/frontend-migration vs main in Makefile
description: PR #25 (feature/frontend-migration) cannot be rebased onto main due to a conflict in Makefile. Main restructured the Makefile (commit 4653914 'chore: restructure Makefile for multi-project build targets') with per-project aggregate targets (build-api, test-api, lint-api, build-executor, etc.), while the feature branch simplified it and added frontend targets (test-frontend, lint-frontend, typecheck-frontend). The conflict is at commit 6c59f05 'ci: add frontend test, lint, and typecheck to CI pipeline'. Resolution: merge both sets of targets — keep the multi-project structure from main and add the frontend targets from the feature branch.

title: Add status query param validation on sessions List endpoint
Registration page sends unauthenticated requests to JWT-protected endpoints
description:
## Summary
The student registration page and accept-invite page both use `publicFetchRaw` (no Authorization header) to call Go backend endpoints that require JWT authentication. These endpoints will return 401 for real users.

## Root cause
All `/api/v1` routes are behind `jwtValidator.Validate` middleware (go-backend/internal/server/server.go:125). The registration routes (server.go:128-138) use `RegistrationStoreMiddleware` for RLS but still require a valid JWT for identity.

## Affected files
- `frontend/src/app/(public)/register/student/page.tsx` (lines 243, 188)
  - `publicFetchRaw('/auth/register-student', { method: 'POST', ... })` — no auth header
  - `publicFetchRaw('/auth/register-student?code=...')` — GET also behind JWT middleware
- `frontend/src/app/(public)/invite/accept/page.tsx` (lines 136, 211)
  - `publicFetchRaw('/auth/accept-invite?...')` — no auth header
  - `publicFetchRaw('/auth/accept-invite', { method: 'POST', ... })` — no auth header

## Expected production flow
1. Student/invitee creates a Firebase Auth account client-side (createUserWithEmailAndPassword)
2. Firebase returns a JWT (Identity Platform token)
3. Frontend sends the JWT with the registration/accept-invite API call
4. Go backend validates JWT, extracts external_id and email from claims
5. Backend creates user_profiles record

## Fix approach
The registration and accept-invite pages need to:
1. Create the Firebase Auth account first (Step 1 of the flow)
2. Wait for the auth state to update (onAuthStateChanged fires)
3. Use `apiFetch` (authenticated) instead of `publicFetchRaw` for the POST
4. The GET endpoints for code validation / invite lookup may need to be moved outside the JWT group, OR the user creates the Firebase account before validation

## Notes
- The Go handler correctly extracts identity from JWT claims, not the request body (security)
- The backend side is correct; only the frontend calls need updating
- This blocks real user registration and invite acceptance

title: Bootstrap secrets.tfvars from 1Password in devcontainer setup
description:

Terraform plan/apply requires centrifugo_api_key and centrifugo_token_secret which are currently passed via TF_VAR_ env vars. Store these in 1Password and have the devcontainer setup script generate infrastructure/terraform/environments/prod/secrets.tfvars automatically.

title: EKS Compute Platform
description:
## Summary
Provision EKS cluster with Fargate, ECR container registry, and ALB ingress controller. This is the compute foundation for all application workloads.

## Scope
Terraform modules for:
- EKS cluster with Fargate profiles (no EC2 nodes to manage)
- ECR repositories for application images (api, executor, centrifugo)
- ECR lifecycle policies (keep last 10 images, expire untagged after 7 days)
- ALB Ingress Controller for external traffic routing
- IAM roles and OIDC provider for service accounts
- Kubernetes namespaces (app, monitoring, keda)

## Fargate Profiles
- `default` - api workloads
- `executor` - code execution pods (isolated)
- `system` - CoreDNS, ingress controller

## Deliverables
- Terraform module: `infrastructure/terraform/modules/eks/`
- Terraform module: `infrastructure/terraform/modules/ecr/`
- kubectl/helm configs for ALB ingress controller
- Outputs: Cluster endpoint, OIDC provider ARN, ECR repo URLs

## Dependencies
- Requires: VPC & Networking Foundation (PLAT-03e)

## References
- See `/docs/MIGRATION.md` Phase 1: Infrastructure Foundation
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for EKS topology

title: Hook up analyze button with Gemini integration
description: Wire up Gemini API for instructor code analysis. Backend stub needs real LLM client, response types need redesign to match frontend WalkthroughScript shape, handler needs to analyze all students (not just one), and frontend needs model/prompt controls.

title: Backend: Config + Gemini client + redesigned AI types
description:
## Summary
Add Gemini API integration to the backend. Redesign AI request/response types to match the frontend's WalkthroughScript shape, implement GeminiClient using google.golang.org/genai SDK, and create the prompt template with boilerplate + editable directions.

## Files to modify
- `go-backend/internal/config/config.go` — add `GeminiAPIKey string` env var
- `go-backend/internal/ai/client.go` — redesign AnalyzeRequest/AnalyzeResponse types
- `go-backend/internal/ai/gemini.go` *(new)* — GeminiClient implementation
- `go-backend/internal/ai/prompt.go` *(new)* — prompt template
- `go-backend/internal/ai/client_test.go` — update StubClient test
- `.env.example` — add GEMINI_API_KEY=

## Files to read for context
- `frontend/src/types/analysis.ts` — WalkthroughScript types the response must match
- `go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go:373` — SessionStudent struct (input data shape)
- `go-backend/internal/ai/client.go` — current types to replace

## Implementation steps
1. Add `GeminiAPIKey string` env var to config.go Config struct
2. Add `GEMINI_API_KEY=` to .env.example
3. Redesign types in ai/client.go:
   - StudentSubmission{UserID, Name, Code}
   - AnalyzeRequest{ProblemDescription, Submissions, Model, CustomPrompt} — NOTE: no SessionID, that's a handler concern
   - AnalysisIssue{Title, Explanation, Count, StudentIDs, RepresentativeStudentID, RepresentativeStudentLabel, Severity} — NOTE: Count field included to match frontend
   - AnalyzeResponse{Issues, FinishedStudentIDs, OverallNote, Summary}
   - AnalysisSummary{TotalSubmissions, FilteredOut, AnalyzedSubmissions, CompletionEstimate, Warning} — NOTE: FilteredOut and Warning included to match frontend WalkthroughSummary
   - CompletionEstimate{Finished, InProgress, NotStarted}
4. Update StubClient to return new response shape
5. Create ai/prompt.go with:
   - DefaultCustomDirections constant (2-3 buckets, no unfinished code bucket, 5 max)
   - BuildPrompt(problemDescription string, submissions []StudentSubmission, customDirections string) string
   - Boilerplate: system framing + data formatting
   - Custom directions section (instructor-editable, with sensible default)
6. Create ai/gemini.go with GeminiClient:
   - NewGeminiClient(apiKey string) constructor
   - AnalyzeCode() builds prompt, calls Gemini with structured output (ResponseMIMEType: "application/json" + ResponseSchema)
   - Supports model selection via req.Model (default: gemini-2.0-flash)
   - go get google.golang.org/genai
7. Write unit tests for prompt building and StubClient

## Testing notes
- Unit test for BuildPrompt: verify boilerplate structure, custom directions insertion, student code formatting
- Unit test for StubClient: returns new response shape
- Unit test for GeminiClient: mock HTTP to verify request format and response parsing

title: Backend: Redesign handler + server wiring
description:
## Summary
Redesign the analyze handler to fetch all student submissions (instead of taking one student's code) and return a WalkthroughScript-shaped response. Wire up the GeminiClient in server.go (falling back to StubClient if no API key).

## Files to modify
- `go-backend/internal/handler/session_analyze.go` — new request shape, fetch all students, return script-wrapped response
- `go-backend/internal/handler/session_analyze_test.go` — update all tests for new shapes
- `go-backend/internal/server/server.go` — wire GeminiClient or StubClient based on config

## Files to read for context
- `go-backend/internal/ai/client.go` — new AI types (from task .1)
- `go-backend/internal/store/session_students.go` — ListSessionStudents method
- `go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go:373` — SessionStudent struct
- `go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go:392` — SessionStudentRepository interface
- `go-backend/internal/config/config.go` — GeminiAPIKey field (from task .1)
- `go-backend/internal/handler/testutil_test.go` — test fixtures (session.Problem JSON at line 214)
- `frontend/src/types/analysis.ts` — WalkthroughScript types the response must match

## Implementation steps
1. Change analyzeHTTPRequest to remove student_id/code, add model and custom_prompt:
   ```go
   type analyzeHTTPRequest struct {
       Model        string `json:"model" validate:"omitempty,oneof=gemini-2.0-flash gemini-2.5-flash"`
       CustomPrompt string `json:"custom_prompt"`
   }
   ```
2. Create HTTP response wrapper struct that adds handler-level fields:
   ```go
   type analyzeHTTPResponse struct {
       Script analyzeScriptResponse `json:"script"`
   }
   type analyzeScriptResponse struct {
       SessionID          string             `json:"session_id"`
       Issues             []ai.AnalysisIssue `json:"issues"`
       Summary            ai.AnalysisSummary `json:"summary"`
       OverallNote        string             `json:"overall_note,omitempty"`
       FinishedStudentIDs []string           `json:"finished_student_ids"`
       GeneratedAt        time.Time          `json:"generated_at"`
   }
   ```
3. Add analyzeTestRepos.ListSessionStudents mock (embed mock function like other test repos)
4. Handler Analyze() changes:
   - Remove student_id/code validation
   - Remove the isCreatorOrParticipant check for student_id since it's no longer in request
   - KEEP the isCreatorOrParticipant check for the auth user (instructor must be session creator/participant)
   - After session lookup, call repos.ListSessionStudents(ctx, sessionID)
   - Extract ProblemDescription from session.Problem JSON (parse the "description" field from the Problem JSONB column — see testutil_test.go:214 for the shape)
   - Build ai.AnalyzeRequest with all submissions (map SessionStudent → StudentSubmission)
   - Pass model and custom_prompt from request
   - Call aiClient.AnalyzeCode()
   - Wrap response in analyzeHTTPResponse with session_id=sessionID and generated_at=time.Now()
5. In server.go, replace `&ai.StubClient{}` with config-based selection:
   ```go
   var aiClient ai.Client
   if cfg.GeminiAPIKey != "" {
       aiClient = ai.NewGeminiClient(cfg.GeminiAPIKey)
   } else {
       aiClient = &ai.StubClient{}
   }
   ```
6. Update all existing tests for new request/response shapes
7. Add test: empty session (0 students) returns valid empty response

## Testing notes
- Update mockAIClient to use new AnalyzeRequest/AnalyzeResponse types
- Update analyzeTestRepos to implement ListSessionStudents via mock function
- All existing error-path tests (401, 403, 404, 500) must still pass with updated request shape
- Add test verifying ProblemDescription is correctly extracted from session.Problem JSON

title: Frontend: Model selector, prompt editor, updated API call
description:
## Summary
Update the frontend to match the simplified backend API (no student_id/code in request), add a model dropdown (Gemini 2.0 Flash / 2.5 Flash), and add an editable prompt textarea so the instructor can iterate on analysis directions.

## Files to modify
- `frontend/src/lib/api/sessions.ts` — update analyzeSession() params (remove student_id/code, add model/custom_prompt)
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/hooks/useAnalysisGroups.ts` — update analyze() signature
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/SessionStudentPane.tsx` — add analysis options UI, update handleAnalyze
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/hooks/__tests__/useAnalysisGroups.test.tsx` — update tests

## Files to read for context
- `frontend/src/types/analysis.ts` — WalkthroughScript types (no changes needed, already correct)
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/constants/analysis.ts` — severity styles (no changes needed)
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/GroupNavigationHeader.tsx` — downstream component (no changes needed)
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/StudentAnalysisDetails.tsx` — downstream component (no changes needed)

## Implementation steps
1. Update analyzeSession() in sessions.ts:
   - Remove studentId, code, problemDescription params
   - Add optional model and customPrompt params
   - POST body: { model, custom_prompt: customPrompt }
2. Update useAnalysisGroups hook:
   - Change analyze() signature to (session_id: string, model?: string, customPrompt?: string)
   - Remove student_id/code/problemDescription params
3. Update SessionStudentPane.tsx:
   - Add state: selectedModel (default "gemini-2.0-flash"), customPrompt (default from constant), showOptions (default false)
   - Add collapsible "Options" toggle link below the analyze button (collapsed by default)
   - When expanded, show:
     - Model dropdown: "Gemini 2.0 Flash" / "Gemini 2.5 Flash"
     - Textarea for custom prompt directions (pre-filled with default directions)
   - Update handleAnalyze():
     - Remove guard on selectedStudentId (analyze all students)
     - Stop reading selectedStudentCode and sessionProblem?.description (backend now fetches these server-side)
     - Pass model and customPrompt to analyze()
   - Keep analyze button label: "Analyze {count} Submissions"
4. Export default prompt directions as a constant (in constants/analysis.ts or inline):
   ```
   Focus on actual bugs, logical errors, and common misconceptions.
   Group similar issues into 2-3 categories (5 maximum).
   Do NOT create a category for unfinished or incomplete code.
   Only create categories that affect multiple students.
   ```
5. Update existing tests for useAnalysisGroups hook

## Testing notes
- Update useAnalysisGroups tests for new analyze() signature
- Test that model and custom_prompt are passed through to API call
- Test collapsible options panel toggle behavior
- Verify analyze button works without a selected student

title: Fix execute.ts: restore random_seed and attached_files parameters
description:

title: Fix duplicate realtime event emission on idempotent session updates
description:
## Summary
The session update handler (sessions.go:172-180) publishes realtime events based on request payload fields rather than comparing against the previous state. This means idempotent re-sends of the same PATCH request will emit duplicate session_ended or featured_student_changed events, potentially causing UI flicker on connected clients.

## Files to modify
- go-backend/internal/handler/sessions.go (lines ~172-180)

## Implementation
Compare the request values against the session's current state before publishing events:
1. For session_ended: only publish if session.Status != "completed" AND req.Status == "completed"
2. For featured_student_changed: only publish if the featured student actually changed (compare against current session.FeaturedStudentID)

title: Mobile layout responsiveness fixes

Should use the shared Table compound component from components/ui/Table.tsx which already handles overflow-x-auto wrapping and consistent styling. See admin/components/UserList.tsx and components/InvitationList.tsx for examples.

title: Smoke test race condition: test runs before new pod is ready

This happened during the public-rls-scope deploy — the page was working but the smoke test hit the old pod and got a 502. The smoke test should either wait for the new deployment to be fully ready or retry with backoff.

title: Accept-invite page uses Supabase token format instead of invitation UUID
description:
## Summary
The accept-invite page reads Supabase-style hash tokens from the URL (`#token_hash=...&type=invite`) but the Go backend expects a simple `?token=<uuid>` query parameter for invitation lookup.

## Affected files
- `frontend/src/app/(public)/invite/accept/page.tsx` (lines 109-126)
  - Reads `locationHash`, parses `token_hash` and `access_token` from URL fragment
  - Requires `type=invite` or `type=magiclink`
  - Sends to Go backend as query params: `?token_hash=...&access_token=...&type=...`
- `go-backend/internal/handler/auth.go` — GetAcceptInvite handler
  - Expects `?token=<uuid>` query parameter (invitation ID)

## Current behavior
1. Invitation email contains link with Supabase-style hash: `/invite/accept#token_hash=<token>&type=invite`
2. Frontend parses hash, sends `GET /auth/accept-invite?token_hash=<token>&type=invite`
3. Go backend looks for `?token=` param, doesn't find it → returns error

## Fix approach
Update the accept-invite page to support the Go backend's URL format:
- Support `?token=<invitation_uuid>` query parameter
- The invitation handler in the Go backend uses the invitation UUID directly
- Update invitation email template (Resend) to generate links in the new format: `/invite/accept?token=<invitation_uuid>`
- Can keep backward compatibility with hash format if needed during transition

## Related
- go-backend/internal/handler/invitations.go — generates acceptURL with invitation ID
- go-backend/internal/email/resend.go — sends invitation emails with acceptURL

title: Add error detail logging to HTTP handlers - 500s only log status code, not underlying error
description:
## Summary
The Go API request logging middleware only logs HTTP method, path, status code, and duration. When handlers return 500, the actual error (e.g., DB errors, RLS violations) is not logged, making production debugging impossible.

## Current behavior
```
level=INFO msg="http request" method=POST path=/api/v1/auth/bootstrap status=500 duration_ms=3.04
```

## Expected behavior
500-level responses should log the error message/cause, e.g.:
```
level=ERROR msg="http request" method=POST path=/api/v1/auth/bootstrap status=500 duration_ms=3.04 error="pq: new row violates row-level security policy for table users"
```

## Files to modify
- go-backend/internal/handler/auth.go - pass errors to a logging-aware error writer
- go-backend/internal/middleware/ - enhance request logging to capture error context
- go-backend/internal/httputil/ - consider adding error context to WriteError

## Approach
Either thread errors through the response writer wrapper so the logging middleware can capture them, or log at the handler level before calling WriteError.
close_reason: WriteError captures error detail on response writer for 5xx; logging middleware reads it back at ERROR level. Tests added.

title: Split section detail page into StudentSectionView and InstructorSectionView components

While the two views share some data fetching, the rendering logic is largely independent and the file is growing large. Split into StudentSectionView and InstructorSectionView components. title: Add contract tests for section-problems endpoints
description:
## Summary
Contract tests needed for 5 section-problems API functions to unblock CI (contract-coverage check fails at 0/5).

## Functions to cover
File: frontend/src/lib/api/section-problems.ts

1. listSectionProblems(sectionId) — GET /sections/{id}/problems → PublishedProblemWithStatus[]
2. publishProblem(sectionId, problemId, showSolution?) — POST /sections/{id}/problems (instructor-only)
3. unpublishProblem(sectionId, problemId) — DELETE /sections/{id}/problems/{problemId} (instructor-only)
4. updateSectionProblem(sectionId, problemId, {show_solution}) — PATCH /sections/{id}/problems/{problemId} (instructor-only)
5. listProblemSections(problemId) — GET /problems/{problemId}/sections → SectionProblem[]

## Test file to create
frontend/src/__tests__/contract/section-problems.integration.test.ts

## Pattern to follow
See frontend/src/__tests__/contract/sections.integration.test.ts for the established pattern:
- configureTestAuth() / resetAuthProvider() from helpers.ts
- getSetupState() for test fixtures (namespaceId, classId, sectionId, instructorToken, etc.)
- expectSnakeCaseKeys() for shape validation
- Add validators for PublishedProblemWithStatus and SectionProblem to validators.ts

## Setup requirements
- Use instructor token from global setup state for publish/unpublish/update operations
- The global setup already creates a namespace, instructor, class, section, and problem
- Publish a problem to the section as part of test setup, then test list/update/unpublish

## Validators to add
In frontend/src/__tests__/contract/validators.ts:

Replace Repos god interface with narrower per-handler interfaces
description:
The store.Repos interface embeds all 13 repository interfaces (~60 methods). Every handler gets access to everything even when it only needs 2-3 methods. This is a god interface anti-pattern that obscures actual dependencies.

Options: (a) typed context helpers like store.Users(ctx) returning UserRepository; (b) per-handler composite interfaces; (c) keep Repos but add handler-level dep documentation via comments.

Files: go-backend/internal/store/store.go:28-42

title: Add Firebase Auth Emulator for auth-flow E2E testing
description:
## Summary
Add Firebase Auth Emulator to E2E test infrastructure to enable auth-flow E2E testing. The SignInButtons component always calls Firebase signInWithPopup — no test mode branch — so registration and invitation acceptance flows cannot be E2E tested without a real or emulated Firebase Auth backend.

## Approach: Option C (Admin SDK end-to-end)
Use the Firebase Admin Go SDK for JWT validation on the backend (PLAT-zz7j, prerequisite). The Admin SDK auto-detects FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST and accepts emulator-issued tokens — no custom backdoor code path needed.

This means the E2E tests exercise the REAL auth flow end-to-end:
- Frontend: signInWithPopup → onAuthStateChanged → token in requests
- Backend: Admin SDK VerifyIDToken → accepts emulator tokens automatically
- No AUTH_MODE=test bypass, no alg:none backdoor, no custom env-var-gated code paths

## Prerequisites
- PLAT-zz7j: Migrate JWT validation to Firebase Admin Go SDK (MUST land first)

## Implementation
1. Add firebase emulators container to Docker Compose (auth only, ~3s startup)
2. Minimal firebase.json config (6 lines)
3. Frontend: add connectAuthEmulator() call gated by env var
4. E2E fixtures: create test users via emulator REST API, sign in via signInWithEmailAndPassword
5. Backend: no changes needed — Admin SDK handles emulator detection automatically
6. Port student-registration.spec.ts and invitation-flow.spec.ts

## Subtasks
- PLAT-d6j.1: Student registration UI E2E test
- PLAT-d6j.2: Invitation acceptance UI E2E test

title: E2E: Student registration UI (student-registration.spec.ts)
description:
## Summary
Add E2E test for the student registration UI flow. First touchpoint for every student — if it breaks, students are locked out. Requires Firebase Auth Emulator (parent epic).

## File to create
`frontend/e2e/student-registration.spec.ts`

## Test: "Student registers via join code on landing page"
1. Create class + section via API
2. Navigate to landing page (/)
3. Enter join code in formatted input (XXX-XXX)
4. Verify section preview shows (class name, semester)
5. Click sign-in button → Firebase emulator authenticates
6. Registration completes → redirect to section detail
7. Verify section appears in My Sections (/sections)

## Key files
- `frontend/src/app/(public)/register/student/page.tsx` — Registration page
- `frontend/src/app/(public)/page.tsx` — Landing page with join code input
- `frontend/src/components/ui/SignInButtons.tsx` — The component that calls signInWithPopup

## Blocked by
Parent epic PLAT-d6j (Firebase Auth Emulator setup)

title: E2E: Invitation acceptance UI (invitation-flow.spec.ts)
description:
## Summary
Add E2E test for the invitation acceptance flow. Only way instructors get platform access — if it breaks, onboarding is blocked. Requires Firebase Auth Emulator (parent epic).

## File to create
`frontend/e2e/invitation-flow.spec.ts`

## Test: "Admin creates invitation, instructor accepts and accesses dashboard"
1. System admin signs in, creates invitation for instructor email + namespace
2. Verify invitation appears as "pending"
3. New browser context: navigate to accept URL with token
4. Verify invitation details display (email, role, namespace)
5. Click sign-in button → Firebase emulator authenticates
6. Accept invitation → redirect to instructor dashboard
7. Verify instructor can access their namespace

## Key files
