## Blocked by
Parent epic PLAT-d6j (Firebase Auth Emulator setup)

title: Per-task worktrees for parallel implementers
description: Give each implementer its own isolated worktree instead of sharing one. Eliminates file clobbering and enables pre-commit hooks.

title: Create rebase skill
description:
Create `.claude/skills/rebase/SKILL.md` — a lightweight haiku-model rebase agent.

## Summary
Rebase agent that rebases a source branch onto a target branch, advances the target ref,
and optionally cleans up the worktree and source branch. Used by coordinator (task
integration) and merge-queue (PR rebases).

## Files to create
- `.claude/skills/rebase/SKILL.md`

## Implementation
Create the skill with:
- Frontmatter: name=rebase, model=haiku
- Input protocol: source branch, target branch, optional worktree path, optional cleanup flag
- Execution: rebase, advance target ref with `git branch -f`, optional cleanup
- Conflict resolution: resolve unambiguous conflicts, escalate ambiguous ones
- Output protocol: RESULT: PASS (with commit count) or RESULT: FAIL (with conflict details, abort rebase)

See plan file `.claude/plans/abstract-swimming-unicorn.md` Epic 1 "Create Rebase Skill" for full content.

title: Update coordinator for per-task worktrees
description:
Rework `.claude/skills/coordinator/SKILL.md` sections 1-3 to use per-task worktrees
and the rebase skill instead of a shared worktree.

## Summary
Each implementer gets its own worktree on its own task branch. After each implementer
completes, a sequential rebase agent integrates commits into the feature branch.

## Files to modify
- `.claude/skills/coordinator/SKILL.md`

## Changes

### Section "1. Setup"
Replace shared worktree creation with feature branch only:
```bash
git fetch origin main
git branch feature/<work-name> origin/main
```
No worktree here — worktrees are per-task in step 2.

### Section "2. Implement Tasks"
Remove "Optimistic conflict recovery" paragraph entirely. Remove symlink warning anti-pattern.

For each task:
- a. Claim (unchanged)
- b. Create per-task worktree:
  ```bash
  git branch feature/<work-name>/<task-id> feature/<work-name>
  git worktree add ../<project>-<task-id> feature/<work-name>/<task-id>
  ln -s /workspaces/eval/frontend/node_modules ../<project>-<task-id>/frontend/node_modules
  ```
- c. Spawn implementer with `WORKTREE: ../<project>-<task-id>`
- d. On SUCCESS: spawn rebase sub-agent (sequential) to integrate into feature branch
  On FAIL: spawn implementer in new worktree to resolve conflict

### Section "3. Pre-PR Review"
After all tasks merged, create worktree for feature branch for reviews and test-runner.

### Anti-Patterns
- Remove: "Blocking on file overlap analysis before spawning tasks"
- Remove: "Running npm ci in a worktree"
- Add: "Running rebase agents in parallel (must be sequential for linear history)"

See plan file `.claude/plans/abstract-swimming-unicorn.md` for full details.

title: Update merge-queue to use rebase skill
description:
Update `.claude/skills/merge-queue/SKILL.md` Step 6 to delegate rebases to the rebase skill
instead of doing rebase inline.

## Files to modify
- `.claude/skills/merge-queue/SKILL.md`

## Changes
Replace Step 6 inline rebase logic (approximately lines 108-128) with:
1. Locate or create worktree for PR branch
2. Spawn rebase sub-agent with source=PR branch, target=origin/main, cleanup=false
3. On PASS: force-push and poll CI to merge
4. On FAIL: file a beads issue describing the conflict

Keep existing post-rebase CI polling and merge behavior.

See plan file `.claude/plans/abstract-swimming-unicorn.md` for full replacement text.

title: Identity Platform Authentication Infrastructure
description:
## Summary
Configure GCP Identity Platform for authentication. This replaces AWS Cognito after the GCP migration.

**Status after GCP migration:** The Terraform module at `infrastructure/terraform/modules/identity-platform/` already exists with basic configuration. This epic now covers completing the setup.

## Scope
- ✅ Identity Platform Terraform module (done in migration)
- ✅ Email/password authentication enabled
- OAuth 2.0 Web Client configuration (manual step)
- SAML provider configuration for university SSO (manual step, separate epic)
- Email templates customization (manual step)
- Validation of end-to-end auth flow

## Architecture Decision: Identity Platform for Authentication Only
Same principle as before: Identity Platform handles authentication ("who is this person?") but NOT authorization or IdP-specific profile data.

**What Identity Platform stores:** UID, email, email_verified, display name
**What it does NOT store:** University-specific claims (affiliation, eppn, department)

IdP claims pass through at login. The Go API captures these and stores them in the application database with per-tenant configurable mappings.

## Manual Configuration Required
See `terraform output manual_setup_instructions` for:
1. OAuth 2.0 Web Client creation
2. SAML provider setup (if needed)
3. Email template customization

## Deliverables
- Complete Identity Platform configuration
- OAuth client configured and credentials in tfvars
- Validated auth flow (signup, login, token refresh)
- Documentation for local development auth flow

## References
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for auth flow overview
- See `infrastructure/terraform/modules/identity-platform/` for module docs
close_reason: Identity Platform infrastructure complete: Terraform module applied, SMTP configured with Resend, OAuth 2.0 client configured. Email templates deferred (PLAT-dar.6). E2E testing will happen with Go API.

title: Create Cognito Terraform module structure
description:
Set up the base Terraform module structure at infrastructure/terraform/modules/cognito/ with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, and versions.tf files.

## Follows Project Structure (PLAT-g8d)
This module follows the canonical Terraform project structure:
- Module lives in `infrastructure/terraform/modules/cognito/`
- Environment-specific values provided via `environments/{staging,prod}/terraform.tfvars`
- No hardcoded environment-specific values in module

## Common Variables
```hcl
variable "environment" {
  description = "Environment name (staging, prod)"
  type        = string
}

variable "project_name" {
  description = "Project name for resource naming"
  type        = string
}
```

## Done When
- [ ] Directory structure exists: `infrastructure/terraform/modules/cognito/`
- [ ] Files created: main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf
- [ ] Common variables defined (environment, project_name)
- [ ] `terraform validate` passes
close_reason: Module structure created during GCP migration. See infrastructure/terraform/modules/identity-platform/

title: Implement Cognito User Pool with password policies
description:
Create the aws_cognito_user_pool resource with: password policy (12+ chars, mixed case, numbers, symbols), email verification required, email-based usernames, optional MFA. Token validity: access 1hr, refresh 30 days.

## Email Configuration
Configure User Pool to use SES (depends on PLAT-7x6):
```hcl
email_configuration {
  email_sending_account = "DEVELOPER"  # Use SES, not Cognito default
  source_arn            = var.ses_identity_arn
  from_email_address    = var.email_from_address  # e.g., noreply@example.com
}
```

Required variables (no defaults):
- `ses_identity_arn` - ARN from SES module
- `email_from_address` - Verified sender address

## Custom Attributes
Do NOT define custom attributes for SAML claims (affiliation, eppn, etc). IdP-specific claims will pass through in the ID token at login and be stored in the application database per-tenant. This keeps Cognito schema-agnostic for multi-tenant flexibility.
close_reason: Identity Platform configuration implemented during GCP migration. Email/password auth enabled with password policies in module.

title: Configure OAuth 2.0 Web Client
description:
## Summary
Create and configure OAuth 2.0 Web Client for Identity Platform authentication.

## Manual Steps (GCP Console)
1. Go to: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials
2. Click "Create Credentials" > "OAuth client ID"
3. Application type: "Web application"
4. Name: "eval-{environment}-web-client"
5. Authorized JavaScript origins: Add app domains
6. Authorized redirect URIs: Add callback URLs
7. Save Client ID and Client Secret

## Update Terraform Variables
After creating OAuth client, update terraform.tfvars:
```hcl
oauth_client_id     = "your-client-id.apps.googleusercontent.com"
oauth_client_secret = "your-client-secret"
```

## Callback URLs by Environment
- Production: `https://app.example.com/auth/callback`
- Staging: `https://staging.example.com/auth/callback`
- Local dev: Uses cognito-local, not GCP

## Done When
- [ ] OAuth client created in GCP Console
- [ ] Client ID/Secret added to terraform.tfvars
- [ ] `terraform apply` succeeds with new values
close_reason: OAuth 2.0 Web Client configured in GCP Console. Client ID/secret added to secrets.tfvars and applied.

title: Implement User Pool Domain
description: Create aws_cognito_user_pool_domain for hosted UI. This is required for SAML flows (covered in separate epic).
close_reason: Not needed for GCP Identity Platform. Auth domains configured automatically via authorized_domains variable.

title: Implement IAM roles for Cognito
description:
## Summary
Create IAM roles and policies required for Cognito operations.

## Scope
Minimal IAM configuration - no Identity Pool needed (Go API mediates all AWS access).

## Required Roles

### Cognito SES Sending Role (if needed)
Cognito may need a role to send emails via SES. However, this is typically handled via SES resource-based policies rather than Cognito IAM roles.

Check if SES identity policy allows Cognito:
```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Principal": {
      "Service": "cognito-idp.amazonaws.com"
    },
    "Action": "ses:SendEmail",
    "Resource": "arn:aws:ses:REGION:ACCOUNT:identity/example.com"
  }]
}
```

If resource-based policy works, no IAM role needed here.

## NOT Needed
- **Identity Pool roles** - Frontend doesn't access AWS directly; Go API mediates all AWS access via its own EKS/Fargate role
- **Unauthenticated roles** - No anonymous AWS access required

## Deliverables
- Verify SES policy allows Cognito sending (may be handled in PLAT-7x6)
- Document any IAM roles if required
- If no roles needed, document that explicitly and close task
close_reason: Not needed for GCP Identity Platform. No separate IAM roles required - Identity Platform uses project-level permissions.

title: Configure Identity Platform email templates
description:
## Summary
Customize email templates for Identity Platform (verification, password reset) and Resend (instructor invitations). HTML templates use the brand indigo palette (#4f46e5 primary) with a clean card layout.

## Email Flows

| Template | Configured in | Audience | Trigger |
|----------|---------------|----------|---------|
| Email verification | GCP Console → Identity Platform → Providers → Email/Password → Email templates | All users (email/pw registration) | Account sign-up |
| Password reset | GCP Console → Identity Platform → Providers → Email/Password → Email templates | All users | Forgot password |
| Instructor invitation | Resend dashboard → Email templates | Instructors | Admin invites instructor to platform |

Note: Students join courses via join codes, not email invitations.

## Setup: Identity Platform (Verification & Password Reset)
1. Go to: GCP Console → Identity Platform → Providers → Email/Password
2. Open the Email templates section
3. Customize subject, sender name, and message body for each template type
4. Save and send test email

## Setup: Resend (Instructor Invitation)
1. Create template in Resend dashboard (https://resend.com/emails)
2. Paste HTML, configure variables for accept URL
3. Note the template ID
4. Update go-backend/internal/email/resend.go to use template ID instead of inline HTML (currently bare-bones HTML at line 47-52)

## Template Design
- Indigo gradient header (#4f46e5 → #6366f1)
- White card on light gray (#f8fafc) background
- Rounded corners, subtle shadow
- Single primary CTA button (brand-600 indigo)
- System font stack (-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, Roboto)
- 480px max width, mobile-responsive via viewport meta

## Done When
- [ ] Email verification template customized in Identity Platform
- [ ] Password reset template customized in Identity Platform
- [ ] Instructor invitation template created in Resend dashboard
- [ ] go-backend/internal/email/resend.go updated to reference Resend template ID
- [ ] Test emails sent successfully for all three flows
close_reason: Superseded by PLAT-4ru6 — email templates now handled via Identity Platform (verification/reset) and Firebase email link sign-in (invitations). Custom SMTP via Resend relay configured in Identity Platform Console.

title: Validate Identity Platform setup
description:
## Summary
Verify Identity Platform works end-to-end after configuration.

## Validation Checklist
1. [ ] Can create test user (GCP Console or Firebase Admin SDK)
2. [ ] Verification email sent successfully
3. [ ] Can complete email verification flow
4. [ ] Can sign in with email/password
5. [ ] JWT tokens issued correctly
6. [ ] Token refresh works
7. [ ] Password reset flow works

## CLI Validation (using gcloud/Firebase Admin)
```bash
# Check Identity Platform is enabled
gcloud services list --enabled | grep identitytoolkit

# Use Firebase Admin SDK or GCP Console for user operations
```

## JWT Validation
Verify tokens contain expected claims:
- `sub` (user ID)
- `email`
- `email_verified`
- `iat`, `exp` (timestamps)

## Done When
- [ ] All checklist items verified
- [ ] Test user cleaned up
- [ ] Any issues documented
close_reason: Infrastructure validated: Identity Platform provisioned, SMTP working (test email sent), OAuth client configured. Full E2E auth flow testing deferred until Go API exists.

title: Local Development Environment
description:
## Summary
Set up Docker Compose-based local development environment that mirrors staging/prod architecture without requiring AWS resources.

## Architecture Decision
- **Dependencies in compose:** Postgres, Redis, cognito-local, Centrifugo
- **API runs locally:** Go API runs in devcontainer with hot reload (air)
- **Devcontainer is the only supported environment** - no macOS/Windows/Linux variations
- **Makefile is the interface:** `make dev`, `make test`, `make reset-db`

## Services
| Production | Local Equivalent |
|------------|------------------|
| RDS PostgreSQL | postgres:15 container |
| ElastiCache Redis | redis:7 container |
| Cognito | cognito-local (jagregory/cognito-local) |
| Centrifugo | centrifugo container (same image as prod) |

## Docker Compose Structure
```yaml
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:15
    ports: ["5432:5432"]
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./migrations:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d
    healthcheck: ...

  redis:
    image: redis:7
    ports: ["6379:6379"]
    healthcheck: ...

  cognito:
    image: jagregory/cognito-local
    ports: ["9229:9229"]
    volumes:
      - ./.cognito:/app/.cognito  # pre-seeded test users
    healthcheck: ...

  centrifugo:
    image: centrifugo/centrifugo:v5
    ports: ["8000:8000"]
    healthcheck: ...
```

Note: Go API is NOT in compose. It runs locally via `air` for hot reload.

## Developer Workflow
```bash
# Start everything (deps + API with hot reload)
make dev

# Just start dependencies
make deps-up

# Reset database to clean state
make reset-db

# Run tests
make test

# Check service health
make status
```

## Auth Strategy
**Decision: cognito-local** (not mock middleware)
- No dev-only code paths that could leak to prod
- Same client code everywhere - just different endpoint URL
- Tests real auth flows locally (signup, login, token refresh)
- Pre-seeded test user: test@example.com / Test1234!$
- Limitation: No SAML support (test SAML in staging)

## Deliverables
- `docker-compose.yml` in repo root (deps only)
- `.cognito/db.json` with pre-seeded test user
- `.air.toml` for hot reload configuration
- `centrifugo.json` for local Centrifugo config
- `.env.example` with local development values
- `Makefile` with all common commands
- Seed data SQL for sample namespace/class/user
- Update `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` to install air

## Done When
- [ ] `make dev` starts all deps + API with hot reload in one command
- [ ] Changing a .go file triggers automatic rebuild (<2s)
- [ ] Pre-seeded test user can log in immediately
- [ ] Sample data exists (namespace, class) for testing
- [ ] All services have health checks
- [ ] `make reset-db` wipes and re-seeds cleanly
- [ ] Works reliably in devcontainer (the only supported environment)

## References
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` Environments section

title: Set up Docker Compose with Postgres and Redis
description:
## Summary
Create base docker-compose.yml with Postgres and Redis for local development.

## Context
- This is a deps-only compose file (no Go API)
- API runs locally in devcontainer with hot reload
- Devcontainer is the only supported environment

## docker-compose.yml
```yaml
version: '3.8'

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:15
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: app
      POSTGRES_USER: app
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: localdev
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./migrations:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U app -d app"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  redis:
    image: redis:7
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  postgres_data:
```

## Notes
- Migrations mount is read-only (`:ro`) - these run on first init only
- Health checks enable proper startup ordering
- Named volume for Postgres data persistence
- cognito-local and Centrifugo added in separate subtasks

## Done When
- [ ] `docker-compose up postgres redis` starts both services
- [ ] Services have working health checks
- [ ] Can connect: `psql postgresql://app:localdev@localhost:5432/app`
- [ ] Can connect: `redis-cli -h localhost ping`
- [ ] Data persists across restarts (named volume)

title: Set up local Centrifugo
description:
## Summary
Add Centrifugo to docker-compose for local WebSocket development.

## Context
- Same image as production (centrifugo/centrifugo:v5)
- Permissive config for local dev (no strict auth)
- Health check for startup ordering

## docker-compose.yml addition
```yaml
  centrifugo:
    image: centrifugo/centrifugo:v5
    command: centrifugo -c config.json
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    volumes:
      - ./centrifugo.json:/centrifugo/config.json:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "--spider", "http://localhost:8000/health"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
```

## centrifugo.json (local dev config)
```json
{
  "token_hmac_secret_key": "local-dev-secret-key-not-for-production",
  "api_key": "local-api-key",
  "admin": true,
  "admin_password": "admin",
  "admin_secret": "admin-secret",
  "health": true,
  "allowed_origins": ["http://localhost:3000", "http://localhost:8080"],
  "namespaces": [
    {
      "name": "session",
      "presence": true,
      "join_leave": true,
      "history_size": 10,
      "history_ttl": "5m"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Notes
- `allowed_origins` includes typical frontend dev ports
- `admin: true` enables admin UI at http://localhost:8000
- Namespace config matches production channel structure
- API key used by Go API to publish messages

## Done When
- [ ] `docker-compose up centrifugo` starts the service
- [ ] Health endpoint responds: `curl http://localhost:8000/health`
- [ ] Admin UI accessible at http://localhost:8000 (admin/admin)
- [ ] WebSocket connection works from browser

title: Configure Firebase Auth Emulator for local development
description:
## Summary
Configure Firebase Auth Emulator as the local Identity Platform replacement with pre-seeded test user.

## Context
- Firebase Auth Emulator is the official GCP local auth simulator
- Same auth flow as prod, just different endpoint
- No dev-only code paths that could leak to prod
- Pre-seeded test user for immediate testing

## docker-compose.yml addition
```yaml
  firebase-emulator:
    image: andreysenov/firebase-tools
    ports:
      - "9099:9099"   # Auth emulator
      - "4000:4000"   # Emulator UI
    command: >
      firebase emulators:start
      --only auth
      --project demo-local
    volumes:
      - ./firebase.json:/home/node/firebase.json
      - ./.firebase:/home/node/.firebase
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:9099"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
```

## Firebase configuration
Create `firebase.json`:
```json
{
  "emulators": {
    "auth": {
      "port": 9099
    },
    "ui": {
      "enabled": true,
      "port": 4000
    }
  }
}
```

## Pre-seeded test user
Create seed script that runs on startup:
```bash
# scripts/seed-auth.sh
curl -X POST http://localhost:9099/identitytoolkit.googleapis.com/v1/accounts:signUp \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "email": "test@example.com",
    "password": "Test1234!$",
    "returnSecureToken": true
  }'
```

## Go API Configuration
```go
type AuthConfig struct {
    // For local dev: "http://localhost:9099"
    // For GCP: leave empty (uses default endpoints)
    EmulatorHost string
    ProjectID    string
}
```

Set `FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST=localhost:9099` for local dev.

## .env.example
```env
# Local development
FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST=localhost:9099
GCP_PROJECT_ID=demo-local
```

## Done When
- [ ] Firebase Auth Emulator container starts
- [ ] Emulator UI accessible at http://localhost:4000
- [ ] Can create/sign in with test user
- [ ] JWT tokens are issued and can be validated by Go API
- [ ] Token refresh works

title: Create local dev documentation and scripts
description:
## Summary
Create Makefile and documentation for local development workflow.

## Context
- Devcontainer is the ONLY supported development environment
- Makefile is the primary interface for all dev tasks
- Documentation should be minimal but complete

## Makefile Targets
```makefile
.PHONY: dev deps-up deps-down wait-deps seed reset-db test status logs

# Start everything: deps + API with hot reload
dev: deps-up wait-deps
	cd go-backend && air

# Manage dependencies
deps-up:
	docker-compose up -d

deps-down:
	docker-compose down

wait-deps:
	@echo "Waiting for services..."
	@until pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -q; do sleep 0.5; done
	@until redis-cli -h localhost ping > /dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 0.5; done
	@until curl -sf http://localhost:9229 > /dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 0.5; done
	@echo "All services ready."

# Database management
seed:
	psql "postgresql://app:localdev@localhost:5432/app" -f scripts/seed.sql

reset-db:
	docker-compose down -v postgres
	docker-compose up -d postgres
	@until pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -q; do sleep 0.5; done
	$(MAKE) seed

# Testing
test:
	cd go-backend && go test ./...

# Diagnostics
status:
	@docker-compose ps

logs:
	docker-compose logs -f
```

## Documentation
Create `docs/LOCAL_DEV.md`:

```markdown
# Local Development

## Prerequisites
- Open this repo in the provided devcontainer (VS Code or GitHub Codespaces)
- That's it. The devcontainer has everything pre-installed.

## Quick Start
\`\`\`bash
make dev
\`\`\`
This starts all services and the API with hot reload. Changes to Go files auto-rebuild.

## Test Credentials
- **Email:** test@example.com
- **Password:** Test1234!$
- **Role:** instructor in "Test University" namespace

## Common Commands
| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `make dev` | Start everything with hot reload |
| `make test` | Run all tests |
| `make reset-db` | Wipe database and re-seed |
| `make logs` | Tail all service logs |
| `make status` | Show service health |

## Services
| Service | Local URL |
|---------|-----------|
| Go API | http://localhost:8080 |
| Postgres | localhost:5432 |
| Redis | localhost:6379 |
| cognito-local | http://localhost:9229 |
| Centrifugo | http://localhost:8000 |

## Troubleshooting
**Services won't start:** `make deps-down && make deps-up`
**Database issues:** `make reset-db`
**Stale containers:** `docker-compose down -v && make dev`
\`\`\`

## .env.example
```env
# Local development environment
# Copy to .env (already gitignored)

APP_ENV=local
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://app:localdev@localhost:5432/app
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
COGNITO_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:9229
COGNITO_USER_POOL_ID=local_pool
COGNITO_CLIENT_ID=local_client
CENTRIFUGO_API_URL=http://localhost:8000/api
CENTRIFUGO_API_KEY=local-api-key
```

## Done When
- [ ] Makefile with all targets works
- [ ] `docs/LOCAL_DEV.md` is complete and accurate
- [ ] `.env.example` has all required variables
- [ ] New developer can start with just `make dev`

title: Set up hot reload with air
description:
## Summary
Configure air for automatic Go API rebuilds on file changes.

## Context
- API runs locally (in devcontainer), deps in docker-compose
- Devcontainer already has Go 1.22 installed
- air watches for file changes and rebuilds/restarts automatically

## Implementation

**Install air in devcontainer:**
Add to `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` onCreateCommand:
```bash
go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest
```

**Create `.air.toml` in go-backend/:**
```toml
root = "."
tmp_dir = "tmp"

[build]
  cmd = "go build -o ./tmp/main ./cmd/server"
  bin = "./tmp/main"
  delay = 1000
  exclude_dir = ["tmp", "vendor", "node_modules"]
  exclude_regex = ["_test.go"]
  include_ext = ["go", "tpl", "tmpl", "html"]
  kill_delay = 500

[log]
  time = false

[misc]
  clean_on_exit = true
```

**Makefile integration:**
```makefile
dev: deps-up wait-deps
	cd go-backend && air

deps-up:
	docker-compose up -d

wait-deps:
	@until pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -q; do sleep 0.5; done
	@until redis-cli -h localhost ping > /dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 0.5; done
```

## Done When
- [ ] `make dev` starts deps + API with hot reload
- [ ] Changing a .go file triggers automatic rebuild
- [ ] API restarts within ~2 seconds of file save
- [ ] Works reliably in devcontainer

title: Create seed data for local development
description:
## Summary
Create seed data so developers have working test data immediately after setup.

## Context
- Devcontainer is the only supported environment
- Need test user in cognito-local with known credentials
- Need sample data in Postgres (namespace, class, users)
- Must be idempotent (safe to run multiple times)

## Seed Data Required

**cognito-local test user:**
Pre-configure `.cognito/db.json` (committed to repo):
```json
{
  "Users": [{
    "Username": "test@example.com",
    "Password": "Test1234!$",
    "Attributes": [
      {"Name": "email", "Value": "test@example.com"},
      {"Name": "email_verified", "Value": "true"}
    ],
    "Enabled": true,
    "UserStatus": "CONFIRMED"
  }]
}
```

**Postgres seed data** (`migrations/seed.sql` or `scripts/seed.sql`):
```sql
-- Idempotent: use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

-- Test namespace
INSERT INTO namespaces (id, name, slug)
VALUES ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'Test University', 'test-u')
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

-- Test user (matches cognito-local user)
INSERT INTO users (id, email, namespace_id, role)
VALUES ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002', 'test@example.com', 
        '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'instructor')
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

-- Sample class
INSERT INTO classes (id, namespace_id, name, created_by)
VALUES ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003', 
        '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001',
        'CS101 Introduction to Programming',
        '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002')
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;
```

**Makefile targets:**
```makefile
seed:
	psql $(DATABASE_URL) -f scripts/seed.sql

reset-db:
	docker-compose down -v
	docker-compose up -d postgres
	@until pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -q; do sleep 0.5; done
	# migrations run automatically via docker-entrypoint-initdb.d
	$(MAKE) seed
```

## Documentation
Add to README or LOCAL_DEV.md:
```
## Test Credentials
- Email: test@example.com
- Password: Test1234!$
- Role: instructor in "Test University" namespace
```

## Done When
- [ ] `.cognito/db.json` committed with test user
- [ ] Seed SQL creates namespace, user, sample class
- [ ] `make seed` runs idempotently
- [ ] `make reset-db` wipes and re-seeds everything
- [ ] Test credentials documented

title: Add health checks and startup ordering
description:
## Summary
Ensure services start in correct order and the API waits for dependencies to be healthy.

## Context
- Postgres and Redis must be healthy before API starts
- docker-compose `depends_on` alone doesn't wait for service readiness
- Makefile handles orchestration; compose just defines services

## Implementation

**docker-compose.yml health checks:**
```yaml
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:15
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: app
      POSTGRES_USER: app
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: localdev
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./migrations:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U app -d app"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  redis:
    image: redis:7
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  cognito:
    image: jagregory/cognito-local
    ports:
      - "9229:9229"
    volumes:
      - ./.cognito:/app/.cognito
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "--spider", "http://localhost:9229"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  centrifugo:
    image: centrifugo/centrifugo:v5
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    command: centrifugo -c config.json
    volumes:
      - ./centrifugo.json:/centrifugo/config.json
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "--spider", "http://localhost:8000/health"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
```

**Makefile wait logic:**
```makefile
wait-deps:
	@echo "Waiting for Postgres..."
	@until pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -q; do sleep 0.5; done
	@echo "Waiting for Redis..."
	@until redis-cli -h localhost ping > /dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 0.5; done
	@echo "Waiting for cognito-local..."
	@until curl -sf http://localhost:9229 > /dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 0.5; done
	@echo "All services ready."

dev: deps-up wait-deps
	cd go-backend && air

deps-up:
	docker-compose up -d
```

**Status check target:**
```makefile
status:
	@docker-compose ps
	@echo ""
	@echo "Service health:"
	@docker-compose ps --format json | jq -r '.[] | "\(.Name): \(.Health // "N/A")"'
```

## Done When
- [ ] All compose services have healthchecks
- [ ] `make dev` waits for all deps before starting API
- [ ] `make status` shows health of all services
- [ ] No race conditions on fresh `docker-compose up`

title: Fix invitation creation: wrong JSON field name + 422 not classified as non-retryable
close_reason: Fixed: target_role field name + 422 classified as non-retryable validation error

title: Fix inconsistent button styles on instructor problem cards
RLS not enforced in production — app user bypasses all row-level security
description:
## Summary

Row-level security policies are not enforced in production. The app database user owns all tables, and PostgreSQL table owners bypass RLS by default. The production RLS middleware (internal/middleware/rls.go) sets session variables (app.user_id, app.namespace_id, app.role) but never calls SET ROLE to drop to a non-owner role. Integration tests correctly use SET ROLE eval_app and pass, masking the fact that production has no RLS enforcement.

Impact: Any authenticated user can potentially read/write data across all namespaces. The entire multi-tenant isolation model is a no-op in production.

## Evidence

- All tables owned by app user (SELECT tableowner FROM pg_tables)
- relforcerowsecurity is false on all tables
- rls.go setRLSVariables() calls set_config() but never SET ROLE
- Integration tests do SET ROLE eval_app at integration_test.go:142 but production does not

## Fix

### Step 1: Migration 008 — create eval_app role

Create eval_app role with CONNECT, USAGE, SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on all tables, USAGE on sequences, EXECUTE on functions. Include ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES for future tables. Conditional creation (IF NOT EXISTS) for CI compatibility. No FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY — defense in depth deferred; the real protection is SET ROLE + integration tests.

Files: migrations/008_eval_app_role.up.sql, migrations/008_eval_app_role.down.sql

### Step 2: Middleware fix — SET ROLE / RESET ROLE

Add SET ROLE eval_app to rls.go before setting session variables in setRLSVariables(). Add RESET ROLE to releaseClean(). Both RLSContextMiddleware and RegistrationStoreMiddleware paths must do SET ROLE.

Files: go-backend/internal/middleware/rls.go, go-backend/internal/middleware/rls_test.go

### Step 3: API/DB integration test harness

New test category for full-stack API-to-DB integration tests. Tests go through the real middleware chain (including RLS middleware) with a real database. Lighter than E2E but validates middleware+DB behavior that store and contract tests miss.

- Location: go-backend/internal/integration/
- Pattern: httptest.NewServer with real server.New(), real pgxpool, real migrations, AUTH_MODE=test
- DB lifecycle: Same Docker postgres as store integration tests (ensure-test-postgres.sh)
- Make target: make test-integration-api
- Build tag: //go:build integration

### Step 4: RLS cross-namespace isolation test

First test using the new harness. Creates two namespaces with users, makes API requests as each user, verifies cross-namespace data is not visible. This test must fail before the Step 2 fix and pass after.

Files: go-backend/internal/integration/rls_isolation_test.go
close_reason: Merged PR #50. RLS enforced via SET ROLE eval_app, plus 3 latent policy bugs fixed (migrations 008-010).

The frontend expects map[user_id]name but gets {}. title: Add invite token support to email sign-in page
description:
## Summary
Add an optional \`?invite=<token>\` query param to \`/auth/signin/email\`. After successful Firebase sign-in, if the param is present, directly call \`acceptInvite(token)\` to create the user and redirect by role. This avoids the AuthContext "no DB user" dead end for testing invite flows with email/password accounts.

## Context
The email sign-in page is a hidden testing backdoor (not linked from the invite accept page). Currently, signing in with an email that has no DB record results in a dead end — AuthContext tries bootstrap, gets 403, sets user to null. The invite accept page can't auto-accept because firebaseAuth.currentUser is null by the time the user navigates there.

## Files to modify
- \`frontend/src/app/(public)/auth/signin/email/page.tsx\` — read \`invite\` search param; after successful sign-in, call \`acceptInvite(token)\` and redirect based on role instead of waiting for isAuthenticated
- \`frontend/src/app/(public)/auth/signin/email/__tests__/page.test.tsx\` — add tests for invite token flow

## Implementation steps
1. Read \`invite\` param from \`useSearchParams()\`
2. After successful \`signInWithEmailAndPassword\` (or test mode setTestUser), if invite param exists:
   - Call \`acceptInvite(token)\` from \`@/lib/api/registration\`
   - On success: redirect based on \`data.role\` (same pattern as invite accept page)
   - On error: show error message (expired, consumed, etc.)
3. If no invite param: existing behavior (wait for isAuthenticated redirect)
4. Add tests for: invite param present + success, invite param + expired token, invite param + consumed token

## Test flow
1. Send invitation to \`jdelfino+test@gmail.com\` as namespace-admin
2. Create email/password account in Identity Platform console
3. Go to \`/auth/signin/email?invite=<invitation-id>\`
4. Sign in → user created as instructor → redirected to dashboard

title: Eliminate raw fetch (apiFetchRaw/publicFetchRaw) from page components
description:
## Summary

Page components are calling apiFetchRaw/publicFetchRaw directly instead of using typed client functions in lib/api/. This caused a production bug where accept-invite referenced data.user.role but the API returns a flat user object — the contract tests didn't catch it because they validate the API client layer, not raw fetch calls.

## Policy

Nothing should use apiFetchRaw or publicFetchRaw outside of the lib/ client layer. Page components must go through typed client functions in lib/api/, which are validated by contract tests.

## Affected Pages

1. frontend/src/app/(public)/invite/accept/page.tsx — uses publicFetchRaw for GET /auth/accept-invite and apiFetchRaw for POST /auth/accept-invite
2. frontend/src/app/(public)/register/student/page.tsx — uses publicFetchRaw for GET /auth/register-student and apiFetchRaw for POST /auth/register-student
3. frontend/src/app/(public)/auth/bootstrap/page.tsx — uses publicFetchRaw for POST /auth/register
4. frontend/src/app/(public)/problems/[id]/page.tsx — uses publicFetchRaw for GET /public/problems/:id (server-side fetch with caching)

## Implementation Steps

1. Create typed client functions in lib/api/auth.ts (or new file) for:
   - getInvitationDetails(token: string): Promise<InvitationInfo>
   - acceptInvite(token: string, displayName?: string): Promise<User>
   - getStudentRegistrationInfo(code: string): Promise<{section, class}>
   - registerStudent(code: string, displayName?: string): Promise<User>
   - bootstrap(email: string, displayName?: string): Promise<User>

2. These typed functions should handle error code mapping and return typed errors (discriminated union or thrown typed errors), so pages get structured errors without parsing raw responses.

3. Add/update contract tests to call these typed functions against the real API.

4. Update all four pages to use the typed functions instead of raw fetch.

5. Consider making apiFetchRaw/publicFetchRaw unexported (or adding a lint rule) to prevent future drift.
close_reason: All raw fetch eliminated from page components (PR #48)

title: Execution settings persistence and propagation
description:
## Summary

Execution settings (stdin, random_seed, attached_files) are not properly persisted or propagated across several flows in the platform. Students can lose their input on page reload, and instructor-facing features like the public view don't receive the full execution context.

## Scope

This epic covers all execution_settings data flow gaps:
1. Student execution_settings not persisted to DB on code update (the column exists but is never written)
2. Featured student view doesn't transmit execution_settings to the public projector view
3. Any other execution_settings gaps discovered during audit

## Background

Execution settings are a JSON object with three fields:
- `stdin` — custom input the student types
- `random_seed` — deterministic randomness seed
- `attached_files` — file attachments for the code to read

These settings follow a 3-layer merge: Problem defaults → Student overrides → Per-execution overrides. The problem is that student-level overrides are tracked in frontend memory but never persisted, causing data loss and downstream propagation failures.
close_reason: Complete. Student execution_settings persistence (goal 1) and featured student propagation (goal 2) both implemented. Per-revision capture (goal 3) deferred as unnecessary.

title: Public view does not display featured student's execution settings (stdin)
description:
## Summary

When an instructor features a student's code on the public view, the student's execution settings (primarily their stdin input) are not transmitted. The public view only receives the student's code, so running the featured code uses the problem-level example input instead of whatever the student actually had in their input box.

This means if a student typed custom input to test their solution, that input is lost when their code is featured — the projector shows different behavior than what the student saw.

## Root Cause

The feature action only sends `student_id` and `code`. The student's `execution_settings` (which includes `stdin`, `random_seed`, `attached_files`) are available on the instructor's real-time session state but never included in the feature request or event.

**Full data flow showing the gap:**

1. **Instructor features student** — `useRealtimeSession.ts:479-493`: `featureStudent()` reads `students.get(studentId)?.code` but ignores `.execution_settings`
2. **API call** — `realtime.ts:74-76`: `apiFeatureStudent()` only accepts `(sessionId, studentId, code)`
3. **Backend handler** — `session_state.go:127-130`: `featureRequest` struct only has `StudentID` and `Code` fields
4. **Real-time event** — `events.go:48-51`: `FeaturedStudentChangedData` only carries `UserID` and `Code`
5. **Public view hook** — `useRealtimePublicView.ts:97-111`: only updates `featured_student_id` and `featured_code`
6. **Public view page** — `public-view/page.tsx:109-120`: passes `problem` to `CodeEditor`, which falls back to problem-level `execution_settings.stdin` (the example input)

## Files to Modify

### Backend
- `go-backend/internal/handler/session_state.go` (~line 127-130) — Add `ExecutionSettings` field to `featureRequest` struct; pass through to store and publisher
- `go-backend/internal/realtime/events.go` (~line 48-51) — Add `ExecutionSettings` to `FeaturedStudentChangedData`
- `go-backend/internal/realtime/session_publisher.go` — Update `FeaturedStudentChanged` signature to accept execution settings
- `go-backend/internal/store/sessions.go` — Add `FeaturedExecutionSettings` to `UpdateSessionParams` and persist to DB (or just pass through without persisting)
- `go-backend/internal/handler/session_state.go` (~line 90-125) — Include featured execution settings in `PublicState` response

### Frontend
- `frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts` (~line 479-493) — Include `execution_settings` in the feature call
- `frontend/src/lib/api/realtime.ts` (~line 74-76) — Add execution_settings parameter to `apiFeatureStudent()`
- `frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimePublicView.ts` (~line 97-111) — Read and store `execution_settings` from event payload
- `frontend/src/app/(projector)/public-view/page.tsx` (~line 109-120) — Pass featured execution settings to `CodeEditor` (e.g. via `exampleInput` prop or a new prop)
- `frontend/src/types/api.ts` — Add `featured_execution_settings` to `SessionPublicState` type

### Database (optional)
- New migration to add `featured_execution_settings JSONB` column to `sessions` table (for HTTP polling fallback). Alternatively, this could be stored only in the real-time event and re-fetched on poll.

## Implementation Steps

1. Add `featured_execution_settings` to the feature request, event payload, and public state response on the backend
2. Update the frontend feature action to include the student's execution_settings
3. Update the public view to read featured execution settings and pass stdin to the CodeEditor
4. Add tests for the new field at handler, publisher, and frontend levels

title: Student execution_settings not persisted to database on code update
description:
## Summary

Student execution_settings (stdin, random_seed, attached_files) are never persisted to the database despite the session_students table having an execution_settings JSONB column. They only live in the frontend's in-memory state. On page reload, all student execution settings are lost.

## Root Cause

The backend PUT /sessions/{id}/code handler ignores the execution_settings field sent by the frontend:

1. **Handler** (go-backend/internal/handler/session_students.go:73-75): `updateCodeRequest` struct only has a `Code` field — the `execution_settings` JSON key from the frontend request body is silently dropped
2. **Store** (go-backend/internal/store/session_students.go:65): `UpdateCode()` only writes `code` to the DB, never `execution_settings`
3. **Frontend** (frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts:419): sends `execution_settings` via `apiUpdateCode()`, but the backend ignores it

The column exists in the DB and is read back (the SELECT includes it), but it's always NULL because nothing ever writes to it.

## Files to Modify

### Backend
- `go-backend/internal/handler/session_students.go` (line 73-75): Add `ExecutionSettings json.RawMessage` to `updateCodeRequest` struct
- `go-backend/internal/store/session_students.go` (line 58): Update `UpdateCode()` signature to accept `executionSettings json.RawMessage`; update SQL to SET `execution_settings = $4`
- `go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go`: Update `SessionStudentRepository.UpdateCode` interface signature

### Tests
- Update handler and store test mocks/signatures to match new parameter

## Implementation Steps

1. Add `ExecutionSettings json.RawMessage` field to `updateCodeRequest` struct
2. Update store `UpdateCode` to accept and write `execution_settings`  
3. Pass `req.ExecutionSettings` through from handler to store
4. Update tests

title: Revisions don't capture execution_settings used during code execution
description:
## Summary

When code revisions are stored (for session history/replay), only the execution_result (output) is saved — not the execution_settings (input: stdin, random_seed, attached_files) that produced that result. This makes it impossible to fully replay a past execution.

## Current State

- `go-backend/internal/store/revisions.go:9-10`: `revisionColumns` includes `execution_result` but not `execution_settings`
- `go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go:402-412`: `CreateRevisionParams` has `ExecutionResult json.RawMessage` but no `ExecutionSettings`
- The `revisions` table schema (migrations/001_initial_schema.up.sql) has no `execution_settings` column

## Files to Modify

### Database
- New migration: Add `execution_settings JSONB` column to `revisions` table

### Backend
- `go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go`: Add `ExecutionSettings json.RawMessage` to `CreateRevisionParams` and `Revision` struct
- `go-backend/internal/store/revisions.go`: Add column to `revisionColumns`, scan helpers, and `CreateRevision` INSERT
- `go-backend/internal/handler/execute.go`: Pass merged execution settings to revision creation

### Frontend
- `frontend/src/types/api.ts`: Add `execution_settings` to `Revision` type
- Session history UI: Display execution settings alongside execution results (if applicable)

## Notes

This is P3 because revisions are primarily for code history, not full execution replay. The current behavior is functional — students can see their past code and outputs — but they can't see what input was used.

description:

title: Remove dead featureCode() function in frontend/src/lib/api/sessions.ts
description:
## Summary

The "Show Solution" feature for instructors is broken. featureCode() in frontend/src/lib/api/sessions.ts sends a POST to /sessions/{id}/feature with only `{ code }` (no student_id). The backend's Feature handler (session_state.go:145-150) interprets a missing student_id as a request to CLEAR the featured student. So clicking "Show Solution" actually clears the projector instead of displaying the solution.

## Root Cause

- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/components/SessionView.tsx:106 calls featureCode(session_id, sessionProblem.solution)
- frontend/src/lib/api/sessions.ts:191-193 sends `{ code }` without student_id
- Backend session_state.go:148-149: `if req.StudentID == nil { params.ClearFeatured = true }`

## Fix Options

1. **Backend: Support solution-only featuring** — Allow featuring code without a student_id (a "solution mode"). The Feature handler would set featured_code without a featured_student_id.
2. **Frontend: Use a sentinel** — Send a known sentinel student_id (e.g. the instructor's own ID) to indicate "this is the solution, not a student."

Option 1 is cleaner — it avoids the sentinel hack and models the actual intent.

## Files to Modify

- go-backend/internal/handler/session_state.go: Support code-only featuring (student_id nil but code present)
- frontend/src/lib/api/sessions.ts: Ensure featureCode() sends code correctly
- Tests for both paths

title: RDS PostgreSQL Database
description:
## Summary
Provision RDS PostgreSQL instance for the application database. This provides the persistent data layer.

## Scope
Terraform modules for:
- RDS PostgreSQL 15+ instance
- DB subnet group (database subnets only)
- Security group (allow access from EKS only)
- Parameter group with RLS-friendly settings
- Automated backups configuration
- Optional: Read replica for prod

## Configuration
- Instance class: db.t3.micro (staging), db.r6g.large (prod)
- Storage: 20GB gp3, autoscaling to 100GB
- Multi-AZ: No for staging, Yes for prod
- Backup retention: 7 days staging, 30 days prod
- Encryption: At rest (AWS-managed keys)

## Connection Management
- Store credentials in AWS Secrets Manager
- Connection string format for Go app
- Max connections sizing for connection pooling

## Deliverables
- Terraform module: `infrastructure/terraform/modules/rds/`
- Outputs: Endpoint, port, Secrets Manager ARN

## Dependencies
- Requires: VPC & Networking Foundation (PLAT-03e)

## References
- See `/docs/MIGRATION.md` Phase 1: Infrastructure Foundation
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for data layer design

title: Database migration: section_problems, student_work tables + session_students/revisions changes
description:
## Summary
Add new tables and add new columns to existing tables. Does NOT drop old columns yet — that happens in a final cleanup task after all Go code is updated.

## Files to create
- migrations/013_section_problems_and_student_work.up.sql
- migrations/013_section_problems_and_student_work.down.sql

## Implementation steps

1. Create section_problems table:
   - Columns: id (UUID PK), section_id (FK sections), problem_id (FK problems), published_by (FK users), show_solution (BOOLEAN DEFAULT false), published_at (TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now())
   - UNIQUE(section_id, problem_id)
   - Indexes on section_id, problem_id
   - RLS policies: section members SELECT; section instructors INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE

2. Create student_work table:
   - Columns: id (UUID PK), namespace_id (FK namespaces), user_id (FK users), problem_id (FK problems), section_id (FK sections), code (TEXT DEFAULT ''), execution_settings (JSONB), created_at, last_update
   - UNIQUE(user_id, problem_id, section_id)
   - Indexes on (user_id, problem_id, section_id), section_id, problem_id
   - RLS: owner SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE own rows; section instructors SELECT

3. Add columns to session_students (do NOT drop old columns yet):
   - ADD column: student_work_id (UUID FK student_work, nullable)

4. Add columns to revisions (do NOT make session_id nullable yet):
   - ADD column: student_work_id (UUID FK student_work, nullable)
   - student_work_id is nullable because existing revision rows won't have it

5. Clean up fake practice sessions:
   - DELETE FROM sessions WHERE status = 'completed' AND ended_at - created_at < interval '1 second'

## Important: What this migration does NOT do
- Does NOT drop code/execution_settings from session_students (old Go code still reads them)
- Does NOT rename last_update to joined_at
- Does NOT make revisions.session_id nullable
- Does NOT make revisions.student_work_id NOT NULL
These changes happen in a separate cleanup migration (Task 9) after all Go code is updated.

## Verification
Run make test-integration-store -- existing tests should still pass since no columns are removed.

title: Store layer: section_problems and student_work repositories
description:
## Summary
Implement Go store layer for the new section_problems and student_work tables. Update existing stores to support the new student_work_id columns while keeping old code/execution_settings columns functional (they are not dropped until the final cleanup migration).

## Files to create
- go-backend/internal/store/section_problems.go
- go-backend/internal/store/student_work.go

## Files to modify
- go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go -- add SectionProblem, PublishedProblemWithStatus, StudentWork types; add SectionProblemRepository and StudentWorkRepository interfaces; update Revision/CreateRevisionParams to include optional StudentWorkID
- go-backend/internal/store/store.go -- add new repositories to Repos composite interface
- go-backend/internal/store/revisions.go -- update CreateRevisionParams to accept optional StudentWorkID; write it when provided
- go-backend/internal/store/session_students.go -- add student_work_id to JoinSession params and scan; keep code/execution_settings columns functional (they still exist in DB)

## Important: Do NOT remove UpdateCode or drop code columns yet
session_students.code and execution_settings still exist in the DB and are still used by the existing handler code. Task 4 rewrites the handlers to use student_work instead, and Task 9 drops the old columns. This task just adds the new store methods alongside the existing ones.

## section_problems.go
Follow pattern from memberships.go (column constants, scan helpers, compile-time interface check):
1. Column constants for section_problems + joined query with problems
2. scanSectionProblem() and scanPublishedProblemWithStatus() helpers
3. ListSectionProblems(sectionID, userID) -- LEFT JOIN with student_work to include per-student work status. Returns []PublishedProblemWithStatus.
4. CreateSectionProblem(params) -- INSERT, handle duplicate via HandleDuplicate
5. UpdateSectionProblem(sectionID, problemID, params) -- UPDATE show_solution
6. DeleteSectionProblem(sectionID, problemID) -- DELETE
7. ListSectionsForProblem(problemID) -- SELECT section_problems WHERE problem_id

## student_work.go
1. Column constants, scan helpers
2. GetOrCreateStudentWork(namespace, userID, problemID, sectionID) -- INSERT...ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING then SELECT (upsert)
3. UpdateStudentWork(id, params) -- UPDATE code, execution_settings, last_update
4. GetStudentWork(id) -- SELECT by ID, include problem data via JOIN
5. GetStudentWorkByProblem(userID, problemID, sectionID) -- SELECT by unique key
6. ListStudentWorkBySession(sessionID) -- JOIN session_students ON student_work_id

## Types (in interfaces.go)
SectionProblem: ID, SectionID, ProblemID, PublishedBy, ShowSolution, PublishedAt
PublishedProblemWithStatus: SectionProblem + Problem + optional StudentWork
StudentWork: ID, NamespaceID, UserID, ProblemID, SectionID, Code, ExecutionSettings, CreatedAt, LastUpdate

## Verification
- Write store integration tests for all new methods
- Run make test-integration-store
- Run make test-api (existing handler tests still pass -- no methods removed)

title: Handler layer: section_problems and student_work endpoints
description:
## Summary
Implement HTTP handlers for publishing problems to sections and for student work CRUD + execution. Only adds new routes -- old practice routes are removed in Task 5.

## Files to create
- go-backend/internal/handler/section_problems.go
- go-backend/internal/handler/student_work.go

## Files to modify
- go-backend/internal/server/server.go -- mount new routes only (do NOT remove old practice routes yet -- that is Task 5)

## Endpoints

### section_problems.go (SectionProblemHandler)
Follow pattern from sections.go (stateless struct, handler methods):

1. GET /api/v1/sections/{id}/problems -- list published problems for section
   - Any section member (RLS enforces visibility)
   - Calls ListSectionProblems(sectionID, authUser.ID)
   - Returns []PublishedProblemWithStatus

2. POST /api/v1/sections/{id}/problems -- publish problem (instructor+)
   - Body: { problem_id, show_solution }
   - Returns 201; 409 on duplicate

3. PATCH /api/v1/sections/{id}/problems/{problemID} -- update settings (instructor+)
   - Body: { show_solution }

4. DELETE /api/v1/sections/{id}/problems/{problemID} -- unpublish (instructor+)
   - Returns 204

5. GET /api/v1/problems/{id}/sections -- list sections where published (instructor+)

### student_work.go (StudentWorkHandler)
Uses RLS-scoped repos (students can INSERT/UPDATE own rows):

1. POST /api/v1/sections/{id}/problems/{problemID}/work -- get or create student_work
   - Any section member
   - Validates problem is published to section via section_problems (RLS handles section membership -- if user is not a member, section_problems query returns empty, so no redundant membership checks needed)
   - Returns StudentWork with code

2. GET /api/v1/student-work/{id} -- get student_work with problem data
   - Owner only (RLS enforces)
   - Returns StudentWork + Problem (via JOIN or nested response)
   - Used by frontend workspace to load state on page open

3. PATCH /api/v1/student-work/{id} -- update code/execution_settings (owner only)
   - Body: { code, execution_settings }
   - Updates last_update

4. POST /api/v1/student-work/{id}/execute -- execute code (owner only)
   - Body: { code, execution_settings }
   - Loads problem from DB via student_work.problem_id
   - Merges execution settings (problem-level + student overrides)
   - Calls executor, returns result
   - Follow ExecuteHandler pattern in handler/execute.go

### Route mounting in server.go (additions only)
Under section {id} routes:
  GET /problems -> sectionProblemHandler.List
  POST /problems -> sectionProblemHandler.Publish (PermContentManage)
  PATCH /problems/{problemID} -> sectionProblemHandler.Update (PermContentManage)
  DELETE /problems/{problemID} -> sectionProblemHandler.Unpublish (PermContentManage)
  POST /problems/{problemID}/work -> studentWorkHandler.GetOrCreate

Top-level:
  GET /student-work/{id} -> studentWorkHandler.Get
  PATCH /student-work/{id} -> studentWorkHandler.Update
  POST /student-work/{id}/execute -> studentWorkHandler.Execute

Under problems/{id}:
  GET /sections -> sectionProblemHandler.ListSectionsForProblem (PermContentManage)

## Verification
- Write handler unit tests (mock repos pattern)
- Run make test-api
- Run make lint-api

title: Unify live session code flow: session_students → student_work
description:
## Summary
Update the live session code flow so student code is stored in student_work instead of session_students. After this task, session_students.code is no longer written to (but the column still exists -- dropped in Task 9).

## Files to modify

### go-backend/internal/handler/session_students.go
- Join(): After creating session_students entry, also get/create student_work for (user, problem, section). Extract problem_id from session.problem JSONB. Store student_work_id in session_students. Return student's existing code from student_work (not session_students).
- UpdateCode(): Rewrite: look up student_work via session_students.student_work_id. Update student_work.code instead of session_students.code. Still record revision and broadcast via Centrifugo. Stop writing to session_students.code (the column still exists but is no longer maintained).

### go-backend/internal/handler/session_state.go
- State() / buildStateResponse(): ListSessionStudents now returns code from student_work via JOIN. API response shape unchanged for frontends.

### go-backend/internal/revision/buffer.go
- Record() signature changes: accept studentWorkID (required) and sessionID (optional, set during live sessions).
- Buffer key changes from sessionID:userID to studentWorkID (since student_work_id is unique per user+problem+section, it is a sufficient key).
- CreateRevision(): pass both studentWorkID and sessionID to store.
- FlushSession(): on session end, create a final snapshot revision for each participant. Look up their student_work_id from session_students.

### go-backend/internal/store/session_students.go
- JoinSession(): now accepts and stores studentWorkID parameter.
- ListSessionStudents(): JOIN with student_work to include code and execution_settings in response. The old code/execution_settings columns in session_students are ignored in the query.
- Remove UpdateCode() method -- no longer needed. The handler now calls UpdateStudentWork() instead.
- Update the SessionStudentRepository interface accordingly.

### go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go
- SessionStudent struct: add StudentWorkID field. Keep Code/ExecutionSettings in the struct for now (populated from student_work JOIN, not from session_students columns).

## Implementation details

### Join flow:
1. Parse session ID, bind request (existing)
2. Get session to extract problem_id: var p struct{ ID uuid.UUID }; json.Unmarshal(session.Problem, &p)
3. Get or create student_work: repos.GetOrCreateStudentWork(namespace, userID, problemID, sectionID)
4. Create session_students with student_work_id
5. Return student_work.Code and student_work.ExecutionSettings to client

### UpdateCode flow:
1. Parse session ID + auth (existing)
2. Get session_student to find student_work_id
3. Update student_work: repos.UpdateStudentWork(studentWorkID, {Code, ExecutionSettings})
4. Record revision with studentWorkID + sessionID (both provided for live sessions)
5. Publish CodeUpdated event via Centrifugo (unchanged)

### Revision buffer key:
Old: sessionID:userID (breaks when there is no session)
New: studentWorkID (unique per user+problem+section, always available)

## Verification
- Update existing handler tests for new code paths
- Run make test-api
- Manual: start live session -> student joins -> types code -> instructor sees code -> session ends -> student code persists in student_work

title: Frontend: unified student workspace (student_work-centric)
description:
## Summary
Rewrite the student workspace page to be student_work-centric. Single page handles both live sessions and practice -- auto-detects active session.

## Files to create
- frontend/src/lib/api/student-work.ts -- API client for student work endpoints
- frontend/src/lib/api/section-problems.ts -- API client for section problems endpoints

## Files to modify
- frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/page.tsx -- full rewrite to student_work-centric
- frontend/src/lib/api/realtime.ts -- remove practiceExecute function
- frontend/src/types/api.ts -- add StudentWork, SectionProblem, PublishedProblemWithStatus types
- frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts -- update as needed (API response shape for students may be unchanged)

## API client: student-work.ts
- getOrCreateStudentWork(sectionId, problemId) -> POST /sections/{id}/problems/{problemId}/work
- getStudentWork(workId) -> GET /student-work/{id} (returns StudentWork + Problem)
- updateStudentWork(workId, { code, executionSettings }) -> PATCH /student-work/{id}
- executeStudentWork(workId, code, executionSettings) -> POST /student-work/{id}/execute

## API client: section-problems.ts
- listSectionProblems(sectionId) -> GET /sections/{id}/problems
- publishProblem(sectionId, problemId, showSolution) -> POST /sections/{id}/problems
- unpublishProblem(sectionId, problemId) -> DELETE /sections/{id}/problems/{problemId}
- updateSectionProblem(sectionId, problemId, { show_solution }) -> PATCH /sections/{id}/problems/{problemId}
- listProblemSections(problemId) -> GET /problems/{id}/sections

## Unified student page rewrite
URL: /student?work_id=X

### Data flow:
1. Read work_id from URL params
2. Fetch student_work + problem via GET /student-work/{id} (Task 3 adds this endpoint)
3. Check for active session: GET /sections/{sectionId}/sessions?status=active, check if any session's problem JSON id matches student_work.problem_id
4. If active session found -> connect WebSocket, enter live mode
5. If no active session -> practice mode

### Live mode (active session detected):
- Join session via POST /sessions/{id}/join (existing -- now links student_work on backend)
- Code updates via PUT /sessions/{id}/code (existing -- now writes to student_work on backend)
- Execution via realtime (existing)
- Show connection status, featured student notifications

### Practice mode (no active session):
- Auto-save code via PATCH /student-work/{id} (500ms debounce)
- Execute via POST /student-work/{id}/execute
- No WebSocket, no connection status
- Solution panel if show_solution is true

### Session end transition:
- WebSocket session-ended event received
- Banner: Session ended -- you can keep practicing
- Switch to practice mode seamlessly (no redirect, same URL)
- Code stays in editor, execution switches to student_work endpoint

### Entry points:
- From section page: click problem -> getOrCreateStudentWork -> navigate to /student?work_id=X
- From active session banner: same flow (getOrCreateStudentWork for the session's problem)

### Backward compatibility:
- The page should also accept ?session_id=X for direct session links (e.g. from instructor sharing a link). In this case: load session, extract problem_id and section_id, getOrCreateStudentWork, then proceed as normal. Replace URL to ?work_id=X via router.replace.

## Verification
- make test-frontend
- make typecheck-frontend
- make check-api-imports
- Manual: student opens problem -> practice mode -> instructor starts session -> student auto-connects -> session ends -> keeps practicing

title: Frontend: problem-centric student section detail page
description:
## Summary
Redesign the student section detail page to show a unified list of published problems instead of sessions.

## Files to modify
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/page.tsx`

## Current behavior
Page shows: Active Sessions (green cards) + Past Sessions (gray cards). Sessions are the primary navigation unit.

## New behavior (student view only — instructor view unchanged)

### Layout:
1. **Section header** (unchanged: name, class, semester)
2. **Active session banner** — if an active session exists, show prominent banner with 'Class is live\! Join now' button. Clicking creates/loads student_work for the session's problem and navigates to `/student?work_id=X`.
3. **Published problems list** — fetched from `GET /sections/{id}/problems`:
   - Each problem card shows: title, description (truncated), tags
   - Student's work status: 'Not started' / 'Last worked: 2h ago' (from student_work.last_update)
   - Badge: 'Live' if this problem matches the current active session's problem
   - 'Practice' button (not started) / 'Continue' button (has work) → calls `getOrCreateStudentWork(sectionId, problemId)` → navigates to `/student?work_id=X`
   - 'View Solution' expandable (if problem.solution exists AND section_problem.show_solution is true)
4. **No past sessions section** — removed for students

### Sort:
- Default: most recently worked on first (by student_work.last_update DESC, nulls last)
- Live session problems pinned to top

### Filter:
- Toggle: 'Show all' / 'Worked on' — filters to problems where student_work exists

### Instructor view:
- Keep existing session management (active + past sessions)
- Add a 'Published Problems' section showing what's published to this section

## Verification
- `make test-frontend`
- `make typecheck-frontend`
- Manual: student navigates to section → sees problem list → clicks Practice → redirected to workspace

title: Frontend: instructor publish UI
description:
