title: Final cleanup migration: drop old session_students columns, tighten revisions
description:
## Summary
After all Go code and frontend code is updated to use student_work, drop the old redundant columns and tighten constraints.

## Files to create
- migrations/014_cleanup_session_students_revisions.up.sql
- migrations/014_cleanup_session_students_revisions.down.sql

## Migration steps

1. Drop old columns from session_students:
   - DROP COLUMN code
   - DROP COLUMN execution_settings
   - RENAME COLUMN last_update TO joined_at

2. Tighten revisions:
   - ALTER COLUMN session_id DROP NOT NULL (now optional -- practice revisions have no session)
   - Backfill: UPDATE revisions SET student_work_id = ... for any existing revisions that lack it (join via session_students.student_work_id or via user_id + session problem_id). If backfill is not feasible, existing revisions can keep student_work_id NULL.
   - Optionally: ALTER COLUMN student_work_id SET NOT NULL (only if all rows are backfilled)

3. Clean up test mock types:
   - Remove FindCompletedSessionByProblem from stubRepos if not already done
   - Remove any remaining practice-related test utilities

## Verification
- Run make test-integration-store
- Run make test-api
- Run make test-frontend
- Run make lint
close_reason: Completed: migration 014 drops old columns, renames last_update→joined_at, makes revisions.session_id nullable, backfills student_work_id

title: Frontend-to-API integration test harness
description:
Set up infrastructure to run frontend tests against the real Go API server.

## Why
The frontend migration (PLAT-uum) changed every API call from Supabase to the Go backend. Current frontend "integration" tests mock fetch and use assumed response shapes — they missed the User PascalCase bug because mocks matched the wrong types. The only way to truly validate the migration is to test against the actual API.

## Scope
1. Test harness that starts Go API + Postgres (reuse existing docker-compose) and runs frontend tests against it
2. Contract tests for critical endpoints: /auth/me, /classes, /sections, /sessions, /admin/users, /realtime/token
3. Validate response shapes match frontend type definitions (snake_case fields, nullable vs required, etc.)
4. CI integration — run as a separate job, not blocking unit tests

## Existing infrastructure
- `make test-integration` already spins up Docker services for Go store integration tests
- `go-backend/internal/store/store_methods_integration_test.go` is the pattern
- Frontend jest config already has an `integration` project

## Relationship to PLAT-uum
This validates the PLAT-uum migration but is separate infrastructure work. PLAT-uum can land first; this epic catches any remaining contract mismatches post-merge.
close_reason: All 6 subtasks complete — contract test harness, tests, CI job all shipped

title: Add AUTH_MODE=test support to Go server
description:
## Summary
Add a test authentication mode to the Go server so integration tests can authenticate without real Firebase/Identity Platform tokens.

## Why
Contract tests need to hit the real API with authenticated requests. The production auth validates RS256 JWTs against Google's JWKS endpoint, which is impossible to use in tests. A test validator accepts simple tokens while keeping all downstream logic (user lookup, RLS context, handlers) running for real.

## Files to modify

### 1. `go-backend/internal/config/config.go`
Add field:
```go
AuthMode string `env:"AUTH_MODE" envDefault:""`
```

### 2. `go-backend/internal/auth/testvalidator.go` (new file)
Create a `TestValidator` implementing `TokenValidator`:
- Accepts tokens in format `test:<external_id>:<email>`
- Parses the parts and returns `*Claims{Subject: externalID, Email: email, EmailVerified: true}`
- Returns error for malformed tokens
- Include unit tests in `testvalidator_test.go`

### 3. `go-backend/internal/server/server.go`
In `NewWithRegistry`, where the auth middleware is wired (lines 108-114):
- If `cfg.AuthMode == "test"`, create `auth.NewTestValidator()` instead of `auth.NewIdentityPlatformValidator(...)`
- Log a warning when test mode is active: `logger.Warn("AUTH_MODE=test: using test token validator — DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION")`
- Everything else (user lookup adapter, RLS middleware) stays the same

## Implementation steps
1. Add `AuthMode` to config struct
2. Create `auth.NewTestValidator()` + unit tests
3. Wire into server.go with conditional
4. Manual smoke test: start server with AUTH_MODE=test, curl /api/v1/auth/me with header `Authorization: Bearer test:test-admin-001:admin@test.local`

title: Add contract test harness orchestration
description:
## Summary
Create a Makefile target and shell script that orchestrates contract tests with full parallel-safety: ensure Postgres via shared script, start a dedicated Go API on a random port, run Jest contract tests with a random namespace, clean up only your test data, kill only your server.

## Why
Contract tests must be safe to run concurrently by multiple agents/branches on the same machine. Postgres is shared infrastructure (multi-tenant via namespaces). Each test run is a single tenant.

## Design principles
- **Postgres**: Managed by shared `scripts/ensure-test-postgres.sh` (PLAT-fs7.5). Never start or stop it here.
- **Go API**: Start own instance on a random port. Kill only your own on exit via trap.
- **Data isolation**: Random namespace per run. Cleanup = `DELETE FROM namespaces WHERE id = '<ns>'` (cascades all child tables). Admin user is shared and never deleted.
- **No global teardown**: Don't stop postgres, don't kill other servers.

## Files to create/modify

### 1. `scripts/run-contract-tests.sh` (new file)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# --- Config ---
DB_HOST=${DATABASE_HOST:-localhost}
DB_PORT=${DATABASE_PORT:-5432}
DB_NAME=${DATABASE_NAME:-eval}
DB_USER=${DATABASE_USER:-eval}
DB_PASS=${DATABASE_PASSWORD:-eval_local_password}
PSQL_URL="postgresql://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASS}@${DB_HOST}:${DB_PORT}/${DB_NAME}"

# --- 1. Ensure Postgres is running + admin seeded ---
./scripts/ensure-test-postgres.sh

# --- 2. Start Go API on random port ---
API_PORT=$(python3 -c 'import socket; s=socket.socket(); s.bind(("",0)); print(s.getsockname()[1]); s.close()')

AUTH_MODE=test \
DATABASE_HOST="$DB_HOST" DATABASE_PORT="$DB_PORT" DATABASE_NAME="$DB_NAME" \
DATABASE_USER="$DB_USER" DATABASE_PASSWORD="$DB_PASS" \
CENTRIFUGO_TOKEN_SECRET=test-contract-secret \
GCP_PROJECT_ID=test-project \
PORT="$API_PORT" \
  go run ./go-backend/cmd/server &
SERVER_PID=$!

# --- 3. Generate random namespace ---
NS="contract-$(openssl rand -hex 4)"

cleanup() {
  # Clean up test data (namespace cascades)
  psql "$PSQL_URL" -c "DELETE FROM namespaces WHERE id = '${NS}';" 2>/dev/null || true
  # Kill only our server
  kill "$SERVER_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
  wait "$SERVER_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT

# Wait for healthy
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  if curl -sf "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/healthz" >/dev/null 2>&1; then break; fi
  sleep 1
done
curl -sf "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/healthz" >/dev/null || { echo "Server failed to start"; exit 1; }

# --- 4. Run contract tests ---
cd frontend
API_BASE_URL="http://localhost:${API_PORT}" CONTRACT_NS="$NS" \
  npx jest --selectProjects contract --no-coverage
```

### 2. `Makefile`
```makefile
test-integration-contract:
	./scripts/run-contract-tests.sh
```
Add to aggregate:
```makefile
test-integration: test-integration-executor test-integration-contract
```

## Implementation steps
1. Create `scripts/run-contract-tests.sh` (make executable)
2. Add Makefile target
3. Test: verify parallel runs don't interfere (start two in background, both pass)

title: Write contract tests for core API endpoints
description:
## Summary
Write TypeScript contract tests that hit the real Go API and validate response shapes match frontend type definitions. Tests create all data via the API (using the well-known admin user), scoped to a random namespace. Add a new \`contract\` Jest project with Node environment and real fetch.

## Why
Mocked tests missed the PascalCase User bug. These tests validate actual API responses against the TS types the frontend uses. Data setup via API is the most realistic path.

## Jest config change (CRITICAL)

The existing \`integration\` project uses \`testEnvironment: 'jsdom'\` and \`setupTests.ts\` mocks fetch. Contract tests need real HTTP. Add a new project in \`frontend/jest.config.js\`:

\`\`\`javascript
{
  displayName: 'contract',
  preset: 'ts-jest',
  testEnvironment: 'node',  // Node 18+ has native fetch
  testMatch: ['<rootDir>/src/__tests__/contract/**/*.integration.test.ts'],
  roots: ['<rootDir>/src'],
  // NO setupFilesAfterEnv — real fetch, no mocks
  moduleNameMapper: { '^@/(.*)$': '<rootDir>/src/\$1' },
  transform: {
    '^.+\\.tsx?$': ['ts-jest', {
      tsconfig: { esModuleInterop: true, allowSyntheticDefaultImports: true }
    }]
  },
}
\`\`\`

Harness runs \`npx jest --selectProjects contract\`.

## Files to create

### 1. \`frontend/src/__tests__/contract/helpers.ts\`
\`\`\`typescript
const API_BASE = process.env.API_BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:8080';
const CONTRACT_NS = process.env.CONTRACT_NS || 'contract-test';

// Auth as the well-known admin
const ADMIN_TOKEN = 'test:contract-admin:contract-admin@test.local';

export function contractFetch(path: string, token: string = ADMIN_TOKEN, options?: RequestInit) {
  return fetch(\`\${API_BASE}\${path}\`, {
    ...options,
    headers: {
      Authorization: \`Bearer \${token}\`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      ...options?.headers,
    },
  });
}

export function testToken(externalId: string, email: string) {
  return \`test:\${externalId}:\${email}\`;
}

export { CONTRACT_NS };
\`\`\`

### 2. \`frontend/src/__tests__/contract/setup.integration.test.ts\`
Runs first (Jest runs files alphabetically, or use \`--testSequencer\`). Creates the test namespace and supporting data via API:
- \`POST /api/v1/admin/namespaces\` — create namespace with id=CONTRACT_NS
- \`POST /api/v1/system/users\` — create instructor user, student users in that namespace
- \`POST /api/v1/classes\` — create a class
- \`POST /api/v1/classes/{id}/sections\` — create a section
- \`POST /api/v1/problems\` — create a problem
- \`POST /api/v1/sessions\` — create a session
- Store created IDs in a shared module or env for other test files

### 3. \`frontend/src/__tests__/contract/auth.integration.test.ts\`
Test \`GET /api/v1/auth/me\`:
- Validate response has fields: \`id\`, \`external_id\`, \`email\`, \`role\`, \`namespace_id\`, \`display_name\`, \`created_at\`, \`updated_at\`
- Validate all fields are snake_case (not PascalCase)
- Validate types: \`id\` is UUID string, \`role\` is one of UserRole values, \`namespace_id\` is string|null
- Test with admin, instructor, and student users

### 4. \`frontend/src/__tests__/contract/classes.integration.test.ts\`
Test \`GET /api/v1/classes\` (as instructor):
- Validate array of Class objects
- Validate fields: \`id\`, \`namespace_id\`, \`name\`, \`description\`, \`created_by\`, \`created_at\`, \`updated_at\`

### 5. \`frontend/src/__tests__/contract/sections.integration.test.ts\`
Test \`GET /api/v1/sections/my\` (as instructor):
- Validate array of Section objects
- Validate fields: \`id\`, \`namespace_id\`, \`class_id\`, \`name\`, \`semester\`, \`join_code\`, \`active\`, \`created_at\`, \`updated_at\`

### 6. \`frontend/src/__tests__/contract/users.integration.test.ts\`
Test \`GET /api/v1/system/users\` (as system-admin):
- Validate array of User objects (same field checks as auth/me)

### 7. \`frontend/src/__tests__/contract/sessions.integration.test.ts\`
Test \`GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/state\` (as instructor):
- Validate SessionState: \`{ session, students, join_code }\`
- Validate nested Session and SessionStudent field shapes

### 8. \`frontend/src/__tests__/contract/realtime.integration.test.ts\`
Test \`GET /api/v1/realtime/token\`:
- Validate response has \`token\` string field

## Validation approach
For each response:
1. Field presence — every TS interface field exists
2. No PascalCase — assert \`Email\`, \`Role\`, etc. are absent
3. Type correctness — strings, nullables, arrays
4. snake_case convention — all keys match \`/^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/\`

## Implementation steps
1. Add \`contract\` Jest project to \`jest.config.js\`
2. Create helpers.ts
3. Create setup test that builds test data via API
4. Write endpoint contract tests
5. Run with \`make test-integration-contract\`

title: Add CI job for contract tests
description:
## Summary
Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the contract tests as a separate CI job, not blocking unit tests.

## Why
Contract tests are slower (start Postgres + Go server) and should run in parallel with, not block, the existing frontend-ci jobs.

## Files to create

### 1. \`.github/workflows/contract-tests.yml\` (new)
GitHub Actions workflow:
- Trigger: push to main (paths: \`go-backend/**\`, \`frontend/**\`, \`migrations/**\`, \`scripts/seed.sql\`, \`scripts/contract-test-seed.sql\`), PRs to main (same paths), workflow_dispatch
- Single job \`contract-tests\` on \`ubuntu-latest\`
- Services: postgres:15 with health check (use GitHub Actions service containers)
- Steps:
  1. Checkout
  2. Setup Go 1.24
  3. Setup Node 20
  4. \`npm ci\` in frontend/
  5. Run migrations against service container postgres
  6. Seed contract admin: \`psql -f scripts/contract-test-seed.sql\`
  7. Build and start Go server with AUTH_MODE=test, random PORT, in background
  8. Wait for /healthz
  9. Generate random namespace, pass as CONTRACT_NS env
  10. Run \`npx jest --selectProjects contract --no-coverage\` in frontend/
  11. Cleanup: delete namespace via psql

Note: Use \`--selectProjects contract\` (not \`integration\`). The \`contract\` Jest project uses \`testEnvironment: 'node'\` with real fetch.

## Implementation steps
1. Create workflow file
2. Test by pushing to a branch and verifying the job runs

title: Extract shared test Postgres lifecycle script
description:
## Summary
Create a shared script that ensures Postgres is running and seeded for any integration test target. Refactor `test-integration-executor` to use it. This prevents each test harness from reimplementing postgres lifecycle and makes all integration tests safe to run while postgres is already running.

## Why
Multiple integration test types (executor, store, contract) all need Postgres. Currently `test-integration-executor` does its own `docker-compose up -d executor --wait` / `docker-compose down executor`. The contract tests (PLAT-fs7.2) also need Postgres. Shared logic avoids duplication and ensures consistent behavior: start if needed, leave running, never tear down.

## Files to create/modify

### 1. `scripts/ensure-test-postgres.sh` (new file)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

DB_HOST=${DATABASE_HOST:-localhost}
DB_PORT=${DATABASE_PORT:-5432}

# Start postgres if not already running
if ! pg_isready -h "$DB_HOST" -p "$DB_PORT" -q 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "Starting Postgres via docker-compose..."
  docker-compose up -d postgres --wait
fi

# Wait for ready (may have just started)
echo "Waiting for Postgres..."
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  if pg_isready -h "$DB_HOST" -p "$DB_PORT" -q 2>/dev/null; then break; fi
  sleep 1
done
pg_isready -h "$DB_HOST" -p "$DB_PORT" -q || { echo "Postgres not ready"; exit 1; }

# Seed well-known contract test admin (idempotent)
DB_NAME=${DATABASE_NAME:-eval}
DB_USER=${DATABASE_USER:-eval}
DB_PASS=${DATABASE_PASSWORD:-eval_local_password}
PSQL_URL="postgresql://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASS}@${DB_HOST}:${DB_PORT}/${DB_NAME}"
psql "$PSQL_URL" -f scripts/contract-test-seed.sql

echo "Postgres is ready"
```
Make executable: `chmod +x scripts/ensure-test-postgres.sh`

### 2. `scripts/contract-test-seed.sql` (new file)
Idempotent seed for the well-known contract test admin user:
```sql
INSERT INTO users (id, external_id, email, role, namespace_id, display_name)
VALUES ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-ffffffffffff', 'contract-admin', 'contract-admin@test.local', 'system-admin', NULL, 'Contract Admin')
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
```

### 3. `Makefile` — update `test-integration-executor`
Before:
```makefile
test-integration-executor:
	docker-compose up -d executor --wait
	cd executor && EXECUTOR_TEST_URL=http://localhost:8081 go test -v -race -count=1 ./... -run Integration
	docker-compose down executor
```
After:
```makefile
test-integration-executor:
	./scripts/ensure-test-postgres.sh
	docker-compose up -d executor --wait
	cd executor && EXECUTOR_TEST_URL=http://localhost:8081 go test -v -race -count=1 ./... -run Integration
```
Key changes:
- Call shared script first
- Remove `docker-compose down executor` — don't tear down services (other runs may be using them)

Note: The executor service doesn't use Postgres, but calling ensure-test-postgres is harmless and keeps all integration targets consistent. If this feels wrong, the executor target can skip it — but it should still stop doing `docker-compose down`.

## Implementation steps
1. Create `scripts/contract-test-seed.sql`
2. Create `scripts/ensure-test-postgres.sh` (make executable)
3. Update `test-integration-executor` in Makefile to use shared script and remove teardown
4. Test: run `./scripts/ensure-test-postgres.sh` when postgres is stopped, verify it starts. Run again, verify it's a no-op.

title: Add test-integration-store target using shared Postgres script
description:
## Summary
Add a dedicated Makefile target for running Go store integration tests that calls the shared Postgres lifecycle script first. Currently these tests are only runnable if you manually set DATABASE_URL and have Postgres running — there's no Makefile target for them.

## Why
The store integration tests (RLS policy tests in integration_test.go and query tests in store_methods_integration_test.go and store_crud_integration_test.go) require a running Postgres but have no Makefile target. Developers must manually start Postgres and set DATABASE_URL. This should be as easy as \`make test-integration-store\`.

## Parallel safety
These tests currently use \`cleanup()\` which deletes ALL rows from ALL tables. This is not safe for concurrent runs. The tests should be updated to:
1. Create a random namespace per test run
2. Scope all test data under that namespace
3. Clean up only that namespace (DELETE FROM namespaces WHERE id = '<ns>' cascades)
4. Never delete data from other namespaces

Note: The system-admin user (no namespace) should be left alone. Tests that need a system-admin should use the well-known contract-admin seeded by ensure-test-postgres.sh, or create their own with a run-specific external_id.

## Files to modify

### 1. \`Makefile\`
Add:
\`\`\`makefile
test-integration-store:
	./scripts/ensure-test-postgres.sh
	cd go-backend && DATABASE_URL="postgresql://eval:eval_local_password@localhost:5432/eval?sslmode=disable" go test -v -race -count=1 ./internal/store/... -run TestIntegration
\`\`\`
Update aggregate:
\`\`\`makefile
test-integration: test-integration-executor test-integration-store test-integration-contract
\`\`\`

### 2. \`go-backend/internal/store/integration_test.go\` (or rls_integration_test.go after PLAT-2md)
Update \`cleanup()\` to be namespace-scoped:
- \`setupTestDB\` should create a random namespace: \`ns-<uuid>\`
- All \`createNamespace\`, \`createUser\`, etc. helpers should use this namespace
- \`cleanup()\` should \`DELETE FROM namespaces WHERE id = '<ns>'\` instead of truncating all tables
- Return the namespace ID so tests can reference it

### 3. \`go-backend/internal/store/store_methods_integration_test.go\`
Same changes as above:
- \`setupIntegrationDB\` creates a random namespace
- All test data scoped to it
- Cleanup deletes only that namespace

### 4. \`go-backend/internal/store/store_crud_integration_test.go\`
Same pattern — namespace-scoped setup and cleanup.

## Implementation steps
1. Add \`test-integration-store\` Makefile target
2. Refactor integration test setup helpers to use random namespaces
3. Replace bulk cleanup with namespace-scoped deletion
4. Verify: run \`make test-integration-store\` twice in parallel, both pass

title: Narrow-scope tests for E2E-discovered bugs
description:
## Summary
Before merging the E2E test branch (feature/e2e-tests, PR #38), analyze all non-test bugs discovered during E2E debugging and add corresponding unit or integration/contract tests at the narrowest feasible scope.

## Context
During E2E test debugging, several production code bugs were found that are real application bugs, not test issues. These bugs are expensive to catch via E2E — we should cover them with cheaper, faster tests.

## Requirements
- For each non-test bug found during E2E work, add a test at the narrowest scope that provides real coverage
- Prefer unit tests > integration tests > contract tests > E2E tests
- The new tests should fail against the old (buggy) code and pass against the fix

## Bugs to cover

### 1. Sessions listing blocked for students (server.go)
GET /sections/{id}/sessions was inside RequirePermission(auth.PermContentManage) — instructor-only. Students got 403 trying to discover active sessions. RLS already enforces visibility.
- **Fix**: Moved route out of permission group.
- **Test needed**: Handler-level test verifying student role can call ListSessions.

### 2. Centrifugo subscription auth deadlock (centrifugo.go)
authorizeSubscription required GetSessionStudent to succeed, but students need to subscribe BEFORE joining. Chicken-and-egg: page waits for isConnected → needs subscription → needs auth → checks participant table → student hasn't joined yet → 403.
- **Fix**: Fall back to RLS-based GetSession check for non-participants.
- **Tests added in this commit**: StudentSectionMember (200) and StudentNotInSection (403) in centrifugo_test.go. ✅ Done.

### 3. Event format mismatch (useRealtimeSession.ts)
Frontend expected {event, payload} but backend publishes {type, data, timestamp}. All real-time event handlers (student_joined, student_code_updated, featured_student_changed, problem_updated, session_ended) received wrong field names.
- **Fix**: Changed destructuring and updated all handlers to match backend schemas.
- **Tests updated**: useRealtimeSession.test.ts event handling tests. ✅ Done.
- **Note**: This is a frontend-only bug; backend was correct. A contract test verifying event wire format could prevent recurrence.

### 4. featureStudent field name mismatch (realtime.ts)
Frontend sent camelCase {studentId} but backend expects snake_case {student_id}. Also didn't send the code field.
- **Fix**: Changed to {student_id, code}.
- **Tests updated**: realtime.test.ts featureStudent test. ✅ Done.
- **Note**: A contract test verifying the feature endpoint request body could prevent recurrence.

## Remaining work
- Bug #1 still needs a handler-level test (sessions listing for students)
- Consider contract tests for event wire format and feature endpoint body
- CLAUDE.md testing policy already updated ✅
close_reason: All bugs covered: #1 student session listing (TestSectionListSessions_StudentAllowed), #2 centrifugo auth (already done), #3 event format (already done), #4 featureStudent fields (already done)

title: Fix student registration 404: join code hyphen mismatch in GetSectionByJoinCode
close_reason: Fixed: frontend was stripping hyphens from join codes before API calls, causing DB lookup miss

title: Eliminate intra-file test dependency chain in realtime-full contract tests
description:
## Summary

realtime-full.integration.test.ts has a sequential dependency chain where joinSession() sets joinedStudentId, which is then used by updateCode(), executeCode(), featureStudent(), and clearFeatured(). These describe blocks cannot run in isolation or be reordered.

## Files to modify

- frontend/src/__tests__/contract/realtime-full.integration.test.ts

## Implementation

1. Move student registration + joinSession into a top-level beforeAll (or a shared helper that caches the result)
2. Each describe block that needs joinedStudentId should either:
   - Reference the shared variable set in beforeAll, OR
   - Create its own student via a local beforeAll
3. Verify each describe block passes when run in isolation via `jest --testNamePattern`

## Current dependency chain

```
joinSession() → sets joinedStudentId (line 62, set at line 103)
  ↓ used by
updateCode() (line 131)
executeCode() (line 157)
featureStudent() (line 178)
clearFeatured() — doesn't use it directly but depends on prior feature
```

title: Establish Terraform project structure
description:
## Summary
Define the canonical Terraform project structure that all infrastructure modules follow. This is the foundational pattern referenced by all module structure tasks.

## Directory Structure
```
infrastructure/terraform/
├── bootstrap/                    # One-time state backend setup
│   ├── main.tf
│   └── README.md
├── modules/                      # Reusable modules (no env-specific values)
│   ├── vpc/
│   ├── eks/
│   ├── rds/
│   ├── cognito/
│   ├── ses/
│   └── ...
└── environments/                 # Environment-specific instantiation
    ├── staging/
    │   ├── main.tf               # Instantiates modules
    │   ├── backend.tf            # S3 state with staging/ prefix
    │   ├── terraform.tfvars      # Staging-specific values
    │   └── outputs.tf
    └── prod/
        ├── main.tf
        ├── backend.tf            # S3 state with prod/ prefix
        ├── terraform.tfvars      # Prod-specific values
        └── outputs.tf
```

## Environment Strategy
| Environment | Purpose | AWS Resources |
|-------------|---------|---------------|
| Local | Fast dev, unit tests | None (Docker Compose) |
| Staging | CI/CD, E2E tests, integration | Full AWS stack |
| Prod | Real users | Full AWS stack (isolated) |

## Module Design Principles
1. **Modules are environment-agnostic** - no hardcoded values
2. **Required variables have no defaults** - forces explicit config per env
3. **Environment configs provide all values** - via tfvars
4. **State isolation** - separate S3 prefixes per environment
5. **Same module, different tfvars** - staging and prod use identical module code

## Common Variables (every module should accept)
- `environment` - "staging" or "prod"
- `project_name` - for resource naming/tagging

## Configuration Flow: Modules → Apps

Infrastructure modules export outputs that applications need (connection strings, IDs, endpoints). The pattern for getting these values to running applications:

```
┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
│ Module outputs  │ ──▶ │ Environment TF      │ ──▶ │ K8s Pod     │
│ (cognito, rds)  │     │ creates ConfigMap/  │     │ gets env    │
│                 │     │ Secret              │     │ vars        │
└─────────────────┘     └─────────────────────┘     └─────────────┘
```

### Example: Cognito → Go API

**Module exports outputs:**
```hcl
# modules/cognito/outputs.tf
output "user_pool_id" { value = aws_cognito_user_pool.main.id }
output "client_id" { value = aws_cognito_user_pool_client.main.id }
output "client_secret" { value = aws_cognito_user_pool_client.main.client_secret sensitive = true }
output "domain_url" { value = "https://${aws_cognito_user_pool_domain.main.domain}.auth.${var.region}.amazoncognito.com" }
```

**Environment TF creates K8s resources:**
```hcl
# environments/staging/main.tf
resource "kubernetes_config_map" "app_config" {
  metadata { name = "app-config" }
  data = {
    COGNITO_USER_POOL_ID = module.cognito.user_pool_id
    COGNITO_CLIENT_ID    = module.cognito.client_id
    COGNITO_DOMAIN       = module.cognito.domain_url
    AWS_REGION           = var.region
    # Add other module outputs as needed
    DATABASE_HOST        = module.rds.endpoint
    REDIS_HOST           = module.redis.endpoint
  }
}

resource "kubernetes_secret" "app_secrets" {
  metadata { name = "app-secrets" }
  data = {
    COGNITO_CLIENT_SECRET = module.cognito.client_secret
    DATABASE_PASSWORD     = module.rds.password
  }
}
```

**K8s deployment references them:**
```yaml
envFrom:
  - configMapRef:
      name: app-config
  - secretRef:
      name: app-secrets
```

### Local Development

For local dev, equivalent values go in `.env`:
```env
# .env.example (committed, no secrets)
COGNITO_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:9229
COGNITO_USER_POOL_ID=local_pool
COGNITO_CLIENT_ID=local_client
DATABASE_HOST=localhost
DATABASE_PORT=5432
REDIS_HOST=localhost
```

App code reads env vars the same way in all environments - only the source differs (K8s ConfigMap vs .env file).

## Deliverables
- [ ] Create directory structure
- [ ] Create `infrastructure/terraform/README.md` documenting the pattern
- [ ] Document ConfigMap/Secret pattern in README
- [ ] Create `.env.example` template for local dev
- [ ] Ensure PLAT-03e.8 (bootstrap) follows this structure

## References
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` Environments section
- Pattern established for VPC in PLAT-03e.7, generalized here
close_reason: Implemented and reviewed - Terraform project structure established with bootstrap module, environment configs, module skeletons, README documentation, and .env.example

title: Add integration tests for registration RLS policies
description:
## Summary

Add integration tests for the 7 registration RLS policies added in migration 004_registration_rls. The middleware unit tests verify the Go code but don't test the actual PostgreSQL RLS policies against a live database.

## Files to Modify

- `go-backend/internal/store/integration_test.go` - Add tests using the existing test infrastructure

## Implementation Steps

1. Add a `TestRLSPolicies_RegistrationContext` test function
2. Set `app.role = 'registration'` via `setRLSContext` (no user_id or namespace_id)
3. Verify allowed operations:
   - SELECT pending invitations (consumed_at IS NULL, revoked_at IS NULL, expires_at > now())
   - SELECT active sections, classes, namespaces
   - INSERT users
   - INSERT student-role memberships
   - UPDATE invitations (consume)
4. Verify denied operations:
   - SELECT consumed/expired/revoked invitations
   - SELECT inactive sections/namespaces
   - INSERT non-student memberships
   - DELETE any records
   - SELECT users (no policy allows this)
   - Access to problems, sessions, revisions, etc.

## Context

These policies were added in PLAT-6yu to replace NoRLS bypass with limited RLS access for registration routes.

title: Preview-as-student: identity model is broken, multiple UX failures
description:
## Summary

The preview-as-student feature (PR #120) has a broken identity model that causes multiple failures when an instructor tries to use the student workflow. The core issue is that the frontend keeps the instructor's identity in auth context while the backend swaps to a preview student — this mismatch causes 403s, permission errors, and navigation dead-ends.

## Bugs

1. **Execute code 403**: Clicking "Run" in practice mode fails with "You can only execute your own code" because `user.id` (instructor) is sent as `student_id` but the backend auth identity is the preview student.

2. **Section view "insufficient permissions"**: Navigating back via breadcrumb to `/sections/{id}` shows a permission error — the preview student likely lacks section access or the preview middleware doesn't apply correctly on that route.

3. **No classes visible**: Clicking "back to classes" shows empty list — the preview student has no class memberships.

4. **No way to exit preview**: From the broken states above, there's no visible UI to stop previewing, leaving the instructor stuck.

## Root Cause

The current implementation uses a split identity model:
- Frontend: instructor's Firebase UID stays in auth context (`user.id`)
- Backend: middleware swaps identity to preview student via `X-Preview-Section` header

This means every API call that passes `user.id` as a student identifier (execute, join, updateCode) sends the instructor's ID, which doesn't match the backend's swapped identity.

## Required Approach

When previewing as a student, the frontend should treat the user AS the student. `user.id` everywhere should be the preview student's ID. The only differences from a real student should be:

1. Auth middleware that handles the identity swap
2. UI affordance (banner) to exit preview and return to instructor view

There should be NO special-casing in handlers, hooks, or components beyond these two concerns. The preview student should have proper enrollment, section access, and class membership so that all normal student flows work without modification.

## Files Involved

- `go-backend/internal/handler/execute.go` — ownership check at line 93
- `go-backend/internal/handler/preview.go` — enter/exit preview endpoints
- `go-backend/internal/middleware/preview.go` — identity swap middleware
- `go-backend/internal/store/preview.go` — preview student data access
- `frontend/src/contexts/PreviewContext.tsx` — preview state management
- `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/page.tsx` — passes `user.id` at lines 194, 249, 348
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/page.tsx` — section view with preview toggle
- `frontend/src/lib/api-client.ts` — X-Preview-Section header injection
close_reason: Fixed in PR #123 (merged). Full identity swap: frontend swaps cached user profile to preview student, api-client injects X-Preview-Section header, backend preview middleware swaps identity. All CI checks pass including e2e tests.

title: Add preview.enter permission, replace role check in middleware
description:
PreviewMiddleware does a raw role check (instructor.Role == auth.RoleStudent) instead of using the permission system. The EnterPreview handler uses RequirePermission(auth.PermContentManage) which is also wrong — preview is not content management.

## Fix
1. Add `PermPreviewStudent Permission = "preview.enter"` to `go-backend/internal/auth/permissions.go`
2. Grant to instructor, namespace-admin, system-admin roles
3. Change PreviewMiddleware (`go-backend/internal/middleware/preview.go` line 46) to use `!auth.HasPermission(instructor.Role, auth.PermPreviewStudent)` instead of `instructor.Role == auth.RoleStudent`
4. Change EnterPreview handler routes (`go-backend/internal/handler/preview.go` line 42) from `RequirePermission(auth.PermContentManage)` to `RequirePermission(auth.PermPreviewStudent)`
5. Add `'preview.enter'` to frontend `frontend/src/lib/permissions.ts` for instructor+ roles

## Tests
- Add unit test for new permission in `go-backend/internal/auth/permissions_test.go` — verify instructor, namespace-admin, system-admin have `preview.enter`; verify student does not
- Update or add middleware test in `go-backend/internal/middleware/preview_test.go` — verify permission-based check instead of role check
- Update handler test in `go-backend/internal/handler/preview_test.go` — verify route uses PermPreviewStudent

## Files
- `go-backend/internal/auth/permissions.go`
- `go-backend/internal/auth/permissions_test.go`
- `go-backend/internal/middleware/preview.go`
- `go-backend/internal/middleware/preview_test.go`
- `go-backend/internal/handler/preview.go`
- `go-backend/internal/handler/preview_test.go`
- `frontend/src/lib/permissions.ts`

title: Frontend preview identity swap via cached profile
description:
When entering preview, user.id everywhere should be the preview student's ID. Currently the frontend keeps the instructor's identity.

## Fix — swap the cached profile
1. When entering preview:
   - Store preview section ID in sessionStorage (`eval:preview-section-id`)
   - Set `_previewSectionId` in api-client (for header injection)
   - Replace the cached user profile in sessionStorage with the preview student's profile (returned by expanded EnterPreview API from PLAT-gvhg.3)
   - Call setUserProfile() on AuthContext to swap the in-memory user
2. When exiting preview:
   - Clear `_previewSectionId` in api-client AFTER calling the exit API (ordering matters — the exit API needs the preview header to identify the preview student)
   - Clear preview sessionStorage keys and cached profile
   - Call refreshUser() to re-fetch the instructor's real profile from the API
3. On page reload during preview:
   - PreviewProvider hydrates `_previewSectionId` from sessionStorage in a useEffect (NOT at module level — sessionStorage is browser-only, module-level access breaks SSR)
   - AuthContext reads cached profile from sessionStorage — gets the preview student's profile (already swapped on enter)
   - user.id is correct everywhere, API calls include the preview header
4. Guard signOut: if called during preview, clear all preview sessionStorage keys first, then sign out Firebase. No need to re-fetch instructor profile since we're signing out.

## SSR safety
The `_previewSectionId` restoration from sessionStorage MUST happen in a useEffect inside PreviewProvider, not at module init time in api-client.ts. Module-level sessionStorage access will throw during SSR (server-side rendering). PreviewProvider should call a `setPreviewSectionId()` setter on api-client during its mount effect.

## AuthContext integration
Expose `setUserProfile(user: User)` on AuthContextType so PreviewContext can swap the user without triggering an API fetch.

## Tests
- Unit test for PreviewContext: enter sets sessionStorage + swaps profile, exit clears + refetches
- Unit test for signOut guard: preview state cleared before Firebase signOut

## Files
- `frontend/src/contexts/PreviewContext.tsx` — persist to sessionStorage, swap cached profile on enter/exit, useEffect hydration
- `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` — expose setUserProfile(), guard signOut for preview
- `frontend/src/lib/api-client.ts` — add setPreviewSectionId()/getPreviewSectionId() (NO module-level sessionStorage access)

title: Cache user profile in sessionStorage with TTL
description:
AuthContext calls getCurrentUser() (GET /auth/me) on every page load/navigation. Profile data rarely changes — this is wasteful.

## Fix
1. After successful profile fetch, cache the User object in sessionStorage (key: `eval:user-profile`) with a timestamp
2. On mount, if sessionStorage has a cached profile AND Firebase has valid auth state AND cache is not expired, use cached profile immediately (skip API call)
3. TTL: Cache expires after 5 minutes. Short TTL prevents stale profile bugs (role changes, namespace moves) while still eliminating redundant fetches during normal navigation
4. Invalidation triggers that re-fetch from API:
   - `signOut()` — clear cache
   - `refreshUser()` — re-fetch + update cache (called after registration and invite acceptance)
   - **403 response from any API call** — clear cached profile so next navigation re-fetches. This catches stale permissions without adding complexity. 403s are rare in normal use, so the extra fetch is negligible
5. `bootstrapUser()` path still fetches from API (first-time user, no cache)

## 403 invalidation
Add a response interceptor or check in `apiFetch` (api-client.ts): on 403, clear the `eval:user-profile` sessionStorage key. This is fire-and-forget — the current request still fails normally, but the next page load will re-fetch a fresh profile.

## Current callers of refreshUser() (correct invalidation points)
- `register/student/page.tsx:129` — after student registration
- `auth/signin/email/page.tsx:83` — after accepting invite

## Tests
- Unit test for cache read/write/TTL expiry logic in AuthContext
- Unit test for 403 invalidation in api-client

## Files
- `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` — cache on fetch, read from cache on mount, clear on signOut
- `frontend/src/lib/api-client.ts` — clear profile cache on 403
- `frontend/src/lib/api/auth.ts` — no change needed

title: Expand EnterPreview API response with full profile
description:
The EnterPreview API currently returns only `preview_user_id` and `section_id`. The frontend needs the preview student's full profile to swap the cached identity without an extra API call.

## Fix
1. Expand `enterPreviewResponse` in `go-backend/internal/handler/preview.go` to include: id, email, role, namespace_id, permissions
2. Compute permissions for the student role (not the instructor's role) and include them in the response
3. Document that `GetSection` (line 65-73) serves as the authorization gate — it verifies the instructor can see this section via RLS before any pool-level operations. Add a code comment noting this security role.
4. Log unenroll failures in `EnterPreview` and `ExitPreview` instead of silently dropping them. These are currently `_ = h.previewRepo.UnenrollPreviewStudent(...)` — change to log.Warn on error so failures are visible in observability. Silent drops make debugging impossible.

## Tests
- Update handler test in `go-backend/internal/handler/preview_test.go` — verify response includes full profile fields with student-level permissions

## Files
- `go-backend/internal/handler/preview.go` — expand enterPreviewResponse struct, EnterPreview handler, log unenroll errors
- `go-backend/internal/handler/preview_test.go` — update response assertions

title: Add outputCollapsible prop to CodeEditor
description:
## Summary

Add an `outputCollapsible` prop to CodeEditor that shows a collapse/expand toggle for the output panel. Used in projector view to maximize code display space.

## Origin

coding-tool commit cc27abb (#57). Port up to: 78067cb.

## Files

- `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/components/CodeEditor.tsx`
- `frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/components/__tests__/CodeEditor-output-collapsible.test.tsx` (new)

## Implementation

1. Add prop: `outputCollapsible?: boolean` (default false)
2. Add state: `const [outputCollapsed, setOutputCollapsed] = useState(false)`
3. Derive: `const showOutputCollapseToggle = outputCollapsible && outputPosition === 'right' && isDesktop`
4. Render toggle button (between editor and output, ~line 1010 area):
```tsx
{showOutputCollapseToggle && (
  <button
    type="button"
    data-testid="output-collapse-toggle"
    onClick={() => setOutputCollapsed(prev => !prev)}
    className="flex items-center justify-center w-5 h-full bg-gray-100 hover:bg-gray-200 border-x border-gray-300 cursor-pointer flex-shrink-0"
    aria-label={outputCollapsed ? 'Expand output panel' : 'Collapse output panel'}
  >
    {outputCollapsed ? <ChevronLeft size={14} /> : <ChevronRight size={14} />}
  </button>
)}
```
5. Modify output panel width: when `outputCollapsed && outputCollapsible`, set `{ width: 0, overflow: 'hidden' }` instead of percentage width

Reference: coding-tool CodeEditor.tsx lines 45, 73, 110-112, 1011-1040
close_reason: Commit 035c5e1: Added outputCollapsible prop to CodeEditor with toggle button. 5 new tests pass.

title: Investigate executor node pool not scaling to zero
description:
## Summary

The executor node pool is configured with min_node_count = 0 but currently has a node running (gke-eval-prod-gke-executor-cf3a5cae-tph8, e2-medium spot + 100GB pd-balanced). When idle, this wastes ~$13/month (compute + disk). The KEDA ScaledObject should be scaling the executor deployment to 0 replicas, which should trigger the cluster autoscaler to remove the node.

## Investigation needed

1. Check the KEDA ScaledObject configuration for the executor deployment — is minReplicaCount: 0 set?
2. Check if the executor deployment has a PodDisruptionBudget preventing scale-down
3. Check if GKE system pods (gmp-system/collector, kube-system DaemonSets) on the executor node are preventing autoscaler eviction
4. Check cluster autoscaler logs: kubectl -n kube-system logs -l k8s-app=cluster-autoscaler --tail=100

## Files to check

- k8s/base/ — Look for executor ScaledObject, Deployment, and PDB manifests
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/gke/main.tf:150-183 — Executor pool config

## Likely root cause

GKE DaemonSets (fluentbit-gke, gke-metrics-agent, netd, pdcsi-node, kube-proxy, gke-metadata-server) and the gmp-system collector run on every node including executor nodes. These system pods may not have the executor-only toleration properly configured, causing the autoscaler to see the node as occupied even when no executor pods are scheduled. However, GKE system DaemonSets are normally exempt from autoscaler consideration — so the issue is more likely in the KEDA ScaledObject configuration or the executor deployment's minReplicas.

## Expected behavior

When no executor pods are running, the KEDA ScaledObject should scale the deployment to 0 replicas, and the cluster autoscaler should remove the executor node within 10 minutes of it becoming idle.
close_reason: Committed c3fdf31 - fixed KEDA ScaledObject to use GMP frontend, added PodMonitoring

Session create has race condition: end-then-create not wrapped in transaction
description:
## Summary

The Create handler (POST /api/v1/sessions) in go-backend/internal/handler/sessions.go:121-195 calls EndActiveSessions then CreateSession as two separate queries without a transaction. Two concurrent requests can both pass EndActiveSessions before either creates, resulting in two active sessions in the same section.

## Race condition

Request 1: EndActiveSessions (ends 0) -> ... blocked ...
Request 2: EndActiveSessions (ends 0) -> CreateSession (session B created)
Request 1: ... resumes ... -> CreateSession (session A created)
Result: both A and B are active in the same section.

## Files to modify

- go-backend/internal/handler/sessions.go - Create handler (~line 121): wrap EndActiveSessions + CreateSession in a transaction
- go-backend/internal/handler/sessions.go - Reopen handler (~line 316): same pattern needed here after fixing PLAT-7iou
- go-backend/internal/handler/sessions_test.go - Verify transactional behavior (unit test can verify both calls use same repos/tx)

## Implementation

1. Use store.WithTx or equivalent to wrap EndActiveSessions + CreateSession in a single transaction
2. Apply the same transactional pattern to Reopen (EndActiveSessions + UpdateSession)
3. This may require the handler to acquire a transaction from the store layer - check existing transaction patterns in the codebase

title: Disable VPC Flow Logs on subnets (~$5-10/mo savings)
description:
## Summary

All three VPC subnets (GKE, Cloud SQL, public) have flow logs enabled at 50% sampling with full metadata (infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf:46-81). For a small project with a $100/month budget, this generates unnecessary logging costs. Flow logs should be disabled or reduced to on-demand debugging only.

## Files to modify

- infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf — Remove or conditionalize log_config blocks on all three subnets (lines 46-49, 61-65, 77-81)
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/variables.tf — Add enable_flow_logs variable (default false)

## Implementation steps

1. Add an enable_flow_logs boolean variable to modules/vpc/variables.tf defaulting to false
2. Wrap each subnet's log_config block in a dynamic block conditioned on var.enable_flow_logs
3. No changes needed in environments/prod/ since the default is false
4. Run terraform plan to verify only log_config removals
5. Do NOT run terraform apply — human operator applies infra changes

## Notes

- Flow logs can be re-enabled on-demand for debugging by setting enable_flow_logs = true in the environment config
- Reducing sampling to 0.1 or disabling metadata are alternatives if some flow logging is desired
close_reason: Committed 707862f - conditionalized flow logs behind enable_flow_logs variable

title: Contract test coverage: 100% of typed API functions
description:
Ensure every typed API function in frontend/src/lib/api/*.ts has a contract test exercising it against the real backend.

## What contract tests verify

Contract tests validate the **API contract** — the boundary between the typed frontend client and the backend:
- Request field names are correct (snake_case, matching what the backend expects)
- Response shape matches the typed interface (correct keys, types, nullability)

Contract tests do NOT verify persistence, round-trip behavior, business logic, authorization, or error handling. Those belong in unit, integration, or E2E tests at the appropriate layer.

## Subtasks

- PLAT-jcb.1: Add contract test for createSystemInvitation (DONE)
- PLAT-jcb.2: Build contract-coverage tool (DONE)
- PLAT-jcb.3: Add contract tests for remaining ~58 uncovered functions

title: Add contract test for createSystemInvitation

title: Build contract-coverage tool to enforce 100% API function coverage
description:
Build an automated script (scripts/check-contract-coverage.ts or similar) that:
1. Scans all exported async functions from frontend/src/lib/api/*.ts
2. Scans all contract tests in frontend/src/__tests__/contract/*.integration.test.ts for which API functions they import/exercise
3. Reports uncovered functions and exits non-zero if coverage < 100%
4. Can be run in CI to prevent regressions

The tool should be robust: parse actual imports in test files to determine coverage rather than relying on string matching. Output should show covered/uncovered functions grouped by module.
close_reason: Coverage tool built with 20 unit tests, Makefile target, npm script in PR #45

title: Add contract tests for remaining 67 uncovered API functions
description:
Add contract tests for all remaining uncovered API functions. Use `make check-contract-coverage` to track progress.

Current coverage: 13/71 (18.3%). Target: 71/71 (100%).

## Scope: what contract tests verify

Contract tests validate the **API contract** between the typed frontend client and the backend:
- Request field names are correct (e.g. snake_case `target_role`, not camelCase `targetRole`)
- Response shape matches the typed interface (correct keys, correct types)
- Required fields are present and non-null; nullable fields are null or string

Contract tests do NOT verify:
- Persistence (that writes actually stick) — that's for unit/integration tests
- Round-trip behavior (create then read back) — that's for integration/E2E tests
- Business logic or authorization rules — that's for handler/middleware tests
- Error responses or edge cases — that's for unit tests

Keep each test focused: call the typed function, validate the response shape. Clean up created resources in afterAll.

## Pattern to follow

See existing tests in `frontend/src/__tests__/contract/` for the pattern:
- `configureTestAuth(TOKEN)` in beforeAll, `resetAuthProvider()` in afterAll
- Use `state.namespaceId`, `state.classId`, etc. from shared-state
- Use `expectSnakeCaseKeys()`, `expectString()`, `expectNullableString()` validators
- Use unique timestamped values for creates (e.g. `contract-test-${Date.now()}@test.local`)
- Clean up created resources in afterAll with best-effort try/catch

## Modules to cover

- sessions.ts: createSession, endSession, updateSessionProblem, getSessionDetails, getSessionPublicState, analyzeSession, featureCode, reopenSession, listSessionHistoryWithFilters (9 funcs)
- problems.ts: listProblems, getProblem, createProblem, updateProblem, deleteProblem (5 funcs)
- classes.ts: getClass, createClass, updateClass, deleteClass, createSection, updateSection, regenerateJoinCode, addCoInstructor, removeCoInstructor (9 funcs)
- sections.ts: joinSection, leaveSection, getActiveSessions, getClassSections, getSectionInstructors, deleteSection (6 funcs)
- realtime.ts: updateCode, executeCode, featureStudent, clearFeatured, joinSession (5 funcs)
- invitations.ts: listInvitations, createInvitation, revokeInvitation, resendInvitation (4 funcs)
- namespace-invitations.ts: listNamespaceInvitations, createNamespaceInvitation, revokeNamespaceInvitation, resendNamespaceInvitation (4 funcs)
- system.ts: listSystemNamespaces, getSystemNamespace, resendSystemInvitation (3 funcs)
- namespaces.ts: createNamespace, updateNamespace, deleteNamespace, createUser, updateUserRole, deleteUser (6 funcs)
- admin.ts: getAdminStats, listAdminUsers, changeUserRole, deleteAdminUser (4 funcs)
- auth.ts: bootstrapUser (1 func)
- instructor.ts: getInstructorDashboard (1 func)
- execute.ts: executeStandaloneCode (1 func)
close_reason: PR #46 merged — contract tests at 100% coverage

title: Fix contract test: retry backoff causes 5s timeout in multiple suites
description:
Multiple contract test suites fail with 'Exceeded timeout of 5000ms' because the api-client's withRetry() retries failed requests with exponential backoff, exceeding Jest's default 5s timeout.

Affected suites and root causes:
- realtime-full: joinSession retries 401s (student user not in system) 3x with backoff -> 10s
- sections-full: joinSection same issue
- admin: beforeAll createNamespace retries -> hook timeout
- namespaces-full: createUser retries -> test timeout
- auth-full: bootstrapUser retries -> test timeout
- sessions-full: analyzeSession retries against missing AI service -> timeout

Fix options (pick one):
1. Increase Jest timeout for contract tests (e.g. jest.setTimeout(30000) in setup)
2. Disable retry for contract tests via env flag
3. Add explicit timeouts to individual slow tests

Files: frontend/src/__tests__/contract/globalSetup.ts or jest config, plus individual test files
close_reason: Fixed: classified 4xx as non-retryable to prevent backoff timeouts

title: Skip executor build-push when code unchanged
description:
## Summary

The executor build-push job (2m 03s) is the long pole of the deploy pipeline's build phase, but executor code rarely changes. Skip the Docker build when the executor's build inputs haven't changed by using a content-addressed image tag.

## Approach

1. In the executor workflow's `build-push` job, compute a content hash of the build inputs using `go list`:
   ```bash
   FILES=$(cd executor && go list -deps -f '{{range .GoFiles}}{{$.Dir}}/{{.}}{{"\n"}}{{end}}' ./cmd/executor | grep "$(pwd)")
   HASH=$(echo "$FILES" | sort | xargs sha256sum | cat - executor/Dockerfile executor/seccomp-python.policy | sha256sum | cut -c1-16)
   ```
2. Check if `$REGISTRY/executor:content-$HASH` already exists in Artifact Registry.
3. If it exists, retag it with the commit SHA instead of rebuilding.
4. If it doesn't exist, run the full Docker build and push with both `content-$HASH` and commit SHA tags.

## Why `go list` instead of path filters

- `go list -deps` uses the compiler's actual dependency graph — no path lists to maintain
- Automatically includes the right `pkg/` subdirectories (executorapi, httputil, httplog, slogutil, httpmiddleware, ratelimit)
- Excludes `pkg/` changes that don't affect executor (e.g., httputil changes only used by go-backend)
- If someone adds a new `pkg/` import, it's included automatically

## What NOT to skip

Always run executor tests/lint/integration-test regardless — they're fast (~1m 27s parallel) and provide ongoing validation. Only skip the build-push step.

## Files to modify

- `.github/workflows/executor.yaml` — add content hash check and conditional skip/retag to the `build-push` job (lines 76-93)

## Expected impact

Saves ~2 min on the deploy pipeline critical path for most deploys (any push that doesn't change executor code). Executor code changes ~1-2x/month; deploy pipeline runs ~45x/month.

Need to restructure the test to create a replacement session while the first is still active.

title: Sandbox uses --chroot / exposing host filesystem to student code
description:
## Summary
The executor sandbox uses `--chroot /` which exposes the entire host filesystem to student code. The nobody user (65534) can read any world-readable file including /etc/passwd, /proc, and potentially environment variables at /proc/1/environ.

## Files to modify
- `executor/internal/sandbox/sandbox.go` (lines 111-128)

## Implementation
1. Create an empty chroot directory (e.g., /sandbox-root) in the Dockerfile
2. Change `--chroot /` to `--chroot /sandbox-root`
3. Use `--bindmount_ro` to explicitly mount only: python3, required libs (/usr/lib, /lib, possibly /usr/lib64), /dev/null, /dev/urandom
4. Mount the working directory as read-write inside the chroot
5. Verify Python can still import standard library modules
6. Update integration tests to verify filesystem isolation
close_reason: Implemented: restricted chroot to /sandbox-root with explicit bind mounts

title: Go API Database Layer
description:
## Summary
Implement database connectivity with connection pooling, transactions, and RLS context middleware for the Go API.

## Scope
- PostgreSQL connection pool (pgx or database/sql with pgx driver)
- Connection configuration from environment/Secrets Manager
- Transaction management utilities
- RLS context middleware (sets session variables)
- Repository pattern base types

## Connection Pool
- Use pgxpool for connection pooling
- Configure pool size based on environment
- Health check with pool stats
- Graceful shutdown

## RLS Context Middleware
\`\`\`go
func RLSContextMiddleware(pool *pgxpool.Pool) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
    return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
        return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
            user := auth.UserFromContext(r.Context())
            
            // Acquire connection and set session variables
            conn, _ := pool.Acquire(r.Context())
            defer conn.Release()
            
            conn.Exec(r.Context(), "SELECT set_config('app.user_id', $1, true)", user.ID)
            conn.Exec(r.Context(), "SELECT set_config('app.namespace_id', $1, true)", user.NamespaceID)
            
            // Attach conn to context for handlers
            ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), dbConnKey, conn)
            next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
        })
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Transaction Utilities
\`\`\`go
func WithTx(ctx context.Context, fn func(tx pgx.Tx) error) error
\`\`\`

## Deliverables
- \`internal/db/pool.go\` - connection pool setup
- \`internal/db/tx.go\` - transaction utilities
- \`internal/middleware/rls.go\` - RLS context middleware
- \`internal/repository/base.go\` - base repository type
- Integration tests with RLS verification

## Dependencies
- Requires: Go API Project Scaffolding (PLAT-4zm)
- Requires: Database Schema & Row-Level Security (PLAT-49o)

## References
- See `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for RLS middleware pattern
- pgx documentation: github.com/jackc/pgx/v5
close_reason: Merged: feat: Go API Database Layer (#5)

title: Remove redundant Cloud NAT gateway (~$32/mo savings)
description:
## Summary

The VPC module (infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf:125-137) creates a Cloud NAT gateway at ~$32/month, while the NAT module (infrastructure/terraform/modules/nat/main.tf) creates a cost-effective NAT VM (e2-micro, ~$6/month) that does the same job. Both are deployed in prod (infrastructure/terraform/environments/prod/main.tf:64-92). Remove the Cloud NAT to eliminate the redundancy.

## Files to modify

- infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/main.tf — Remove the google_compute_router_nat.nat resource (lines 125-137) and the google_compute_router.router resource (lines 110-119) if no longer needed
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/variables.tf — Remove nat_ip_allocate_option and source_subnetwork_ip_ranges_to_nat variables if they become unused
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/outputs.tf — Remove any Cloud NAT-related outputs

## Implementation steps

1. Verify the NAT VM route covers GKE private node outbound traffic (check route_tags in the NAT module vs GKE node network tags)
2. Remove google_compute_router_nat.nat resource from vpc/main.tf
3. Remove google_compute_router.router if nothing else references it (check for other uses first)
4. Clean up any unused variables and outputs
5. Run terraform plan to verify only the NAT resources are destroyed
6. Do NOT run terraform apply — human operator applies infra changes

## Risk

The NAT VM must handle GKE private node egress (image pulls, API calls). Verify the custom route created by the NAT module applies to GKE nodes before removing Cloud NAT. If the route uses tags that GKE nodes don't have, the nodes will lose internet access.
close_reason: Committed 3f6f387 - removed Cloud NAT/Router from VPC module

Remove unjustified optional chaining on state after loading/error guards in public-view page
description:
## Summary

In `frontend/src/app/(projector)/public-view/page.tsx`, line 102 uses `state?.problem` after the loading guard (line 83) and error guard (line 91) have already returned early. At this point `state` is guaranteed non-null by the hook contract: `useRealtimePublicView` sets `setState(data)` on success and `setError(...)` on failure. The `?.` creates a false impression that `state` might be absent.

## File to modify

`frontend/src/app/(projector)/public-view/page.tsx`

## Implementation

Line 102 — change:
```typescript
const problem = state?.problem as { title: string; description?: string; starter_code?: string } | null;
```
to:
```typescript
const problem = state.problem as { title: string; description?: string; starter_code?: string } | null;
```

title: Bootstrap endpoint (POST /auth/bootstrap) returns 500 - CreateUser fails against production DB
description:
## Summary
POST /api/v1/auth/bootstrap returns HTTP 500 on first login. Firebase auth succeeds (JWT is valid, custom claim check passes), but CreateUser INSERT into the users table fails silently.

## Symptoms
- Browser shows repeated 500s on POST /auth/bootstrap after successful Firebase sign-in
- Go API logs show status=500 but no error details (see PLAT-kkx)
- healthz and readyz pass, so DB connectivity works for basic checks

## Likely causes
1. RLS policy blocking the INSERT - the bootstrap endpoint may not set the right session variables (app.user_id, app.namespace_id, app.role) before the INSERT
2. Missing namespace_id - CreateUser requires namespace_id but the bootstrap handler passes zero-value CreateUserParams (no NamespaceID set)
3. Schema mismatch - production DB migrations may be incomplete

## Files to investigate
- go-backend/internal/handler/auth.go:300-330 (PostBootstrap handler)
- go-backend/internal/store/users.go:183-195 (CreateUser)
- go-backend/internal/middleware/ (RLS context injection)
- migrations/ (users table schema, RLS policies)

## Reproduction
1. Create Identity Platform user with role=system-admin custom claim
2. Sign in via the frontend
3. Frontend calls POST /api/v1/auth/bootstrap
4. Observe 500 response
close_reason: Fixed: migration 005 adds users_registration_select policy. Regression test added in integration_test.go.

title: Centrifugo WebSocket routing: use cluster-internal URL, not public ingress
description:
## Summary
The frontend currently plans to connect to Centrifugo via the public domain (e.g., `wss://eval.delquillan.com/connection/websocket`), meaning WebSocket traffic would exit the cluster through Ingress and come back in. Instead, the frontend should connect to Centrifugo via a cluster-internal Service URL.

## Problem
If the frontend's `NEXT_PUBLIC_CENTRIFUGO_URL` points to the public domain, WebSocket connections go: browser → Ingress → (would need an Ingress path rule for `/connection/websocket`) → Centrifugo pod. This adds latency, exposes Centrifugo through the public load balancer, and requires additional Ingress path configuration.

## Solution
Since the frontend is deployed in the same GKE cluster as Centrifugo:
1. The `NEXT_PUBLIC_CENTRIFUGO_URL` is used by **client-side JavaScript running in the browser**, NOT server-side code. The browser is outside the cluster, so it CANNOT use cluster-internal URLs.
2. Therefore, this needs either:
   - A dedicated Centrifugo Ingress/path rule (e.g., `/ws/*` → centrifugo service), OR
   - A separate subdomain for Centrifugo (e.g., `ws.eval.delquillan.com`), OR
   - The current approach of using the public domain IS correct, but needs an Ingress path rule for `/connection/websocket` → centrifugo service

## Action items
- Decide routing approach for Centrifugo WebSocket from browser
- Add appropriate Ingress path rule or separate Ingress for Centrifugo
- Update `NEXT_PUBLIC_CENTRIFUGO_URL` in the Terraform ConfigMap accordingly

## Context
Discovered during PLAT-663 (Frontend CD) planning. Centrifugo is already deployed in the cluster but its public routing hasn't been configured for the new Ingress architecture.
close_reason: Ingress path rule + BackendConfig added for Centrifugo WebSocket routing

title: Type SessionPublicState.problem as a proper interface instead of unknown
Split auth middleware: JWT validation + user lookup as separate steps
description:
## Summary

Split the auth middleware into two separate steps so registration endpoints can validate JWTs without requiring the user to already exist in the database.

## Current State

The `Authenticator` middleware in `go-backend/internal/middleware/authn.go` does two things:
1. Validates JWT (Firebase token)
2. Looks up user by `external_id` in DB — fails with 401 if not found

This breaks registration flows (`/auth/accept-invite`, `/auth/register-student`) because new users don't exist in the DB yet.

## Security Issue

Current registration endpoints take `external_id` from the **request body**, not from validated JWT claims. An attacker who obtains an invitation token could claim it with their own Firebase UID.

## Implementation

1. **Split middleware:**
   - `JWTValidator` — Validates token, extracts claims, adds claims to context
   - `UserLoader` — Looks up user by claims.Subject, adds user to context (can be skipped)

2. **Update route mounting in `server.go`:**
   - All /api/v1 routes: JWT validation
   - Registration routes (`/auth/accept-invite`, `/auth/register-student`): skip user lookup
   - All other routes: user lookup required

3. **Update registration handlers:**
   - Get `external_id` from JWT claims (context), not request body
   - Remove `external_id` field from request structs

## Files to Modify

- `go-backend/internal/middleware/authn.go` — Split into two middlewares
- `go-backend/internal/middleware/authn_test.go` — Update tests
- `go-backend/internal/auth/context.go` — Add ClaimsFromContext helper
- `go-backend/internal/server/server.go` — Update route mounting
- `go-backend/internal/handler/auth.go` — Use claims from context
- `go-backend/internal/handler/auth_test.go` — Update tests
close_reason: Completed: Split auth middleware into JWTValidator + UserLoader, updated handlers to use claims from context for external_id, fixed contract tests with global setup

Four handler-level negative test cases are missing:
