title: E2E Auth Testing Infrastructure
description:
## Summary
Set up infrastructure for end-to-end testing of registration and authentication flows, including email verification.

## Scope
- Email receiving service for tests to read verification codes
- Test utilities for Cognito user lifecycle
- E2E tests covering: registration → email verification → login → token refresh

## Email Service Options (verify free tiers)

| Service | Free Tier | Notes |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| **Mailtrap** | 100 emails/month | Likely sufficient for E2E |
| **testmail.app** | 100 emails/month | Simple API |
| **Mailosaur** | Free trial only | No permanent free tier (~$10/mo) |
| **Ethereal** | Unlimited | Fake SMTP, good for dev, not real delivery |

**Recommendation:** Start with Mailtrap or testmail.app (free tier), switch if limits are hit.

## Test Cases
1. **Registration flow:** Sign up → receive email → extract code → verify → login
2. **Password reset:** Forgot password → receive email → extract code → reset → login
3. **Token refresh:** Login → wait → refresh token → verify still authenticated

## Implementation Notes
- Use Cognito Admin APIs for test setup/teardown (create users, clean up)
- Email service only needed for the `extract verification code` step
- Consider running full E2E sparingly (nightly) vs admin-bypass tests in CI

## Dependencies
- Requires working Cognito setup (PLAT-dar)
- Requires Go API auth endpoints (PLAT-nqj)

## Future Work
- SAML flow testing (requires mock IdP)

title: Add unit tests for useRealtimeSession and useRealtimePublicView hooks
Clean up unjustified optional chaining and untyped problem fields in frontend
description:
## Summary

Audit found several instances of optional chaining that hide issues rather than handling them, all stemming from `Session.problem` and `SessionPublicState.problem` being typed as `unknown`. Fix the root cause (proper types) and then clean up the surface symptoms (unnecessary `?.`, `as any` casts, silent event dropping).

## Subtasks

1. Type `Session.problem` as `Problem | null` instead of `unknown`
2. Type `SessionPublicState.problem` as a proper interface instead of `unknown`
3. Add warning log when WebSocket events are silently dropped due to uninitialized state
4. Remove unjustified `state?.problem` after loading/error guards in public-view page
5. Replace `as any` cast with proper type on problem access in public-view page

title: Reduce GKE boot disk size from 100GB to 30GB (~$14/mo savings)
description:
## Summary

Both GKE node pools use 100GB pd-balanced boot disks, but actual usage is minimal (6% CPU, 32% memory on the default node). The default COS_CONTAINERD image needs ~15-20GB. Reducing to 30GB saves ~$14/month across both pools.

## Files to modify

- infrastructure/terraform/modules/gke/main.tf — Add disk_size_gb to both node_config blocks (lines 129-138 for default pool, lines 161-177 for executor pool)
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/gke/variables.tf — Add default_pool_disk_size_gb and executor_pool_disk_size_gb variables with 30GB defaults
- infrastructure/terraform/environments/prod/main.tf — Pass disk size variables to the GKE module if overriding defaults

## Implementation steps

1. Add disk_size_gb variable for each pool in modules/gke/variables.tf with default of 30
2. Add disk_size_gb = var.default_pool_disk_size_gb to google_container_node_pool.default.node_config
3. Add disk_size_gb = var.executor_pool_disk_size_gb to google_container_node_pool.executor.node_config
4. Run terraform plan to verify the change
5. Do NOT run terraform apply — human operator applies infra changes

## Notes

- Changing disk size requires node pool recreation (nodes will be drained and replaced)
- Both pools use spot instances, so recreation is low-risk
- 30GB is comfortable for COS_CONTAINERD images + container layers
close_reason: Committed 14af3d4 - reduced GKE boot disk from 100GB to 30GB

title: Extract shared noopRegisterer, health response types, and error helpers across services

Add GET /api/v1/public/problems/{id} endpoint (no auth) for public problem pages
description: The frontend page at (public)/problems/[id]/page.tsx calls getPublicProblem() via publicGet('/public/problems/{id}'), but the backend endpoint was never built. The contract test explicitly skips with 'The backend does not yet have a /public/problems/{id} endpoint'. title: Add gemini-2.5-flash-lite as default model and increase analyze timeout
description: The analyze endpoint times out with 11-23 student submissions because gemini-2.5-flash (a thinking model) exceeds the 30s server timeout. Three changes: (1) Increase analyze route timeout from 30s to 120s in server.go, (2) Change default model from gemini-2.5-flash to gemini-2.5-flash-lite in gemini.go, (3) Add gemini-2.5-flash-lite as a dropdown option in the frontend model selector and make it the default.

title: Backend error responses lack 'code' field; frontend error-code branching is dead code
description: The Go backend's httputil.WriteError (pkg/httputil/response.go:36) returns {"error": "message"} with no code field. The frontend pages (invite/accept, register/student) have extensive error-code mapping logic (INVITATION_CONSUMED, INVALID_CODE, SECTION_INACTIVE, OTP_EXPIRED, etc.) that reads (error as any).code, which is always undefined. This is a pre-existing issue predating the typed-clients PR. Either add a WriteErrorWithCode to httputil and update handlers to send codes, or remove the dead error-code mapping from the frontend.

title: Add warning log when WebSocket events are silently dropped due to uninitialized state
Strip solution and test_cases from Problem in student-facing section-problems response
description:
## Summary
The ListSectionProblems handler returns full Problem objects to students, including solution and test_cases fields that should be instructor-only.

## Details
- Handler: go-backend/internal/handler/section_problems.go lines 34-59
- Store query: go-backend/internal/store/section_problems.go lines 72-103 — SELECT includes p.solution and p.test_cases
- Struct: go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go lines 122-137 — Problem.Solution and Problem.TestCases are public with JSON tags

The handler returns []PublishedProblemWithStatus directly to JSON with no field filtering. Students see the full Problem struct including solution code and test case definitions.

## Fix
In the List handler (section_problems.go ~line 55), nil out sensitive fields before encoding:
```go
for i := range results {
    results[i].Problem.Solution = nil
    results[i].Problem.TestCases = nil
}
```
Alternatively, exclude these columns from the store query, but handler-level is simpler and keeps the store method reusable for instructor contexts.

Respect the SectionProblem.ShowSolution flag — only strip solution when show_solution is false.
close_reason: Fixed: strip solution/test_cases in List handler

title: Frontend Migration: Supabase to Go Backend + Firebase Auth + Centrifugo
description:
## Summary
Migrate the Next.js frontend at /workspaces/coding-tool from Supabase to the new Go backend + Identity Platform (Firebase Auth SDK) + Centrifugo stack. Big-bang cutover — no feature flags or incremental migration.

## Context: What We're Migrating From
The frontend is a Next.js 16 App Router app (React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind) at /workspaces/coding-tool with:
- **Auth**: Supabase Auth (email/password) via @supabase/supabase-js. AuthContext in src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx provides signIn/signOut/refreshUser. Server-side auth provider at src/server/auth/supabase-provider.ts. MFA via cookies.
- **Realtime**: Supabase Broadcast (WebSocket) via @supabase/supabase-js. Channel pattern: session:{sessionId}. Events: student_joined, student_code_updated, session_ended, featured_student_changed, problem_updated. Main hook: src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts with 2s polling fallback.
- **API**: ~50 Next.js API routes in src/app/api/ — many are thin proxies to Supabase, some have business logic. 9 Supabase repository classes in src/server/persistence/supabase/ (users, namespaces, classes, sections, memberships, problems, sessions, invitations, revisions).
- **Client data fetching**: Custom React hooks (useSessionOperations, useRealtimeSession, useSections, useClasses, useNamespaces, useInvitations, useRevisionHistory, usePermissions) calling internal /api/* routes via fetch() with retry logic from src/lib/api-utils.ts.

## Context: What We're Migrating To
- **Auth**: Firebase Auth SDK (client-side). onAuthStateChanged for state, getIdToken() for Bearer tokens, signInWithEmailAndPassword/createUserWithEmailAndPassword for flows. User profile from GET /api/v1/auth/me.
- **Realtime**: centrifuge-js library. Connection token from GET /api/v1/realtime/token, subscription token from GET /api/v1/realtime/token?channel=session:{id}. Same event names and similar payloads.
- **API**: Direct browser-to-Go-backend calls (no BFF). Go backend at /workspaces/eval serves all endpoints under /api/v1/. Auth via Authorization: Bearer <firebase-id-token> header.
- **Architecture**: Delete all Next.js API routes (src/app/api/), all server persistence (src/server/), all Supabase deps. Frontend becomes a pure client-side app calling the Go backend directly.

## Prerequisites
- PLAT-vyf (API Feature Parity) must be complete — Go backend needs all endpoints
- Go backend deployed and accessible
- Identity Platform configured with email/password auth
- Centrifugo deployed with JWT auth

## Architecture Decisions
- Direct browser → Go backend calls (no BFF/proxy layer)
- Delete all Next.js API routes and server persistence layer
- Delete Supabase dependencies entirely
- Firebase Auth SDK on the client for auth state + ID tokens
- centrifuge-js for realtime
close_reason: Frontend fully migrated to Firebase Auth + Go backend + Centrifugo

title: Create typed Go backend API client module
description:
## Summary
Create a typed API client module that all hooks and components will use to call the Go backend directly from the browser, replacing the current pattern of calling internal Next.js /api/* routes.

## Context
Currently all data fetching goes through ~50 Next.js API routes (src/app/api/) which proxy to Supabase. After migration, the browser calls the Go backend at /api/v1/* directly. This client is the foundation -- every other migration task depends on it.

The existing retry logic in src/lib/api-utils.ts (withRetry, fetchWithRetry, fetchJsonWithRetry with exponential backoff + jitter) should be reused or consolidated into the new client.

IMPORTANT: The Go backend uses snake_case JSON (namespace_id, display_name, created_at) but the frontend TypeScript types use camelCase. The API client MUST include a response transformer that converts snake_case keys to camelCase on all responses, and a request transformer that converts camelCase to snake_case on outgoing bodies. This is a cross-cutting concern -- solving it here prevents every hook from independently handling the mapping.

## Files to create
- src/lib/api/client.ts -- base client with auth token injection, error handling, retry logic, snake_case/camelCase transformation
- src/lib/api/types.ts -- TypeScript types matching Go backend response shapes in camelCase
- src/lib/api/transform.ts -- snake_case/camelCase key transformation utilities

## Files to modify
- src/lib/api-utils.ts -- reuse existing retry logic, or consolidate into new client

## Implementation
1. Create snake_case/camelCase key transformers (recursive, handles nested objects and arrays)
2. Create ApiClient with get/post/put/patch/delete methods
3. Base URL from NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL env var
4. Auto-inject Authorization: Bearer token from a token getter function (accepts () => Promise<string>) to avoid circular dependency with auth context
5. Apply camelCase transformation to all JSON responses automatically
6. Apply snake_case transformation to all outgoing request bodies automatically
7. Standard error handling: parse JSON error responses, map to typed ApiError
8. Reuse existing withRetry / exponential backoff from api-utils.ts
9. Export typed endpoint methods organized by resource (api.sessions.list(), api.classes.get(id), etc.)

## Go backend response shapes reference
See /workspaces/eval/go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go for domain types. JSON uses snake_case.

title: Replace auth: Supabase → Firebase Auth SDK

## Current State

## Target State

- src/lib/api-utils.ts (keep retry logic, remove Supabase-specific parts)

description:

title: Replace Supabase Auth with Firebase Auth SDK
description:
## Summary
Replace Supabase Auth with Firebase Auth SDK for client-side authentication. This is the most critical change -- every API call depends on Firebase ID tokens.

## Context
Current auth: Supabase Auth via @supabase/supabase-js. AuthContext (src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx) provides user, isAuthenticated, isLoading, mfaPending, signIn(email, password), signOut(), refreshUser(), sendMfaCode(), verifyMfaCode(code). Server-side provider at src/server/auth/supabase-provider.ts. MFA uses cookies (mfa_pending).

Target auth: Firebase Auth SDK (firebase/auth). Client-side only. onAuthStateChanged for state, getIdToken() for Bearer tokens. User profile fetched from Go backend GET /api/v1/auth/me after Firebase auth succeeds. Go backend validates Firebase JWTs (RS256, issuer: https://securetoken.google.com/{projectID}).

## Files to create
- src/lib/firebase.ts -- initialize Firebase app with config from NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_* env vars

## Files to modify
- src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx -- rewrite: onAuthStateChanged, getIdToken(), signInWithEmailAndPassword, createUserWithEmailAndPassword, signOut. Fetch user profile from GET /api/v1/auth/me after auth.
- src/app/(public)/auth/signin/page.tsx -- call Firebase signInWithEmailAndPassword directly
- src/app/(public)/auth/bootstrap/page.tsx -- migrate bootstrap/admin-setup flow to Firebase auth + Go backend
- src/app/(public)/register/ -- call Firebase createUserWithEmailAndPassword + Go backend POST /api/v1/auth/register-student
- src/app/(public)/invite/accept/page.tsx -- Firebase auth + Go backend GET/POST /api/v1/auth/accept-invite

## Files to delete
- src/server/auth/supabase-provider.ts
- src/server/auth/mfa-cookie.ts
- src/lib/supabase/client.ts

## Implementation
1. npm install firebase / npm uninstall @supabase/supabase-js
2. Create src/lib/firebase.ts with Firebase app initialization
3. Rewrite AuthContext to use Firebase Auth
4. Update sign-in page
5. Update bootstrap page (initial admin setup)
6. Update registration flows (student registration, invite acceptance)
7. MFA: Firebase supports TOTP MFA natively -- wire up or defer based on current usage

title: Review accept-invite auth flow for Go backend
description:
## Summary
The invite accept page (src/app/(public)/invite/accept/page.tsx) was migrated from Supabase but the auth flow may be incomplete. The POST /auth/accept-invite call uses raw fetch with no auth header. Need to verify the Go backend accept-invite endpoint handles token verification correctly.

## Files
- frontend/src/app/(public)/invite/accept/page.tsx
close_reason: Accept-invite auth flow verified, handler tests and E2E tests pass

title: ExecutionResult type duplicated in 3 places with conflicting casing
description:
## Summary
ExecutionResult is defined in three places with inconsistent field naming:
- types/api.ts (lines 161-167): uses execution_time (snake_case, matches API)
- types/session.ts (lines 35-41): uses executionTime (camelCase)
- instructor/types.ts (lines 52-57): uses executionTime (camelCase)

apiPost<ExecutionResult> in useRealtimeSession uses the camelCase type but API returns snake_case, so executionTime is always undefined.

## Fix
Consolidate to one definition. API-response shape in types/api.ts, client camelCase shape in one place with explicit mapping.

title: ExecutionResult type duplicated in 3 places with conflicting casing
description:
## Summary
ExecutionResult is defined in three places with inconsistent field naming:
- types/api.ts (lines 161-167): uses execution_time (snake_case, matches API)
- types/session.ts (lines 35-41): uses executionTime (camelCase)
- instructor/types.ts (lines 52-57): uses executionTime (camelCase)

apiPost<ExecutionResult> in useRealtimeSession uses the camelCase type but API returns snake_case, so executionTime is always undefined at runtime.

## Fix
Consolidate to one definition. API-response shape in types/api.ts, client camelCase shape in exactly one place with explicit mapping.

title: Centrifugo event payloads assumed camelCase but backend sends snake_case
description:
## Summary
useRealtimeSession.ts (lines 194-304) handles Centrifugo events with camelCase field access:
- student_joined handler accesses student.userId, student.name, student.code, student.executionSettings
- student_code_updated handler accesses payload.studentId
But Go backend publishes snake_case (user_id, last_update, execution_settings, student_id).

Students won't appear or will have missing data in real-time updates.

## Files
- frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts:194-304

## Fix
Either add a mapping function for incoming Centrifugo payloads or update field access to match the wire format. Verify the exact wire format from Go publisher code in go-backend/internal/handler/.

title: Accept-invite POST missing invitation token/ID
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/app/(public)/invite/accept/page.tsx (lines 207-215): POST to /auth/accept-invite sends only displayName and password, but does not include the token_hash, access_token, or invitation_id from the initial GET verification step. The backend has no way to associate this POST with the verified invitation. Invitation acceptance will fail.

## Files
- frontend/src/app/(public)/invite/accept/page.tsx:207-215

## Fix
Include the invitation token or ID (from URL params or GET verification response) in the POST body so the backend can associate the registration with the correct invitation.

title: Instructor session page accesses camelCase fields on snake_case API data
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/session/[id]/page.tsx (lines 76, 114, 120): The realtimeSession (from API SessionStateResponse) is cast to SessionStateFromAPI with camelCase fields (sectionId, sectionName, joinCode), but Go backend returns section_id, section_name, join_code. These properties will always be undefined, causing the instructor session page to show 'Session' instead of the real section name, and the join code will never display.

## Files
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/session/[id]/page.tsx:76,114,120

## Fix
Either update the local type to match snake_case wire format, or add a mapping function when receiving the API response.

title: Bootstrap page /auth/bootstrap has no access control
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/app/(public)/auth/bootstrap/page.tsx (lines 1-140): The admin bootstrap page at /auth/bootstrap is always accessible with no server-side or client-side guard checking whether an admin already exists. An attacker could create additional system-admin accounts at any time. The page says 'for initial system setup only' but there is no enforcement.

## Files
- frontend/src/app/(public)/auth/bootstrap/page.tsx:1-140

## Fix
Must be gated by either a server-side check (the /auth/register endpoint should reject if an admin exists) or a client-side pre-check that queries whether bootstrap is still allowed.

title: initialLoadRef prevents re-loading when sessionId changes
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts (lines 113-149): The ref initialLoadRef is set to true after the first load but never reset when sessionId changes. If the component re-renders with a different sessionId (e.g. navigating between sessions), the initial load will be skipped entirely, showing stale data from the previous session.

## Files
- frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts:113-149

## Fix
Reset initialLoadRef.current = false when sessionId changes (e.g. in the useEffect dependency or a separate effect that watches sessionId).

title: Debounced updateCode swallows errors as unhandled promise rejections
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts (lines 392-396): The debounce wrapper fires-and-forgets the async updateCodeImmediate. Any errors thrown by the API call become unhandled promise rejections rather than being caught by callers. The student page at line 175 calls realtimeUpdateCode but cannot detect failures.

## Files
- frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts:392-396
- frontend/src/app/(fullscreen)/student/page.tsx:175

## Fix
Add a .catch() in the debounce wrapper that sets error state, or restructure so the caller can handle errors.

title: api-client error test doesn't verify error message or status property
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/lib/__tests__/api-client.test.ts:147 — Error handling test uses bare .rejects.toThrow() without verifying the error message or .status property. A wrong error message or missing status would still pass.

## Files
- frontend/src/lib/__tests__/api-client.test.ts:147

## Fix
Assert the thrown error's message string and .status field explicitly.

title: Replace Supabase Broadcast with centrifuge-js for realtime
description:
## Summary
Replace Supabase Realtime Broadcast with centrifuge-js for WebSocket session realtime.

## Context
Current realtime: Supabase Broadcast via @supabase/supabase-js. Channel pattern: session:{sessionId}. Events: student_joined, student_code_updated, session_ended, featured_student_changed, problem_updated. Main hook: src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts (~400 lines) with 2s polling fallback when disconnected. Broadcast helper at src/lib/supabase/broadcast.ts.

Target realtime: Centrifugo via centrifuge-js. Connection token from GET /api/v1/realtime/token (HMAC-SHA256 JWT, 15min expiry). Subscription token from GET /api/v1/realtime/token?channel=session:{id} (requires session participant). Same event names. Event payload format: {type: string, data: any, timestamp: string}.

## Files to create
- src/lib/centrifugo.ts -- Centrifuge client factory. Uses the API client from PLAT-uum.1 for token fetching (GET /api/v1/realtime/token). Auto-reconnect with token refresh via centrifuge-js getToken callback.

## Files to modify
- src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts -- complete rewrite using centrifuge-js. Maintain same public API: session, students, featuredStudent, loading, error, connectionStatus, isConnected, updateCode, executeCode, featureStudent, clearFeaturedStudent, joinSession
- src/components/ConnectionStatus.tsx -- update connection state types if needed

## Files to delete
- src/lib/supabase/broadcast.ts

## Implementation
1. npm install centrifuge
2. Create src/lib/centrifugo.ts -- use API client (from PLAT-uum.1) for token requests, not raw fetch()
3. Rewrite useRealtimeSession to use Centrifuge subscription
4. Handle all 5 event types with matching payload shapes
5. Keep polling fallback for disconnected state
6. Connection token refresh before expiry via centrifuge-js getToken callback

title: useNamespaces error test swallows rejection in try/catch
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/hooks/__tests__/useNamespaces.test.ts:72-78 — Error path test swallows the rejection with a try/catch inside act(). This masks whether the hook actually propagates the error correctly to callers vs. only setting internal state. The test should verify the returned promise rejects (or does not) depending on design intent.

## Files
- frontend/src/hooks/__tests__/useNamespaces.test.ts:72-78

## Fix
Restructure test to verify both internal error state AND whether the promise rejects/resolves as expected.

title: No integration tests for api-client auth + fetch pipeline
description:
## Summary
All hook tests mock the entire api-client module. No real HTTP logic, URL construction, or error transformation is exercised. A completely broken api-client would still let all tests pass. Zero integration-level tests exercise the real api-client fetch path (Firebase token acquisition -> URL construction -> fetch -> error parsing -> retry).

## Files
- frontend/src/lib/api-client.ts
- frontend/src/hooks/__tests__/*.test.ts

## Fix
Add MSW-based (or similar) integration tests for at least the critical auth + fetch flow to catch real regressions.

title: Centrifugo tests only verify mock wiring, not connection lifecycle
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/lib/__tests__/centrifugo.test.ts — Tests mock both the Centrifuge constructor and fetch. The createCentrifuge test only verifies the constructor was called with the right URL and that getToken works, but never tests what happens when the Centrifuge client is actually used (connect, subscribe). Real connection error handling is untested.

## Files
- frontend/src/lib/__tests__/centrifugo.test.ts

## Fix
Add tests that exercise connect -> subscribe -> receive publication -> disconnect lifecycle, at least at the mock level verifying event handler registration and error callback behavior.

title: Duplicate Session type in types/api.ts vs types/session.ts with incompatible shapes
description:
## Summary
Session is defined in two places with incompatible shapes:
- types/api.ts (line 92): snake_case fields, string dates, problem: unknown
- types/session.ts (line 57): camelCase fields, Date objects, typed Problem import

Both are actively imported. useRealtimeSession receives API snake_case JSON but sets state typed as camelCase Session, meaning types silently mismatch at runtime.

## Files
- frontend/src/types/api.ts:92
- frontend/src/types/session.ts:57
- frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts

## Fix
Consolidate to one wire-format type in types/api.ts and one client type in types/session.ts with an explicit mapping function between them.

title: Dead types/classes.ts duplicates types/api.ts
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/types/classes.ts defines camelCase duplicates of Class, Section, SectionMembership from types/api.ts. It is not imported anywhere — dead code left from Supabase migration that will confuse future developers.

## Files
- frontend/src/types/classes.ts

## Fix
Delete types/classes.ts.

title: Dead types/testing.ts duplicates types/problem.ts
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/types/testing.ts defines identical test case interfaces to types/problem.ts. Only imported by one test file. All production code uses types/problem.ts.

## Files
- frontend/src/types/testing.ts

## Fix
Delete types/testing.ts. Update the one test file to import from types/problem.ts.

title: Student type defined in 3 incompatible places
description:
## Summary
Student is defined with incompatible shapes in three places:
- types/session.ts (line 46): { userId, name, code, lastUpdate: Date, executionSettings? }
- instructor/types.ts (line 18): { id, name, hasCode, executionSettings? } (different field names, id vs userId, boolean hasCode vs actual code)
- instructor/types.ts (line 29): RealtimeStudent { id, name, code?, executionSettings? }

The instructor Student/RealtimeStudent types overlap with types/session.ts Student but use different field names. This will cause bugs when passing data between hooks (which use session.ts types) and instructor components (which use local types).

## Files
- frontend/src/types/session.ts:46
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/types.ts:18,29

## Fix
Consolidate to canonical Student type(s) with explicit mapping where shapes differ.

title: ExecutionSettings duplicated inline in instructor/types.ts
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/types.ts (lines 22-26, 33-37) defines inline object type { randomSeed?, stdin?, attachedFiles? } instead of importing the canonical ExecutionSettings from types/problem.ts (line 14).

## Files
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/types.ts:22-26,33-37
- frontend/src/types/problem.ts:14

## Fix
Import ExecutionSettings from types/problem.ts instead of re-declaring.

title: Dead fetchWithRetry/fetchJsonWithRetry in api-utils.ts bypasses auth
description:
## Summary
frontend/src/lib/api-utils.ts (lines 224-268) contains fetchWithRetry and fetchJsonWithRetry which duplicate the functionality of apiFetch/apiGet in api-client.ts. Both wrap fetch with retry logic and JSON parsing. api-client.ts uses withRetry from api-utils.ts internally but the standalone functions are an alternative code path that bypasses auth. No call sites use them — dead code that could mislead developers into bypassing authentication.

## Files
- frontend/src/lib/api-utils.ts:224-268

## Fix
Delete fetchWithRetry and fetchJsonWithRetry. Keep withRetry (used by api-client.ts).

title: No systematic snake_case to camelCase mapping layer for API responses
description:
## Summary
types/api.ts uses snake_case (matching Go JSON tags) while types/session.ts, types/classes.ts, types/problem.ts use camelCase (frontend convention). There is no mapping layer and we don't want one.

This is the root cause of multiple bugs: ExecutionResult.executionTime undefined, instructor session page sectionName undefined, Centrifugo payload field access failures.

## Decision
Standardize on snake_case throughout the frontend, matching the wire format. No transformation layer — that's just cruft.

## Files
- frontend/src/types/api.ts (snake_case — canonical)
- frontend/src/types/session.ts (camelCase — must convert to snake_case)
- frontend/src/types/problem.ts (camelCase — must convert to snake_case)
- frontend/src/app/(app)/instructor/types.ts (camelCase — must convert to snake_case)
- All components/hooks that access these fields

## Fix
1. Delete camelCase type definitions (types/session.ts, types/classes.ts or merge into api.ts)
2. Update all field access across components and hooks to use snake_case
3. Update all test mocks to use snake_case (this also prevents mock/reality divergence)

title: Migrate all data-fetching hooks to Go backend API client
description:
## Summary
Update all custom React hooks to use the new typed API client (from task PLAT-uum.1) instead of calling internal /api/* routes via fetch().

## Context
Currently hooks call internal Next.js API routes like fetch('/api/sessions/...') which proxy to Supabase. After migration, hooks use the typed API client to call the Go backend directly at /api/v1/*.

## Files to modify
- src/hooks/useSessionOperations.ts — /api/sessions/* → api.sessions.* (list, get, create, update, delete, reopen, history)
- src/hooks/useRevisionHistory.ts — /api/sessions/*/revisions → api.revisions.* (list, create)
- src/hooks/useSections.ts — /api/sections/* → api.sections.* (list, get, join, leave, members, regenerateCode, instructors)
- src/hooks/useClasses.ts — /api/classes/* → api.classes.* (list, get, create, update, delete)
- src/hooks/useNamespaces.ts — /api/system/namespaces/* → api.namespaces.* (list, get, create, update, capacity)
- src/hooks/usePermissions.ts — update if it fetches from API
- src/hooks/useInvitations.ts — /api/namespace/invitations/* → api.invitations.* (list, get, create, revoke, resend)

## Implementation
1. For each hook, replace fetch('/api/...') with typed API client calls
2. Response shapes should be nearly identical since PLAT-vyf targets API parity, but verify and add mapping if Go backend uses different field names (Go uses snake_case JSON)
3. Remove any header/cookie manipulation — auth is handled by API client's token injection
4. Update error handling to use API client's typed errors
5. The API client handles retry logic, so remove any per-hook retry wrappers

title: Inconsistent error handling pattern across data hooks
description:
## Summary
Error handling varies across hooks:
- useClasses.ts: err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'An error occurred'
- useSessionOperations.ts: err.message || 'Failed to create session' with catch (err: any)
- useRealtimeSession.ts: e.message || 'Failed to load session state' with catch (e: any)

The codebase has createUserFriendlyError and classifyError in lib/error-messages.ts / lib/api-utils.ts, but no hook uses them. Either adopt the error classification utilities consistently or remove them.

## Files
- frontend/src/hooks/useClasses.ts
- frontend/src/hooks/useSessionOperations.ts
- frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts
- frontend/src/lib/error-messages.ts
- frontend/src/lib/api-utils.ts

## Fix
Adopt createUserFriendlyError/classifyError consistently across all hooks, or delete the unused utilities if a simpler pattern is preferred.

title: No integration test for ProtectedRoute + AuthContext redirect flow
description:
## Summary
The ProtectedRoute and AuthContext are tested separately but never together in a single test that verifies the full redirect flow from 'Firebase fires auth state' through 'ProtectedRoute renders or redirects.'

## Files
- frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx
- frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx

## Fix
Add an integration test that wires AuthContext (with mocked Firebase) and ProtectedRoute together, verifying the full flow: unauthenticated -> redirect, authenticated wrong role -> redirect, authenticated correct role -> render children.

title: No integration test for ProtectedRoute + AuthContext redirect flow
description:
## Summary
ProtectedRoute and AuthContext are tested separately but never together in a single test that verifies the full redirect flow from 'Firebase fires auth state' through 'ProtectedRoute renders or redirects.'

## Files
- frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx
- frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx

## Fix
Add an integration test that wires AuthContext (with mocked Firebase) and ProtectedRoute together, verifying: unauthenticated -> redirect, authenticated wrong role -> redirect, authenticated correct role -> render children.

title: Add frontend test jobs to CI (lint, typecheck, unit tests)
description:
## Summary
The frontend has 1780+ Jest tests, TypeScript strict mode, and ESLint, but none of these run in CI. Cloud Build only builds/tests the Go backend.

## Fix
Add CI steps (Cloud Build or GitHub Actions) that run on PRs touching frontend/:
1. npx tsc --noEmit (typecheck)
2. npx jest --no-coverage (unit tests)
3. npx next lint (ESLint)

## Files
- cloudbuild.yaml or .github/workflows/ (new or modified)
- frontend/package.json (verify scripts exist)

title: Duplicate Problem and Session interfaces in types/api.ts vs types/problem.ts and types/session.ts
Duplicate Problem and Session interfaces — consolidate type hierarchy
description: types/api.ts defines Problem (line 76) and Session (line 92) as wire-format interfaces with string timestamps and unknown/untyped fields. types/problem.ts (line 64) and types/session.ts (line 56) define incompatible versions with Date timestamps, typed TestCase[], and optional vs nullable fields. Consumers import from different files creating silent type mismatches. Consolidate into a single hierarchy: api.ts for wire types, problem.ts/session.ts extend with rich client types and explicit mapper functions.

title: ExecutionResult defined in 3 places — consolidate
description: ExecutionResult is defined in types/api.ts (line 161, canonical), OutputPanel.tsx (line 6, local copy), and CodeEditor.tsx (line 15, local copy missing stdin field). Delete local re-definitions in OutputPanel.tsx and CodeEditor.tsx; import from types/api.ts.
close_reason: Deleted duplicate ExecutionResult, import from api.ts

title: useApiDebugger calls fetch directly without auth
description: hooks/useApiDebugger.ts line 36 calls raw fetch('/api/sessions/...') without auth headers or retry logic. Should use apiFetch from lib/api-client.ts. Will fail on any endpoint requiring authentication.

title: Debounced updateCode never cancelled on unmount
description: The debounce helper in useRealtimeSession.ts (lines 15-32) returns a debounced function with no cancel() method. On unmount, pending calls can fire, calling setStudents/setError on unmounted components and making unexpected API calls. Add cancel support and call it in the cleanup effect.
close_reason: Added cancel() to debounce, cleanup on unmount

title: instructor/types.ts parallel type system — collapse into canonical types
description: app/(app)/instructor/types.ts defines ClassInfo, ClassWithSections, Student, RealtimeStudent, ProblemSummary as local types instead of importing/extending from types/api.ts and types/problem.ts. Student shape diverges from types/session.ts Student. ExecutionSettings is inlined rather than imported. Collapse into re-exports and extensions of canonical types.

title: Migrate page components with direct API calls
description:
## Summary
Update page components that make direct fetch() calls to /api/* routes (bypassing hooks) to use the API client or delegate to hooks.

## Files to modify (search all .tsx files for fetch('/api/ and fetch(\`/api/)
Likely candidates:
- src/app/(app)/instructor/session/[id]/ — session management pages
- src/app/(app)/admin/ — admin pages
- src/app/(app)/sections/ — section management
- src/app/(app)/namespace/ — namespace/invitation management
- src/app/(fullscreen)/student/ — student coding interface
- src/app/(projector)/public-view/ — projector display

## Implementation
1. grep -r "fetch.*['\`]/api/" src/app/ to find all direct fetch calls
2. Replace with API client calls or delegate to existing hooks
3. Ensure auth context is available in all component trees (it should be — AuthProvider wraps the root layout)
close_reason: Completed — 30 page/component files migrated to api-client

title: Add test for null currentUser in api-client
description: lib/__tests__/api-client.test.ts always sets mockCurrentUser. No test for what happens when firebaseAuth.currentUser is null (token expired, user logged out mid-request). Add tests verifying behavior when getIdToken is unavailable.

title: Add cross-layer integration tests (hook + api-client + component, only fetch mocked)
description: All hook tests mock api-client. All component tests mock hooks or fetch. No test wires a real hook with real api-client (mocking only fetch) feeding into a real component. This is exactly how the PascalCase User bug went undetected — mocks matched assumed shapes, not actual API responses. Add tests that exercise the full hook -> api-client -> component chain with only fetch mocked.

title: Hook tests are mock-in/mock-out — add tests that exercise real logic paths
description: Most hook tests (useClasses, api-client, etc.) mock the dependency, call the hook, and assert the mock return value propagates. They test wiring, not logic. Refactor to exercise real code paths: state transitions, error recovery, retry behavior, race conditions.
close_reason: Logic path tests added for hooks

title: Admin mock stats shape unverified against actual API contract
description: admin/__tests__/page.test.tsx mock stats use nested structures (users.byRole, sessions.active) that appear invented. Verify this matches the real /admin/stats endpoint response shape from the Go backend and fix the mock if needed.
close_reason: Mock verified against SystemStats interface, comment added

title: Add tests for null/undefined API response shapes (missing fields, null objects)
description: No tests for API returning unexpected shapes: missing fields, extra fields, null where object expected, empty arrays. Add tests verifying frontend handles degraded responses gracefully.
close_reason: Tests added for unexpected API response shapes

title: Remove dead code: withRetryInfo and RetryResult unused in production
description: lib/api-utils.ts exports withRetryInfo and RetryResult (~50 lines). No production code imports them; only the test file does. Delete dead code.

title: Inconsistent apiGet vs apiFetch usage across hooks — standardize
description: Some hooks (useClasses, useNamespaces) use typed apiGet<T>(). Others (useRealtimeSession, useSessionOperations) use apiFetch + manual .json(). Pick one pattern and standardize.

title: Error classification regex patterns match substrings too aggressively
description: lib/error-messages.ts patterns like /400/, /500/, /502/ match digits anywhere in error strings. "port 5003" would classify as server error. Use word boundaries (/\b400\b/) or check .status property instead.
close_reason: Regex patterns fixed with word boundaries

title: Public pages call fetch directly — add lightweight publicFetch wrapper
description: Public pages (register/student, invite/accept, auth/bootstrap, problems/[id]) call fetch directly. They can't use apiFetch (requires auth) but miss retry logic and consistent error handling. Add a lightweight publicFetch wrapper.
close_reason: publicFetch wrapper added, public pages updated

title: Delete Supabase server layer and API routes
description:
## Summary
Remove all server-side code that is no longer needed after the frontend calls the Go backend directly.

## Files to delete
- src/app/api/ -- entire directory (~50 Next.js API route files)
- src/server/persistence/supabase/ -- all 9 Supabase repository classes
- src/server/persistence/service-role-revision-repository.ts
- src/server/auth/ -- Supabase auth provider, api-auth, api-helpers, rbac, permissions, types, interfaces, mfa-cookie
- src/server/supabase/ -- client.ts, types.ts
- src/server/code-execution/ -- executor backends (now in Go backend)
- src/server/services/ -- gemini-analysis-service.ts, session-service.ts
- src/server/invitations/ -- capacity-service, invitation-service, student-registration-service
- src/server/classes/ -- join-code-service
- src/server/revision-buffer.ts -- diff-match-patch revision buffering (now in Go backend)
- src/server/testing/ -- supabase-mock, validation
- src/server/rate-limit.ts
- src/server/sandbox.ts
- src/server/types.ts and src/server/types/
- src/lib/supabase/ -- entire directory (client.ts, broadcast.ts)

## Implementation

2. Remove @supabase/supabase-js and related deps from package.json
3. Remove Supabase env vars from .env.example
4. Verify build passes with no dead imports (tsc --noEmit)
5. Check for any remaining references to deleted modules

title: Update and fix tests for new auth/API/realtime stack
description:
## Summary
Update existing tests to work with Firebase Auth, the new API client, and centrifuge-js. Delete tests for removed server-side code.

## Files to modify
- src/__mocks__/ — replace Supabase mocks with Firebase auth mock, API client mock, centrifuge-js mock

title: Copy Next.js frontend into eval repo
description:
## Summary
Copy the coding-tool Next.js app into /workspaces/eval/frontend/ as the starting point for migration.

## Steps
1. Create frontend/ directory in eval repo
2. Copy all source files from /workspaces/coding-tool: src/, package.json, package-lock.json, tsconfig.json, next.config.js, tailwind.config.js, postcss.config.js, jest.config.js, eslint.config.mjs, next-env.d.ts, .env.example, public/ (if exists)
3. Do NOT copy: node_modules/, .git/, .next/, .beads/, supabase/, ops/, e2e/, .github/, docs/, history/, .devcontainer/, .claude/, .copier-answers.yml, vercel.json, playwright.config.ts, scripts/
4. Run npm install in frontend/
5. Update .env.example to remove Supabase vars and add Firebase + Go backend vars
6. Verify the copy is complete and npm install succeeds
close_reason: Completed — frontend copied to eval repo, committed and pushed

title: CI failing on main: supabase CLI npm postinstall download corruption (transient)
description: Contract Tests workflow failing on main due to npm postinstall error downloading supabase CLI binary. Error: incorrect header check in gunzip. This is a transient CDN/network issue. Fix: re-run the workflow, or pin supabase CLI version.

title: Cross-namespace data access in namespace handler
description:
GET /namespaces/{id}/users and GET /namespaces/{id}/capacity don't verify that the calling namespace-admin's namespace matches the {id} URL param. A namespace-admin from namespace A can query namespace B's users/capacity.

Files: go-backend/internal/handler/namespaces.go (ListUsers and GetCapacity)

Fix: After extracting {id}, compare against authUser.NamespaceID for non-system-admin callers. Return 403 if mismatch. Follow pattern from handler/users.go DeleteNamespaceScoped.
close_reason: Fixed in c661c3d — added requireNamespaceAccess helper to ListUsers, GetCapacity, UpdateCapacity

title: Enhance contract coverage tool and add to CI
description:
The contract coverage tool (frontend/scripts/check-contract-coverage.ts) currently only checks that every exported async function in lib/api/*.ts has a corresponding contract test import. It reports 100% even when entire API routes have no client functions at all (e.g., registration routes had no client functions, so there was nothing to flag).

Two improvements needed:

1. Add the coverage check to CI as a required step. Add a make target (make check-contract-coverage) and include it in the frontend CI workflow so PRs fail if coverage drops below 100%.

2. Consider enhancing the tool to also cross-reference against known API routes (perhaps from the Go router or a route manifest) to detect missing client functions. This is a harder problem but would catch the class of bug where routes exist but no typed client function covers them.
close_reason: Implemented: check-api-imports.ts enforces import boundary, both checks added to frontend CI workflow

title: Sync analyze prompt with upstream coding-tool version
description: Update DefaultCustomDirections and BuildPrompt in go-backend/internal/ai/prompt.go to match upstream prompt quality. Add conciseness directive, title/explanation guidance, frequency ordering, pedagogical filter, finished vs in-progress classification. Keep max 5 issues. Sync frontend DEFAULT_PROMPT to match.

title: Re-review PR #13: Core API Routes (PLAT-cty)
description:

## Steps

2. Run /work-style reviews: correctness, test quality, architecture
3. File any discovered issues with discovered-from links
close_reason: Reviewed PR #13 with 3 specialized reviewers. Filed 5 follow-up issues.

title: Migrate Terraform from AWS to GCP
description:
## Summary
Rewrite infrastructure Terraform from AWS to GCP to reduce monthly costs from ~$160 to ~$77 (~$1k/year savings).

## GCP Project Setup (DONE)
- **Project ID:** eval-prod-485520
- **Region:** us-east1 (South Carolina) - cheapest tier, good east coast latency
- **State bucket:** gs://eval-prod-485520-terraform-state (versioned, private)
- **Auth:** ADC with personal account for local dev
- **Budget:** $100/mo with alerts at 50%, 90%, 100%
- **APIs enabled:** compute, container, sqladmin, identitytoolkit, iam, storage

## GCP Architecture Mapping

| AWS | GCP | Notes |
|-----|-----|-------|
| EKS + Fargate | GKE Autopilot | No control plane fee |
| RDS PostgreSQL | Cloud SQL db-g1-small | ~$15/mo |
| Cognito | Identity Platform | SAML supported |
| ElastiCache | In-cluster Redis pod | $0 extra |
| ALB | Cloud Load Balancing | ~$20/mo |
| fck-nat | NAT VM (e2-micro) | ~$6/mo (not Cloud NAT @ $32) |
| S3 (state) | GCS | Already created |
| DynamoDB (locks) | GCS native locking | Built into Terraform GCS backend |
| SES | TBD (SendGrid?) | GCP has no native email |

## Current State
- **Worktree:** /workspaces/eval-terraform-foundation (feature/terraform-foundation branch)
- **PR #1:** Has AWS Terraform - needs to be replaced with GCP
- **Devcontainer:** Already updated to use gcloud CLI instead of AWS CLI

## Files to Change

### Delete (AWS-specific)
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/* - all AWS module implementations
- infrastructure/terraform/environments/* - AWS backend configs
- infrastructure/terraform/bootstrap/* - S3/DynamoDB setup

### Create (GCP)
- infrastructure/terraform/bootstrap/ - GCS backend (already exists, just reference it)
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/vpc/ - GCP VPC with subnets
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/gke/ - GKE Autopilot cluster
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/cloudsql/ - Cloud SQL PostgreSQL
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/identity-platform/ - Auth config
- infrastructure/terraform/modules/nat/ - NAT VM (e2-micro)
- infrastructure/terraform/environments/prod/ - GCP backend config

### Update
- infrastructure/terraform/README.md - GCP patterns
- .env.example - GCP-specific env vars (already has gcloud)
- docs/ARCHITECTURE.md - Update diagrams from AWS to GCP

## Cost Target
~$77/month for prod environment:
- GKE Autopilot: $0 control plane + ~$35 pods
- Cloud SQL db-g1-small: ~$15
- Cloud Load Balancing: ~$20
- NAT VM (e2-micro): ~$6
- Redis: in-cluster ($0 extra)

## Open Questions
- Identity Platform vs self-hosted Keycloak for SAML?
- SendGrid vs other email provider?

Namespace-level (/api/v1/admin/users, namespace-admin+):

title: Admin stats, audit log, and clear-data endpoints
description:
## Summary
Admin endpoints for system statistics, audit log, and data clearing.

## Endpoints
- GET /api/v1/admin/stats — NEW: aggregate query (users by role, classes, sections, active sessions)

title: Auto-create revisions on code save via server-side revision buffer
description:
## Summary
Auto-create revisions on code save via server-side buffer, matching coding-tool design intent. Coding-tool's RevisionBuffer was broken on Vercel (stateless serverless). Go on GKE is persistent so this works correctly.

## Design
- In-memory map keyed by sessionID-userID, goroutine-safe
- Tracks previousCode for diff computation
- Flush triggers: 5s typing pause, 30s background interval, 100-revision buffer limit, session end
- Storage: full snapshot every 10th revision, diffs otherwise (if <1000 char change)
- Diff computation via go-diff

## Files to create/modify
- internal/revision/buffer.go (new) — RevisionBuffer
- handler/session_students.go — UpdateCode adds to buffer
- handler/sessions.go — session end flushes pending revisions
- go.mod — add go-diff dependency

title: Fix race condition: signIn() and onAuthStateChanged both call fetchUserProfile() concurrently
description:
## Summary

In `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`, calling `signIn()` triggers both an explicit `fetchUserProfile()` (line 164) and an `onAuthStateChanged` callback that also calls `fetchUserProfile()` (line 138). This causes two concurrent `GET /auth/me` requests on every sign-in.

For existing users this is invisible (both return 200). For first-time system admin bootstrap, both get 401, both attempt `POST /auth/bootstrap`, one gets 409 conflict, and the user sees ~4 console errors plus a multi-second delay.

## Files to modify

- `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`

## Fix

Remove the redundant `fetchUserProfile()` call from `signIn()` (lines 164-165). The `onAuthStateChanged` listener already handles fetching the profile after sign-in completes. The `signIn` function only needs to call `signInWithEmailAndPassword` — the state change listener does the rest.

Alternatively, use a shared promise/dedup mechanism so concurrent calls to `fetchUserProfile` coalesce into a single network request.

## Observed behavior

4x `GET /auth/me` 401 errors in console during first system admin login, several-second delay before profile loads.

## Expected behavior

At most 1 expected 401 (bootstrap detection), no redundant API calls.
close_reason: Fixed: removed redundant fetchUserProfile() from signIn(). Commit 9bfd868.

title: Add section channel publisher methods to realtime package
description:

Both constants and wire values use the `InSection` / `_in_section` suffix for consistency.
- `go-backend/internal/realtime/session_publisher.go` — Add `sectionChannel(sectionID)` helper returning `"section:" + sectionID`. Add `publishToSection()` helper mirroring existing `publish()` but targeting section channel. Add `SessionStartedInSection(ctx, sectionID, sessionID, problem)` and `SessionEndedInSection(ctx, sectionID, sessionID)` to interface + implementation.
- `go-backend/internal/realtime/async_publisher.go` — Add async wrappers for both methods following existing pattern (fire-and-forget goroutine via `runAsync`)

title: Frontend: useSectionEvents hook and StudentSectionView integration
description:
## Summary
Create a `useSectionEvents` hook that subscribes to the `section:{sectionID}` Centrifugo channel and returns reactive `activeSessions` state. Wire it into `StudentSectionView` so the "Class is live!" banner appears/disappears in real-time.

## Files to create
- `frontend/src/hooks/useSectionEvents.ts` — New hook

## Files to modify
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/components/StudentSectionView.tsx` — Replace static `activeSessions` prop usage with reactive state from hook

## Files to read for context
- `frontend/src/hooks/useRealtimeSession.ts` — Reference pattern for Centrifugo subscription lifecycle (createCentrifuge, newSubscription, getToken, publication handler, cleanup)
- `frontend/src/lib/centrifugo.ts` — `createCentrifuge()` and `getSubscriptionToken()` helpers (reuse as-is)
- `frontend/src/types/api.ts` — `Session` and `Problem` types (lines 90-105)
- `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/components/StudentSectionView.tsx` — Current component, banner at lines 115-140, Live badges at line 166

## Hook design
```typescript
interface UseSectionEventsOptions {
  sectionId: string;
  initialActiveSessions: Session[];
}
function useSectionEvents(options: UseSectionEventsOptions): { activeSessions: Session[] }
```

- Initialize state from `initialActiveSessions`
- Subscribe to `section:{sectionId}` using `createCentrifuge()` + `getSubscriptionToken()`
- On `session_started_in_section` event: construct partial `Session` from event data (`{ id: session_id, problem, status: 'active', ... defaults }`), add to state (replacing any with same id). Note: the constructed Session has empty defaults for fields the banner doesn't use (namespace_id, section_name, creator_id, etc.) — this is safe because the banner only reads `problem?.id`. Add a code comment noting this trade-off.
- On `session_ended_in_section` event: filter session out of state by `session_id`
- Clean up (unsubscribe + disconnect) on unmount or sectionId change
- No polling fallback needed (refresh is graceful degradation)

## StudentSectionView change
Minimal — add hook call at top of component:
```typescript
const { activeSessions } = useSectionEvents({
  sectionId,
  initialActiveSessions: activeSessions, // the prop
});
```
The rest of the component (banner check, Live badges, join handler) all reference `activeSessions` which is now the reactive local variable, so no other changes needed.

## Testing
**Hook test** (`frontend/src/hooks/__tests__/useSectionEvents.test.ts`):
- Returns initial active sessions on mount
- Subscribes to correct `section:{sectionId}` channel
- Adds session on session_started_in_section event (correct id + problem)
- Removes session on session_ended_in_section event
- Handles duplicate session_started_in_section (replaces, doesn't duplicate)
- Cleans up subscription on unmount

**Component test update** — Mock `useSectionEvents` to return `initialActiveSessions` unchanged so existing tests pass. Add one test verifying hook is called with correct args.

## Implementation steps
1. Create `useSectionEvents.ts` hook
2. Add hook call to StudentSectionView
3. Add hook unit tests
4. Update/add component tests

## Verification
`make test-frontend && make lint-frontend && make typecheck-frontend`

title: Fix frontend/backend payload mismatch in useSectionEvents hook
description:
## Summary
The useSectionEvents hook reads payload.problem_id and payload.section_id from session_started_in_section events, but the backend sends { session_id, problem } where problem is the full JSON object. This means problem is always null on the client, breaking the "Class is live!" banner and Live badges.

## Files to modify
- frontend/src/hooks/useSectionEvents.ts — Read payload.problem (full object) instead of payload.problem_id. Remove payload.section_id reference (use sectionId from hook params).
- frontend/src/hooks/__tests__/useSectionEvents.test.ts — Update test payloads to use { session_id, problem: { id, ... } } wire format matching backend. Add assertion that activeSessions[0].problem.id is correctly populated.

## Also fix (trivial)
- go-backend/internal/handler/centrifugo.go — Collapse parseSessionChannel and parseSectionChannel into a single parseChannelUUID(channel, prefix) helper.

## Implementation steps
1. In useSectionEvents.ts: change the session_started_in_section handler to read payload.problem directly as the Problem object instead of constructing one from payload.problem_id
2. In useSectionEvents.test.ts: update all test payloads to match real wire format { session_id: "...", problem: { id: "...", ... } }
3. In useSectionEvents.test.ts: add assertion on activeSessions[0].problem.id after session_started_in_section
4. In centrifugo.go: replace parseSessionChannel + parseSectionChannel with parseChannelUUID(channel, prefix string)

## Verification
make test-frontend && make lint-frontend && make typecheck-frontend && make test-api && make lint-api

title: Fix parseTime for PostgreSQL ::text cast format in ListSectionProblems
description: ListSectionProblems query casts sw.created_at::text and sw.last_update::text which produces PostgreSQL text format (e.g. '2024-01-15 10:30:00+00'), not RFC3339. parseTime uses time.Parse(time.RFC3339, s) which silently fails, returning zero time. StudentWork.CreatedAt and LastUpdate will always be 0001-01-01T00:00:00Z. Fix: either remove the ::text cast and scan as time.Time, or update parseTime to handle PostgreSQL text format.
close_reason: Fixed: removed ::text cast, scan as time.Time

title: Move publishAsync and logger out of handler layer into realtime package
description:
## Summary
SessionHandler and SessionStudentHandler diverge from all other handlers by accepting *slog.Logger in their constructors. The logger is only needed for the publishAsync free function in handler/publish.go, which spawns fire-and-forget goroutines. This couples the handler layer to goroutine management and logging concerns that belong in the realtime package.

- go-backend/internal/realtime/session_publisher.go — add async publish support with internal logger
- go-backend/internal/server/server.go — update constructor calls

## Implementation

2. Remove *slog.Logger from SessionHandler and SessionStudentHandler constructors
3. Move the fire-and-forget goroutine pattern into the realtime package

The go-api backend already handles this correctly (publishAsync is fire-and-forget, errors are logged but don't affect HTTP responses). The frontend behavior is unknown.

## Areas to audit
- What happens when WebSocket connection fails or drops?
- Does the UI show stale presence data or error states?
- Do code submission / session join flows break if realtime events aren't delivered?
- Is there a reconnection strategy with backoff?

title: Fix executor scale-to-zero: deploy GMP frontend, configure KEDA properly
description:
## Summary

The executor KEDA ScaledObject is configured with minReplicaCount: 0, but scale-to-zero doesn't work due to three issues. Currently set to minReplicaCount: 1 as a workaround (~$11/mo cost).

## Problems to fix

1. **No GMP query frontend deployed** — The ScaledObject references `http://frontend.gmp-system.svc:9090` but this service doesn't exist. GKE Managed Prometheus collects metrics but doesn't deploy an in-cluster query frontend by default. Deploy the frontend Deployment+Service in gmp-system namespace per GCP docs.

2. **KEDA ignoreNullValues** — When executor has 0 replicas, no pods emit `executor_active_executions`. KEDA 2.16.1 treats missing metrics as TriggerError (default ignoreNullValues: true). Add `ignoreNullValues: "false"` to the ScaledObject trigger metadata so KEDA treats missing metrics as 0.

3. **Scale-from-zero trigger** — Even with 1+2 fixed, KEDA can't scale FROM zero because the metric is only emitted by executor pods. Options:
   - Add a Redis queue depth metric from go-api (preferred — already uses Redis for rate limiting)
   - Add a KEDA cron trigger for business hours
   - Use a KEDA webhook/HTTP trigger from go-api's submission endpoint

## Files to modify

- k8s/base/executor-scaledobject.yaml — Add ignoreNullValues, potentially add second trigger for scale-from-zero
- k8s/base/gmp-frontend.yaml — New file, deploy GMP query frontend (Deployment + Service)
- k8s/base/kustomization.yaml — Add gmp-frontend.yaml

## Current workaround

minReplicaCount: 1 in executor-scaledobject.yaml. The executor node pool always has 1 node running (~$11/mo spot).

title: Full-screen editor for student code review
description: Explore showing student code in a full-screen editor with run capability, instead of the current inline pre block on the student work review page. Consider adding an 'Open in editor' link/button that opens the student's code in a read-only (or runnable) full-screen editor view.

title: Fix executor smoke test flakiness — add health polling before sandbox tests
description: The executor smoke test in scripts/smoke-test.sh has no retry logic, unlike all other smoke checks. After a Recreate deploy, Service endpoints may lag behind rollout status, causing Connection refused failures. Fix: poll executor healthz endpoint before running tests.

title: Fix E2E tests that navigate to deleted /sections page
Drain HTTP response body on success in realtime client for connection reuse
description:
## Summary
In go-backend/internal/realtime/client.go, the Publish method does not drain the response body on success (2xx) before closing it. This prevents the HTTP transport from reusing the connection, creating a new TCP connection for each publish call.

## Files to modify
- go-backend/internal/realtime/client.go (in the Publish method, after the status check)

## Implementation
Add `io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)` before returning nil on success.

title: Fix namespace-invitations.ts to use namespace-scoped endpoints (/namespaces/{nsID}/invitations) instead of system endpoints (/system/invitations) which require system-admin role, causing 403 for namespace-admin users on the admin page
close_reason: Fixed: deleted broken namespace-invitations.ts module that called /system/invitations. Admin page and namespace invitations page now use correct endpoints based on user role.

title: Introduce can_manage_section() and fix namespace-admin RLS gaps across all section-scoped tables

Creates can_manage_section() helper and updates policies on 7 tables: sections, sessions, section_problems, section_memberships, student_work, session_students, session_backend_state. Also adds HandleForbidden to store methods and ErrForbidden->403 in handlers.

title: Scope instructor/namespace-admin views to 'my data' via section_memberships
Add ListMyClasses store method and update List handler to scope /classes to "my classes"
description:
## Summary

The GET /classes endpoint returns ALL classes visible via RLS, which for namespace-admins and instructors means every class in the namespace. It should only return classes the user is involved with: classes they created OR classes where they have an instructor section membership.

The instructor dashboard (GET /instructor/dashboard) already does this correctly via its query in store/dashboard.go. This task applies the same scoping to the /classes list.

## Files to modify

### go-backend/internal/store/interfaces.go (~line 97)
Add `ListMyClasses(ctx context.Context, userID uuid.UUID) ([]Class, error)` to ClassRepository interface.

### go-backend/internal/store/classes.go (after ListClasses, ~line 57)
Add new method using query pattern from dashboard.go (lines 38-42):
- `SELECT DISTINCT c.id, c.namespace_id, c.name, c.description, c.created_by, c.created_at, c.updated_at FROM classes c LEFT JOIN sections sec ON sec.class_id = c.id WHERE c.created_by = $1 OR sec.id IN (SELECT section_id FROM section_memberships WHERE user_id = $1 AND role = 'instructor') ORDER BY c.created_at`
- Note: `classColumns` constant uses bare column names, so this query needs explicit `c.` prefixed columns since it JOINs tables.

### go-backend/internal/handler/classes.go (lines 42-56)
Update `List` handler: get auth user from context via `auth.UserFromContext`, call `ListMyClasses(ctx, authUser.ID)` instead of `ListClasses(ctx)`. Return 401 if no auth user.

### go-backend/internal/handler/classes_test.go
- Add `listMyClassesFn func(ctx context.Context, userID uuid.UUID) ([]store.Class, error)` to `classesTestRepos` struct (line 23) and `mockClassRepo` struct (line 818)
- Implement `ListMyClasses` method on both mock types
- Update `TestListClasses_Success`, `TestListClasses_Empty`, `TestListClasses_InternalError` to use new mock function and inject auth user into context

### go-backend/internal/handler/testutil_test.go
Add `ListMyClasses` panic-stub to `stubRepos`. This is required because `stubRepos` must satisfy the `store.Repos` interface, and adding `ListMyClasses` to `ClassRepository` changes the interface. Without this, all handler tests will fail to compile.

### go-backend/internal/store/store_methods_integration_test.go
Add integration test for ListMyClasses: two instructors in same namespace, each creates a class with a section, add instructor A as co-instructor on instructor B's section, verify A sees both classes and B sees only their own.

## Steps
1. Add ListMyClasses to ClassRepository interface
2. Add ListMyClasses panic-stub to stubRepos in testutil_test.go
3. Implement ListMyClasses in store/classes.go
4. Update List handler to call ListMyClasses with auth user ID
5. Update handler test mocks and tests
6. Add store integration test
7. Run `make test-api` and `make lint-api`

title: Fix section detail back-navigation for instructor+ roles
description:
## Summary

When an instructor navigates from /classes/[id] to a section detail page (/sections/[section_id]), the BackButton hardcodes `href="/sections"` with label "Back to My Sections". This points to the student-only sections list page, which is empty for instructors. The back button should navigate instructors back to the class they came from.

## Files to modify

### frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/page.tsx
- Line 120: Main BackButton — change href from `/sections` to role-aware value
- Line 109: Error-state BackButton — section data is null here, so classId is unavailable. Use `/classes` as a safe fallback for instructor+ roles.

The page already fetches class data (line 55: `getClass(sectionData.class_id)`) and knows the user role (line 63: `role: user!.role`). Use these to set the back button:

**Main back button (line 120):**

Before:
```tsx
<BackButton href="/sections">Back to My Sections</BackButton>
```

After — compute href/label based on role:
```tsx
const isInstructor = ['instructor', 'namespace-admin', 'system-admin'].includes(user.role);
// In main view (section loaded):
<BackButton href={isInstructor ? `/classes/${section.classId}` : '/sections'}>
  {isInstructor ? 'Back to Class' : 'Back to My Sections'}
</BackButton>
```

**Error-state back button (line 109):**
classId is unknown since the fetch failed. Use generic fallback:
```tsx
<BackButton href={isInstructor ? '/classes' : '/sections'}>
  {isInstructor ? 'Back to Classes' : 'Back to Sections'}
</BackButton>
```

Add a `classId: string` field to the `SectionDetail` interface (lines 11-18), set it from `sectionData.class_id` in loadSectionData.

## Testing

Add unit test for the role-aware back button. Test file: `frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/__tests__/page.test.tsx` (create if needed). Test cases:
- Instructor role: main BackButton href contains `/classes/{classId}`
- Student role: main BackButton href is `/sections`
- Error state with instructor role: BackButton href is `/classes`

## Steps
1. Add `classId: string` to the SectionDetail interface
2. Set it from `sectionData.class_id` in the loadSectionData function
3. Compute isInstructor from user.role
4. Update main BackButton (line 120): role-aware href and label using classId
5. Update error-state BackButton (line 109): role-aware with generic fallback (no classId)
6. Add unit tests for both role paths
7. Run `make test-frontend` and `make typecheck-frontend`

title: Delete /sections list page and clean up navigation
description:
## Summary

The /sections list page is student-only (nav config restricts to `student` role), queries section_memberships which has been an unreliable source of truth, and creates a broken navigation target for instructors. Remove it and clean up the navigation config.

The section detail page at /sections/[section_id] stays — it's the shared detail view. Only the list page and its student-specific SectionCard component are removed.

## Files to delete

### frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/page.tsx
Student sections list page. No longer needed.

### frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/components/SectionCard.tsx
Student-specific SectionCard with polling for active sessions. Not used by any other page. (The instructor SectionCard at frontend/src/app/(app)/classes/components/SectionCard.tsx is unrelated and stays.)

### frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/components/__tests__/SectionCard.test.tsx
Test file for the deleted student SectionCard. Must be deleted to avoid orphaned imports.

## Files to modify

### frontend/src/config/navigation.ts
- Lines 41-48: Remove the "my-sections" NavItem entry (roles: ['student'], href: '/sections')
- Lines 105-106: Remove `/sections` from BREADCRUMB_HIERARCHY (it's no longer a valid navigation target). Update `/sections/[section_id]` parent to `null` (it becomes a top-level page).

### frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/join/page.tsx
- Line 15 (approx): After a successful join, `router.push('/sections')` redirects to the now-deleted page. Update to redirect to the section detail: `router.push(`/sections/${membership.section_id}`)` so the student lands on the section they just joined.

### frontend/src/hooks/useSections.ts
Keep this file — it's still used for joinSection, leaveSection, and getActiveSessions. Only the sections list page imported fetchMySections; no other consumer exists.

## Steps
1. Delete frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/page.tsx
2. Delete frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/components/SectionCard.tsx
3. Delete frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/components/__tests__/SectionCard.test.tsx
4. Remove "my-sections" nav item from navigation.ts
5. Update BREADCRUMB_HIERARCHY: remove `/sections` entry, change `/sections/[section_id]` parent to null
6. Update sections/join/page.tsx redirect from `/sections` to `/sections/${membership.section_id}`
7. Verify no other imports reference the deleted files: grep for 'sections/page' and 'sections/components/SectionCard'
8. Run `make test-frontend`, `make lint-frontend`, `make typecheck-frontend`

title: File follow-up issues for class-level membership, student dashboard, admin view
description:
## Summary

File follow-up issues identified during planning for the "scope instructor views" epic. These are out of scope for the current work but should be tracked.

## Issues to create

### 1. Class-level membership model (priority 3)
Currently classes have a single `created_by` owner. Ideally classes would support multiple instructors via a class_memberships table (like sections have section_memberships). This would support: multiple instructors co-owning a class, ownership transfer, and decoupling class visibility from section assignments. For now, `created_by + section memberships` is sufficient.

### 2. Student home/dashboard page (priority 3)
With the /sections list page removed, students join sections via join code and land on section detail directly. A student dashboard/home page showing enrolled sections, active sessions, and recent activity would improve the student experience.

### 3. Namespace admin oversight view (priority 4)
Admin page for namespace admins to see all classes/sections in the namespace. The current RLS policies already grant broad read access to namespace-admins; this just needs a dedicated UI that uses unscoped queries. Separate from the day-to-day teaching views.

## Steps
1. Create each issue with bd create, linking back to the epic with discovered-from dependency
2. Set appropriate priorities as noted above
close_reason: Filed 3 follow-up issues: PLAT-4kdh, PLAT-49tb, PLAT-49nw

title: Section detail page routes instructors to student workspace for active sessions
description:
## Summary

The section detail page (/sections/[section_id]/page.tsx) always routes users to the student workspace (/student?session_id=...) when clicking active session buttons, regardless of role. Instructors, namespace-admins, and system-admins should be routed to the instructor session dashboard (/instructor/session/{id}) instead.

## Files to modify

- frontend/src/app/(app)/sections/[section_id]/page.tsx

## Implementation steps

1. In handleJoinSession (line 83-85), check the isInstructor boolean (already computed on line 106, will need to move it earlier or compute inline) and route to /instructor/session/{session_id} for teaching roles, /student?session_id={session_id} for students.
2. Update the button label from Join Now to View Dashboard (or similar) for instructors, since they are not joining as participants.

## Example

Before: All roles see Join Now -> /student?session_id=abc
After: Instructors see View Dashboard -> /instructor/session/abc, Students see Join Now -> /student?session_id=abc

title: Post-deploy smoke tests
Migrate JWT validation from custom code to Firebase Admin Go SDK
description:
## Summary
Replace the hand-rolled JWT validation (820 lines across 5 files) with Firebase Admin Go SDK's `VerifyIDToken`. Auth is not our core competency — we should use mature, tested libraries maintained by Google's security team.

## Motivation
- Custom JWT validation is a security liability: missing `sub` validation, no clock skew tolerance, no `auth_time` check, no JWKS response size limit
- No visibility into upstream security patches — the Admin SDK gets CVE attention, our custom code doesn't
- Prerequisite for Firebase Auth Emulator (PLAT-d6j): the Admin SDK auto-detects `FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST` and accepts emulator tokens. This eliminates the need for a custom backdoor code path gated by an env var.
- Removes the need for `AUTH_MODE=test` backend bypass entirely once emulator is in place

## Files to modify
- `go-backend/internal/auth/jwt.go` (192 lines) — replace `identityPlatformValidator` with Admin SDK wrapper
- `go-backend/internal/auth/jwks.go` (161 lines) — delete entirely (Admin SDK handles JWKS)
- `go-backend/internal/auth/jwt_test.go` (221 lines) — replace with Admin SDK integration tests
- `go-backend/internal/auth/jwks_test.go` (188 lines) — delete entirely
- `go-backend/internal/auth/testvalidator.go` (57 lines) — keep for now (still needed until emulator replaces AUTH_MODE=test)
- `go-backend/internal/server/server.go` (~lines 130-141) — simplify validator creation
- `go-backend/go.mod` — add `firebase.google.com/go/v4`

## Implementation steps
1. Add `firebase.google.com/go/v4` dependency
2. Create a new `TokenValidator` implementation that wraps `auth.Client.VerifyIDToken()`
3. Handle the `Claims` mapping (Admin SDK returns `auth.Token`, map to our `Claims` struct)
4. Wire it up in `server.go` — replace `NewIdentityPlatformValidator` with the new wrapper
5. Keep `AUTH_MODE=test` path for now (testValidator still needed until emulator work lands)
6. Delete `jwt.go`, `jwks.go`, and their test files
7. Verify existing integration tests pass with the new validator

## Security issues in current code (for reference)
1. No `sub` (subject) validation — empty sub propagates as a user with no identity
2. No clock skew tolerance — Firebase Admin SDK allows 5 min, we allow 0
3. No `auth_time` validation — Firebase spec requires checking it's in the past
4. No JWKS response size limit — `io.ReadAll` with no cap
5. No test for `alg:none` attack vector (code rejects it, but untested)

## Notes
- The Admin SDK requires a GCP project ID and optionally a service account. On GKE, Application Default Credentials handle this automatically.
- For local dev, `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` or a service account JSON is needed.
- When `FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST` is set, the Admin SDK automatically routes to the emulator and accepts emulator-issued tokens — no custom code needed.
